events.prayer: Communities in Plateau State grieving 20 people killed in an armed attack
events.alert_summary: An armed attack in Plateau State, Nigeria killed at least 20 people.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Borno
events.body:
Nigerian authorities have freed nearly 50 people who had been held captive by suspected Islamic State members in Borno state, in the country's northeast. The region has faced years of armed group activity, and kidnappings remain a recurring threat to local communities.

No details were available in the source about how the rescue took place, how long the captives had been held, or whether anyone was injured during the operation. The freed individuals are now in the care of authorities.

Borno state borders Lake Chad and has long been a focal point of armed group activity in the region. Civilians in rural and semi-rural areas remain vulnerable to abductions, which disrupt family life, livelihoods, and access to basic services.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Nearly 50 kidnapping survivors in Borno freed from suspected Islamic State captivity
events.alert_summary: Nigerian authorities freed nearly 50 people kidnapped by suspected Islamic State members in Borno state.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Moscow
events.body:
Ukraine launched close to 200 drones at targets around Moscow in what Russian officials described as the largest Ukrainian drone attack on the capital since the full-scale war began. Seventeen people were wounded in the Moscow region, according to the regional governor. One person was killed when an oil depot was struck in the southern Rostov region.

Russia's defence ministry reported that almost 1,000 drones and four Ukrainian cruise missiles were intercepted and destroyed across the country within a 24-hour period. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky described the strikes as "long-range sanctions," a term his government uses for long-distance attacks inside Russian territory.

Residents near affected areas reported thick smoke rising from strike sites. The scale of the attack has raised questions about whether Russia will adjust its military approach in response.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: 18 people killed or wounded in Russia amid drone strikes on Moscow and Rostov regions
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow wounded 17 and killed 1 in Rostov; nearly 1,000 drones intercepted across Russia in 24 hours.

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Entebbe
events.body:
A Kenyan lawyer and former justice minister was turned away at Entebbe airport when she arrived to help defend a Ugandan opposition leader on trial for treason. No reason was given by Ugandan authorities, though the lawyer said the move was unlawful and politically motivated. A colleague who traveled with her for the same purpose was allowed in.

The opposition leader has been held since late 2024, when he was taken from Kenya and brought to Uganda by force. Last week, a second lawyer on the defense team was himself charged with a treason-related offense and remanded to prison after appearing in court.

The Kenyan lawyer was also deported from Tanzania last year under similar circumstances, when she attempted to represent another opposition figure facing treason charges there. Legal bodies in Kenya have called on Uganda to explain why she was denied entry.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Detained opposition leader Kizza Besigye and his defense lawyers facing restricted access to legal representation in Uganda
events.alert_summary: Ugandan authorities blocked a Kenyan defense lawyer from entering the country at Entebbe airport, giving no reason.

events.title: Ras Laffan
events.body:
An explosion at a liquified natural gas facility in Qatar's Ras Laffan industrial zone on Sunday night killed at least 13 people and injured 66 others. Officials described the incident as a "technical accident" and said it was not the result of sabotage or hostile action. All those killed were workers from India and Pakistan.

The blast occurred as workers were restarting operations at the Barzan local gas supply facility, which had been shut down since December 2025 for urgent maintenance and had only resumed operations two days before the explosion. The fire has since been brought under control, and officials say there are no environmental risks.

Qatar's energy minister said it would be difficult to say when operations would fully resume. The facility is part of the world's largest LNG export site, and officials said the explosion would not affect the country's exports. An investigation into the cause is underway.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Families of 13 workers from India and Pakistan killed in the Ras Laffan gas explosion
events.alert_summary: An explosion at the Barzan gas facility in Ras Laffan killed 13 and injured 66, all victims from India and Pakistan.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sagaing
events.body:
A new UN Human Rights Office report covering August 2024 through January 2025 documents a minimum of 702 verified civilian deaths in Myanmar, among them 224 women and 153 children. Air strikes were described as the single largest cause of destruction. The Sagaing region recorded the highest toll, with 191 deaths.

Two attacks in Sagaing stand out. In October, munitions struck a crowd gathered outside a school in Chaung-U during a candlelit event marking the end of Buddhist Lent, killing 23 people including four children and wounding more than 60. In December, a military aircraft bombed a tea shop in Tabayin where people had gathered to watch football, killing at least 19 and wounding 20 more.

The report also notes that Rohingya civilians have faced forced recruitment, killings, arbitrary arrests, and sexual violence. UN officials warned that a pullback in international aid funding is leaving millions with fewer protections as the conflict continues.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: 702 civilians—including 153 children—killed by military forces across Myanmar in six months
events.alert_summary: Myanmar's military killed at least 702 civilians, including 153 children, in six months from August 2024 to January 2025, per a UN report.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Côte-des-Neiges, Montreal
events.body:
A man opened fire in the Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood of Montreal around midday on Monday, killing a police officer, one civilian, and wounding another officer before being shot and killed by police. A witness reported seeing a man dressed in military clothing with a weapon before officers arrived. The officer killed was a 34-year-old who had served on the force since 2021. The civilian killed was identified as a member of Montreal's Jewish community. Police have not identified the shooting as an antisemitic attack.

Quebec's security minister said the motive remained unclear. A French-language broadcaster reported the gunman may have drawn inspiration from the "incel" movement, though authorities have not confirmed this. Following the attack, Canada's national police force sent an alert to police services across the country regarding a document that allegedly encouraged violence against officers. The incident was the first line-of-duty death for a Montreal officer in nearly 25 years.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Families of the police officer and civilian killed in the Montreal midday shooting
events.alert_summary: A midday shooting in Montreal's Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood killed a police officer, one civilian, and the suspect, with one other officer wounded.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh al-Fawqa
events.body:
Israeli soldiers shot and killed two men in the southern Lebanese town of Nabatieh al-Fawqa, according to Lebanon's health ministry. The men were part of a team from an emergency service linked to Hezbollah, working to reopen roads and recover bodies from rubble in the area.

Israel's military said its soldiers fired on four people riding a bulldozer and motorcycle who had crossed into its declared security zone east of the town and ignored warning shots. Hezbollah called the shooting a ceasefire violation and said it had followed the agreement up to that point, though it did not say whether it would retaliate.

The ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah has largely held since Sunday, the longest pause in weeks of escalating fighting. Israeli officials stated troops would remain in southern Lebanon and retain freedom to act against any perceived Hezbollah threat, while diplomatic talks between Lebanese and Israeli officials opened in Washington.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Two men killed near a bulldozer in southern Lebanon during a fragile ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Israeli soldiers shot and killed two men near a bulldozer in Nabatieh al-Fawqa, southern Lebanon, the first fatal incident since a ceasefire took hold Sunday.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Ankara
events.body:
Turkish authorities detained 209 people on Tuesday as part of a pre-summit security sweep in Ankara. Prosecutors issued 241 arrest warrants, with 32 suspects still at large. Of those arrested, 56 were alleged members of the Islamic State group and 35 were linked to a communist armed group designated as a terrorist organization by Turkey. A separate operation in Istanbul led to 24 additional arrests across eight provinces.

The crackdown comes ahead of the NATO leaders' summit scheduled for July 7–8 in Ankara. Turkish authorities have announced a ban on public demonstrations beginning June 28, with road restrictions near airports and sealed-off zones around summit venues and delegation hotels.

Turkey carried out a similar nationwide sweep last month, detaining 324 people on suspicion of links to the Islamic State. Residents and visitors in Ankara can expect tightened movement restrictions and an increased security presence through the summit dates.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: 209 people detained in Ankara ahead of NATO summit under broad security crackdown
events.alert_summary: Turkish security forces detained 209 people on suspicion of links to armed groups ahead of a July NATO summit in Ankara.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Herat
events.body:
In early June, Taliban authorities detained at least 30 women in Herat over alleged violations of dress-code rules, according to the UN Assistance Mission in Afghanistan. The arrests prompted protests in the Injil district, a predominantly Shiite area of the city. Taliban forces opened fire on demonstrators, killing at least two people — including a child — and wounding more than 20 others. The Taliban have denied the arrests took place.

The protests drew attention to ongoing restrictions on women in Afghanistan, with women's rights groups describing the detentions as part of a broader pattern of repression. Observers note that residents in parts of the country remain willing to challenge Taliban authority despite serious personal risk.

Separately, the Taliban have also been moving to restrict smartphone use, including among their own officials, a step that analysts say reflects concern about information reaching the public and organizing happening beyond the regime's control.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Afghan women and protesters in Herat facing arrests and gunfire over dress-code enforcement
events.alert_summary: Taliban forces shot at protesters in Herat, killing at least 2 and injuring more than 20, after detaining 30 women over dress-code violations.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Nanyuki
events.body:
Plans to build a tented Ebola quarantine facility at Laikipia Air Base, about 200 kilometers from Nairobi, sparked protests that turned violent, killing three people. The facility was intended to house US nationals who might contract Ebola during the ongoing outbreak in eastern Congo. Kenya has never recorded an Ebola case, and residents raised concerns about bringing infected patients into the country.

A Kenyan court had found the Health Minister in contempt after the government continued construction despite court orders to pause. The minister appeared before the court Tuesday and pledged a full stop to all construction and site preparation at the base, pending a court review. The judge discharged the minister without further punishment but warned against future disobedience.

The broader outbreak in Congo has now surpassed 1,000 confirmed cases and 267 deaths, with 25 cases confirmed in displacement camps in eastern Congo.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Residents near Nanyuki amid protests over a planned Ebola quarantine facility
events.alert_summary: Protests over a planned US-backed Ebola quarantine facility near Nanyuki left three people dead.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
Israeli soldiers opened fire on two men standing near an excavator unblocking a road in a town near the city of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon, killing both and wounding two others. The Lebanese state news agency reported the incident on Tuesday. It is the first reported loss of life from Israeli fire in Lebanon in three days, since a US-brokered ceasefire largely took hold on Sunday.

Hezbollah denounced the shooting as a violation of the truce but did not say whether it would respond. Earlier, a senior Hezbollah official warned the group would respond "in kind" to any ceasefire violation. The Israeli military said it struck armed individuals who posed an immediate threat to soldiers in what it calls a security zone in southern Lebanon.

Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 4,106 people since March 2, and at least 1.2 million people have been displaced. US-mediated negotiations between Israel and Lebanon are ongoing in Washington, with talks expected to continue through Thursday.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Two men killed near Nabatieh as a fragile ceasefire faces its first reported deaths in three days
events.alert_summary: Israeli soldiers shot and killed two men and wounded two others near Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Pietermaritzburg
events.body:
Police in South Africa are investigating the death of a Malawian man in Pietermaritzburg, near Durban. He was attacked following a protest, fled to a nearby informal settlement, and was later found at a riverbank with head and mouth injuries. A murder case has been opened.

Hundreds of Malawians have gathered at a center in Pietermaritzburg seeking flights home, as several African governments have sent aircraft to repatriate thousands of their nationals. At least two people have died in weeks of anti-migrant unrest across the country.

Small but organized protest groups have issued an ultimatum demanding undocumented migrants from several African countries leave South Africa by June 30, a demand that carries no legal weight. Security has been tightened nationwide ahead of that deadline. Four additional people linked to political parties were shot dead during voter registration at the weekend, adding to an already tense atmosphere for both migrants and residents.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Malawian and other African migrants in South Africa facing mob violence and forced displacement
events.alert_summary: A 29-year-old Malawian national was killed in Pietermaritzburg during anti-migrant unrest that has left at least 2 dead across South Africa.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kryvyi Rih
events.body:
A Russian missile carrying a cluster munition warhead struck the Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rih, killing three people and injuring more than 20. Ukrainian authorities confirmed the strike on the city.

Separately, Ukraine's Special Operations Forces announced the destruction of a railway bridge over the North Crimean canal in Russian-occupied Crimea. The bridge had served as a key route for moving cargo and military supplies between Crimea and mainland Russia via the Kerch Strait. Ukraine's defense minister said the military is working to cut off Crimea by drone, warning it could effectively become an island.

Ukraine has also struck oil refineries in Crimea, disrupting fuel supplies on the peninsula. Russia's government is now considering a diesel export ban and other measures to stabilize its domestic fuel market. Residents on both sides of the conflict continue to face risks to daily life as strikes on energy and civilian infrastructure persist.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Three people killed and over 20 injured in a cluster munition strike on a Ukrainian city
events.alert_summary: A Russian ballistic missile with a cluster munition warhead struck Kryvyi Rih, killing 3 and injuring over 20.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kebbi
events.body:
Police shot and killed a man described as a suspected leader of a kidnapping network in Kebbi State, northwestern Nigeria. Officers also rescued a victim being held by the group, though details about the victim's condition or how long they had been held were not provided in available reporting.

Kidnapping for ransom has been a persistent problem across Nigeria's northwest, where armed groups have targeted civilians in rural communities. Local police confirmed the operation, though no further details about additional suspects detained or the size of the network were immediately available.

The rescued victim's safe return is the central outcome reported. Residents in affected areas continue to face the threat of abduction in their daily lives.

events.prayer: Kidnapping victim rescued in Kebbi and communities living under threat of abduction
events.alert_summary: Police killed a suspected kidnapping leader and rescued one victim in Kebbi State.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon
events.body:
A prominent Lebanese conservationist died after an Israeli airstrike struck her home in Lebanon. The source does not name a specific city or give a date for the strike.

The woman was known for her conservation work in Lebanon. Her death was reported by multiple outlets following the strike on her residence.

The incident is part of ongoing Israeli military operations in Lebanon that have killed and displaced civilians across the country.

events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike on a woman's home in Lebanon killed a leading conservationist; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Montreal
events.body:
A gunman ambushed police officers in a Montreal neighborhood, killing one officer. Reporting describes the attacker as connected to the "incel" movement — an online community whose members have been linked to previous attacks in North America.

The attack took place in an area described as home to a significant Jewish community. Residents in the neighborhood were caught in the situation as officers responded to the scene.

One officer was killed in the attack. The incident adds to concerns about ideologically motivated violence targeting both law enforcement and civilian communities.

events.prayer: Montreal officer killed and residents shaken by a targeted ambush in a residential neighborhood
events.alert_summary: A gunman ambushed police officers in Montreal, killing one officer in a residential neighborhood.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ukraine
events.body:
Drone strikes hit multiple locations across Ukraine, killing at least five people. The attacks targeted civilian areas, continuing a pattern of overnight aerial strikes that have caused casualties and damage across the country.

Residents in affected areas faced destruction to homes and infrastructure. Emergency crews responded to strike sites to search for survivors and assess damage.

The strikes add to the ongoing toll on Ukrainian civilians, who have endured repeated aerial attacks throughout the conflict.
