events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine killed and wounded in overnight drone strikes
events.alert_summary: Russian drone attacks across Ukraine killed at least 5 people, according to reports.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mashegu
events.body:
Armed bandits attacked the Mashegu area of Niger State, Nigeria, but police confirmed afterward that no kidnappings took place during the incident.

Bandit attacks in Niger State have at times involved abductions, so the confirmation that no one was taken was notable. Authorities did not release details on casualties or property damage from the attack.

Mashegu is in a rural part of Niger State that has seen recurring insecurity from armed groups operating across the northwest region of Nigeria.
events.source_title: "Sahel OR Burkina Faso OR Mali OR Niger attack OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Mashegu affected by an armed bandit attack
events.alert_summary: Police confirmed no kidnappings occurred during a bandit attack in Mashegu, Niger State.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Plateau state
events.body:
Armed attackers killed 18 people in Plateau state, Nigeria, according to a report from Haberler.com. No additional details about the location within the state, the identities of those responsible, or the circumstances of the attack were available in the source material.

Plateau state has experienced recurring violence in recent years, often involving clashes between farming and herding communities, as well as attacks by armed groups. Residents in affected areas continue to face risks to their safety and livelihoods.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: 18 people killed in an armed attack in Plateau state, Nigeria
events.alert_summary: An armed attack in Nigeria's Plateau state killed 18 people; no further details reported.

events.title: Bucharest
events.body:
Starting on February 10, 2024, criminals used ransomware called BackMyData to attack Romania's hospital network through a widely used medical records system called Hippocrates. The attackers broke into the software company that made it, then spread through hospitals across the country, scrambling files and demanding payment in bitcoin. More than 100 hospitals were ordered to disconnect from the internet to stop the spread. Investigators later confirmed 26 hospitals had been infected.

With no connected devices, email, or web browsers available, medical staff switched to pen and paper. Doctors improvised offline systems to track patients, request lab work, and manage supplies. Some hospitals used spreadsheets on disconnected computers. Uninfected hospitals were brought back online the following day with added protections.

The four-day crisis has since become a reference point for emergency planners in other countries studying how to respond to large-scale hospital cyberattacks. The FBI has noted that healthcare is now the most targeted area of critical national infrastructure.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Hospital staff and patients across Romania affected by a ransomware attack on 100 hospitals
events.alert_summary: A ransomware attack infected 26 Romanian hospitals through shared medical software, forcing more than 100 hospitals offline for four days starting February 10, 2024.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Rimal district, Gaza City
events.body:
A 17-year-old girl was walking to school to take an exam when she was killed by an Israeli airstrike in the Rimal district of Gaza City. A paramedic was also killed in the same strike, which targeted a vehicle in the area. Four other people were wounded.

The girl had been on her way to sit an exam when the strike occurred. Her death drew widespread attention because she was killed while attempting to continue her education during the ongoing conflict.

Four additional people were wounded in the strike. Civilians, including students and medical workers, continue to be caught in the violence across Gaza.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: A 17-year-old girl and a paramedic killed in a strike in Gaza City's Rimal district
events.alert_summary: An Israeli strike in Gaza City's Rimal district killed a 17-year-old girl and a paramedic, wounding four others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in central Philippines
events.body:
Three students were killed and seven others wounded in a school shooting in the central Philippines. The incident is described as rare for the country.

Two teenage suspects are believed to have carried out the attack. Investigators are looking into whether a grudge over bullying was the motive.

The seven wounded students remain at risk, and the broader school community is affected by the violence.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Three students killed and seven wounded in a school shooting in the central Philippines
events.alert_summary: A school shooting in the central Philippines killed 3 students and wounded 7 others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kyaikto
events.body:
A funeral held for victims of an earlier airstrike in Kyaikto was itself targeted by another military airstrike, according to Karen News. The attack struck mourners gathered to bury those already killed in the first strike.

This kind of repeated strike on the same community — first hitting civilians, then hitting those who gather in the aftermath — has been reported in other parts of Myanmar during the ongoing conflict between the military and resistance forces. Such attacks leave communities unable to grieve or recover safely.

Kyaikto is located in Mon State. Residents in the area face ongoing risk, and the back-to-back strikes will likely push more families to flee the area.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Mourners in Kyaikto struck by a second airstrike while burying victims of the first attack
events.alert_summary: A military airstrike struck a funeral gathering in Kyaikto for victims of a previous airstrike; casualty figures not confirmed in source.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mon State
events.body:
Myanmar's military carried out an airstrike in Mon State that damaged homes belonging to former members of the New Mon State Party (NMSP), a Mon ethnic armed group that reached a ceasefire agreement with the junta years ago.

The strike targeted a civilian area associated with people who had laid down arms, raising concern among residents about the safety of those living in former rebel-affiliated communities. Details on casualties were not available from the source material.

Mon State, in southeastern Myanmar, has seen periodic military activity despite past peace agreements. Civilians in the area remain at risk as the junta continues air operations across the country.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families of former NMSP members in Mon State whose homes were struck by airstrikes
events.alert_summary: A junta airstrike damaged homes of former NMSP members in Mon State, Myanmar.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Myanmar
events.body:
A United Nations report found that Myanmar's military killed more than 700 civilians over a six-month period. The findings point to a sustained pattern of violence against civilian populations across the country during the ongoing civil conflict.

The UN's documentation reflects continued attacks on villages and populated areas, a pattern that has been ongoing since the military took power in 2021. Civilians, including women and children, have been among those killed.

The report adds to a growing body of international concern over conditions inside Myanmar, where millions have been displaced and access for humanitarian workers remains restricted. For people living in conflict-affected areas, the findings reflect daily dangers that continue with little sign of easing.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians across Myanmar killed by military attacks over six months
events.alert_summary: The UN found Myanmar's military killed over 700 civilians in six months, with no single location specified.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Baidoa
events.body:
Fighting broke out near Baidoa, a city in southwestern Somalia, between Somali government forces and rebel fighters. The source does not specify which armed group was involved, though al-Shabaab remains the primary rebel force operating in the Bay region where Baidoa is located.

Baidoa serves as the capital of South West State and has been a recurring flashpoint in Somalia's long-running conflict. Clashes in and around the city put local residents at risk and can disrupt humanitarian operations in an area that already faces food insecurity.

No casualty numbers or details about the scale of the fighting were available from this source at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians near Baidoa caught between Somali forces and rebel fighters
events.alert_summary: Clashes broke out near Baidoa between Somali government forces and rebel fighters; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mandera
events.body:
Kenyan Defence Forces stopped a suspected al-Shabaab attack targeting a security camp in Mandera, a county in northeastern Kenya near the Somali and Ethiopian borders. The area has seen repeated activity by the Somalia-based armed group over the years.

No casualty figures were reported in connection with this incident. Kenyan forces are regularly stationed in the region due to the ongoing threat posed by al-Shabaab, which has carried out attacks on both military and civilian targets across the border region.

Mandera residents live with the constant possibility of armed incidents, and the presence of security forces provides some protection, though the threat in the area remains.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Soldiers and civilians in Mandera facing an armed attack on a security camp
events.alert_summary: Kenyan forces repelled a suspected al-Shabaab attack on a security camp in Mandera; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in El Obeid
events.body:
A regional body called the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) has urged an immediate ceasefire in Sudan's El Obeid region, citing drone attacks and rising violence in the area. El Obeid is the capital of North Kordofan state and a significant population center in central Sudan.

The call reflects concern that fighting in the area is putting civilians at risk. Drone attacks have become an increasingly common feature of Sudan's ongoing war, which has displaced millions of people since conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces broke out in April 2023.

No specific casualty figures were provided in the report. Residents of El Obeid and surrounding communities face the threat of aerial attacks on top of the broader disruptions caused by more than two years of war.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in El Obeid caught in drone attacks and ongoing fighting
events.alert_summary: IGAD called for an immediate ceasefire in Sudan's El Obeid region following drone attacks and reported violence.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Southern Somalia
events.body:
U.S. forces carried out an airstrike against al-Shabaab in southern Somalia, according to a report from FSX Business. No further details about the specific location, date, or outcome were provided in the available source material.

Al-Shabaab is an armed group that has operated across Somalia for years, regularly attacking civilians, government forces, and aid workers. U.S. military strikes in Somalia have been ongoing as part of efforts to weaken the group's presence and capabilities.

No casualty figures — for fighters or civilians — were included in the report. People living in areas where al-Shabaab operates and where airstrikes occur face risks from both the group's activities and the fighting itself.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Somalia near al-Shabaab territory during U.S. airstrikes
events.alert_summary: U.S. forces conducted an airstrike targeting al-Shabaab militants in southern Somalia; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kordofan
events.body:
Fighters from the Rapid Support Forces and allied rebel groups attacked communities in Kordofan province in Sudan, killing 14 people. The attack is part of the ongoing conflict between Sudan's military and the RSF that has been tearing through the country since April 2023.

Kordofan has seen repeated violence throughout the war, with civilian areas caught between the warring sides. Residents of the region have faced threats to their safety, movement, and access to food and basic services.

The death toll from the broader conflict now runs into the tens of thousands, with millions displaced across Sudan and into neighboring countries.
events.source_title: "Sudan RSF OR fighting OR airstrike OR El Fasher when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: 14 people killed in Kordofan and communities caught in ongoing RSF attacks
events.alert_summary: An RSF and allied rebel attack in Sudan's Kordofan province killed 14 people.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Niamey
events.body:
An attack took place at the airport in Niamey, Niger's capital, according to reporting that also noted a reaction from the U.S. embassy in the country. Details about casualties, the number of attackers, or the group responsible were not available in the source material.

The U.S. embassy issued a statement in response to the incident, though the content of that statement was not included in the available summary.

Niamey is Niger's largest city and political center. An attack on its airport would affect civilian travel, international operations, and security across the capital.
events.source_title: "Sahel OR Burkina Faso OR Mali OR Niger attack OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians and airport workers in Niamey following an armed attack
events.alert_summary: An attack occurred at Niamey's airport, prompting a response from the U.S. embassy in Niger.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oyo
events.body:
Security forces have surrounded the area where students were taken from a school in Oyo state, Nigeria, working to cut off routes the kidnappers could use to move the children. Reports indicate the armed group has lowered their ransom demands, though negotiations are ongoing and the students remain in captivity.

The situation is still unresolved. Families of the missing students are waiting for news as military and security personnel maintain pressure on the kidnappers. No casualty figures have been reported in connection with this incident.

School kidnappings have become a serious concern in parts of Nigeria, and this case has drawn attention from both local authorities and the wider public watching for updates on the students' safety.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Students kidnapped from a school in Oyo state, families waiting for their return
events.alert_summary: Armed groups kidnapped students from a school in Oyo state as troops moved to block escape routes.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tacloban
events.body:
Two students, aged 14 and 15, opened fire inside a classroom at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City, killing three students and wounding six others — three by gunfire and four hurt while fleeing. One suspect was arrested shortly after the attack; the other surrendered later. Investigators say the shooting appeared to be motivated by a grudge over bullying.

The weapons used included a 9mm pistol belonging to a policewoman related to one of the suspects, who is now in custody. About 40 shell casings were recovered from the scene. Officials noted that warning signs in the teens' behavior had been visible on social media but were not acted upon.

Authorities and the education ministry have announced a review of school security, bullying policies, and student monitoring. Police are calling on gun owners to secure their firearms, and the policewoman whose weapon was used faces potential charges.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Three students killed and seven others wounded in a school shooting in Tacloban, Philippines
events.alert_summary: Two teenage students opened fire at a high school in Tacloban, killing 3 and wounding 7 others.

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Entebbe
events.body:
A Kenyan lawyer and former justice minister was stopped at Entebbe airport and ordered to return home while attempting to join a legal team defending a Ugandan opposition figure. She was reportedly held at the airport and remained unreachable, with her phones switched off, as of Monday. A colleague who traveled with her for the same purpose was allowed to enter without issue.

The lawyer had been part of a team representing an opposition leader currently jailed on treason charges since being brought back to Uganda from Kenya in late 2024. A second Ugandan lawyer on the same defense team was arrested at his home last week, appeared in court looking unwell, denied related charges, and was sent to prison pending further proceedings.

Ugandan immigration authorities have not responded to questions about why entry was denied. The incident raises concerns about access to legal representation for those facing treason charges in the country.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Detained opposition lawyers and their families in Uganda facing restricted legal representation
events.alert_summary: Ugandan authorities blocked a Kenyan lawyer from entering the country at Entebbe airport; no reason was given.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sagaing
events.body:
A UN Human Rights report covering August 2024 through January 2025 found that Myanmar's military killed at least 702 civilians during the country's election period, including 224 women and 153 children. Air strikes were identified as the single largest cause of death and destruction. The Sagaing region saw the most civilian deaths, with 191 people killed.

Two attacks in Sagaing were documented in detail. In October, munitions struck a crowd gathered outside a school in Chaung-U during a candlelit event, killing 23 people including four children and wounding more than 60 others. In December, a military aircraft bombed a tea shop in Tabayin where people had gathered to watch a football match, killing at least 19 and wounding 20 more.

The report also notes that Rohingya people have faced forced recruitment, killings, arbitrary arrests, and sexual violence. UN officials warned that cuts to international aid are making conditions worse for millions of civilians across the country.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: 702 civilians, including 153 children, killed by military attacks across Myanmar in six months
events.alert_summary: Myanmar's military killed at least 702 civilians, including 153 children, over six months from August to January, a UN report found.

events.title: Ras Laffan
events.body:
An explosion at a natural gas processing facility in Qatar's Ras Laffan industrial zone killed at least 13 workers and injured 66 others on Sunday night. All of those killed were nationals of India and Pakistan. The blast lit up the sky over the area and was felt as far as 70 kilometers away in central Doha, where residents reported rattling windows and widespread alarm.

Qatar's energy minister described the incident as an accident, not sabotage, and said it posed no environmental risk. The facility had been shut down since December 2025 for maintenance and had only restarted operations two days before the explosion. An investigation into the cause is underway.

The facility had already suffered damage from Iranian strikes earlier in the year during regional conflict, which had halted about a fifth of the world's LNG supply. The energy minister said it would be difficult to say when operations would resume, leaving the timeline for recovery uncertain.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Families of 13 workers killed in the Ras Laffan gas facility explosion, many from India and Pakistan
events.alert_summary: An explosion at the Barzan gas facility in Ras Laffan killed 13 and injured 66, all victims from India and Pakistan.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tyre
events.body:
A 77-year-old marine ecologist and turtle conservationist died from wounds she received when an Israeli air strike hit her home near Tyre, in southern Lebanon, on June 4. She had been severely injured in the strike and did not survive her injuries.

People across Lebanon have been mourning her death. She was known for her work protecting sea turtles along the Lebanese coast.

Her death is one of many civilian losses in Lebanon since fighting intensified in the region. Residents near Tyre continue to face danger from ongoing strikes in the south of the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: A 77-year-old marine ecologist and her community near Tyre, mourning a life lost to an air strike
events.alert_summary: An Israeli air strike on a home near Tyre on June 4 fatally wounded a 77-year-old turtle conservationist, who has since died.

events.title: Displacement in Ituri
events.body:
The Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has passed 1,000 confirmed cases, with 254 deaths and 365 people currently in hospital or isolation since the outbreak was declared on May 15. It is caused by the Bundibugyo strain, for which there is no approved vaccine or specific treatment. Health officials say contact tracing has reached only about 55 percent of those potentially exposed, and many infections are likely going undetected.

Armed attacks by a group linked to ISIL have cut off villages in Ituri province and forced thousands to flee into overcrowded camps. At one camp near Bunia, more than 20,000 people are sheltering. Officials reported 10 unexplained deaths at that site last week and called for an urgent investigation, though no Ebola cases have been confirmed there.

Community leaders at the camp warn that the crowded and fragile conditions could turn any outbreak into a wider disaster.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Over 20,000 displaced civilians crowded into camps in Ituri amid an Ebola outbreak with no approved vaccine
events.alert_summary: Ebola has infected 1,003 people and killed 254 in DR Congo's Ituri province since May 15, 2026.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Voronezh
events.body:
Ukraine's military struck a missile electronics plant in Russia's Voronezh region and the Dubna satellite communications centre near Moscow using air-launched cruise missiles. Russian officials confirmed the Voronezh plant was damaged and three people were injured. Russia downed 301 Ukrainian drones overnight, temporarily closing four airports near Moscow.

In Ukraine, a Russian drone attack in the Sumy region killed a 13-year-old boy, his father, and an elderly woman. Overnight drone strikes on Zaporizhzhia killed two people and wounded seven. An Iskander ballistic missile hit an agricultural facility in the Odesa region on Sunday evening, killing one and wounding three, and setting vehicles and fuel tanks on fire.

A Russian drone also struck a Turkish cargo ship in the Black Sea, killing one crew member — an Egyptian cook — and forcing eight others to evacuate by lifeboat.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Odesa killed or wounded in overnight drone and missile strikes
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian strikes hit a missile electronics plant in Voronezh and a satellite centre near Moscow; Russian attacks killed at least 6 and wounded 10 across Ukraine.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kwara
events.body:
Two people who had been held captive by bandits in a forest camp in Kwara State managed to escape when a gun battle broke out among their captors. The fighting between armed factions gave the victims an opening to flee.

Kidnapping for ransom has become a growing problem across several states in Nigeria, with rural and forested areas often used as holding grounds for victims. Bandit groups in these regions frequently clash with one another over money, territory, or disputes over ransom payments.

The two survivors are now free, but the broader situation in the area remains uncertain. Residents living near affected forest zones continue to face the risk of abduction.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Two kidnapping survivors who escaped a forest camp during a gun battle in Kwara
events.alert_summary: Two kidnap victims escaped a bandit camp in a Kwara forest during a gun battle among their captors.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Amarillo
events.body:
A gang-related shootout in Amarillo, Texas sent six people to the hospital. The incident appears to involve rival gang members, though specific details about what led to the shooting were not available in early reports.

Six people received hospital care following the exchange of gunfire. The extent of their injuries was not specified in initial coverage.

Residents in the area where the shooting occurred may face ongoing concerns about safety as authorities investigate.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Six people wounded in a gang-related shooting in Amarillo, Texas
events.alert_summary: A gang-related shootout in Amarillo, Texas left six people hospitalized; no fatalities reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza
events.body:
Israeli forces carried out an operation in Gaza that local sources say resulted in the deaths of a man and a young girl. The Israeli military stated the strike also eliminated an individual it identified as a participant in the October 7, 2023 attacks.

The deaths of the two civilians have not been independently verified. The military's account and reports from Gaza differ, a pattern common throughout the ongoing conflict. No figures for wounded were given in the source.

Civilian casualties continue to be reported across Gaza as Israeli military operations persist. Families in the area remain at risk from ongoing strikes.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Gazan civilians, including a girl, killed during an IDF operation in Gaza
events.alert_summary: An IDF operation in Gaza killed a man and a girl; the military says it also eliminated a participant in the October 7 attacks.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Shevchenkove
events.body:
A Russian airstrike struck the town of Shevchenkove, hitting a school and residential homes. Civilians were wounded in the attack.

The strike caused structural damage to the school building and nearby houses. The full extent of the damage and the number of wounded were not specified in available reporting.

Residents in the area face ongoing risk as strikes continue to affect civilian infrastructure, including places used by children.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Wounded civilians and families in Shevchenkove following an airstrike on homes and a school
events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike hit Shevchenkove, wounding civilians and damaging a school and homes.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Shevchenkove, Kharkiv region
events.body:
An airstrike struck the town of Shevchenkove in the Kharkiv region of northeastern Ukraine, injuring civilians, according to Ukrainian national news reporting. The number of people wounded and the extent of damage to buildings or infrastructure were not specified in the available report.

Shevchenkove is a small town in Kharkiv Oblast, a region that has experienced repeated attacks since the early stages of Russia's full-scale invasion. Strikes in this area frequently affect residential neighborhoods and local infrastructure.

Residents in the affected area face ongoing risk from aerial attacks, with emergency and medical personnel responding to casualties on the ground.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Shevchenkove, Kharkiv region wounded in an airstrike
events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike struck Shevchenkove in Ukraine's Kharkiv region, injuring civilians; no casualty count reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Anbar
events.body:
Iraqi forces launched airstrikes against an ISIS hideout in the Anbar desert, according to reporting from Rudaw. Anbar province, which borders Syria, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia, has long been an area where ISIS remnants have sought refuge in remote desert terrain.

No casualty figures were given in the report. Iraqi security forces have continued periodic operations in this region as part of ongoing efforts to prevent ISIS from regrouping in sparsely populated desert areas.

Civilians living in or near Anbar's desert zones remain in an uncertain environment as military activity continues in the area.
events.source_title: "Syria OR Iraq airstrike OR clashes OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Anbar desert region amid ongoing military operations against armed groups
events.alert_summary: Iraqi forces carried out airstrikes targeting an ISIS hideout in the Anbar desert; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ye Township
events.body:
Military aircraft struck a housing complex in Ye Township, destroying five buildings. Among the structures destroyed was a primary school serving the local community. The site was associated with the Nippon Foundation, an international organization involved in humanitarian and development work in the region.

Details on casualties were not included in the source material. The destruction of a school alongside residential buildings raises concerns for families and children living in the area.

Ye Township is in Mon State in southern Myanmar. Airstrikes on civilian infrastructure have been a recurring pattern in the conflict between Myanmar's military and armed resistance groups across the country.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents near destroyed homes and a primary school in Ye Township
events.alert_summary: An airstrike destroyed five buildings, including a primary school, at a Nippon Foundation housing site in Ye Township.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Hiiraan
events.body:
Clan fighting has broken out in the Hiiraan region of Somalia, prompting the country's parliament speaker to call for a ceasefire. The appeal signals that the clashes have reached a level serious enough to draw a response from national leadership.

Hiiraan is a central Somalia region that has seen recurring clan-based violence over the years. When such clashes flare, local communities often bear the heaviest burden, with civilians caught in the crossfire and daily life disrupted.

No casualty figures or details about the specific clans involved were included in available reporting. It is not yet known whether the ceasefire call has had any effect on the fighting.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Hiiraan caught between ongoing clan clashes with no ceasefire yet in place
events.alert_summary: Somalia's parliament speaker called for a ceasefire amid clan clashes in the Hiiraan region; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Haiti
events.body:
Armed men kidnapped a security expert in Haiti, according to reporting from AOL.com. The person was described as respected in the security field. No further details about the location within Haiti or the timing of the abduction were available from the source summary.

Haiti has been dealing with widespread gang activity that has affected daily life across much of the country. Kidnappings have become a common threat, targeting people from many walks of life, including those working in security, humanitarian, and community roles.

The fate of the kidnapped individual was not reported in the available source material.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A kidnapped security expert and families living under gang violence in Haiti
events.alert_summary: Armed men kidnapped a security expert in Haiti; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Crimea
events.body:
Gas sales across Russian-controlled Crimea have stopped following Ukrainian drone strikes on the peninsula's fuel supply route. The strikes disrupted the flow of fuel to the region, causing shortages that emptied stations and left drivers unable to fill their vehicles.

With no fuel available, residents have begun looking for other ways to get around. The scope of the disruption is not yet clear from available reports, but the halt affects everyday movement for people living in the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Crimea facing fuel shortages after strikes on supply routes
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian drone strikes on Crimea's supply route halted gas sales, leaving drivers without fuel.

events.title: Ras Laffan Industrial City
events.body:
An explosion struck the Barzan factory at Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar's main liquefied natural gas processing hub, on Monday. Authorities attributed the blast to a "technical malfunction." Emergency teams were deployed and brought a resulting fire under control.

Qatar's Ministry of Interior reported 54 injuries but gave no information on the conditions of those hurt. Eighteen people remained missing as search and rescue teams conducted operations at the site. Officials stated there was no leakage posing a danger to public safety.

Ras Laffan, located about 80 kilometers north of Doha, produces roughly one-fifth of the world's LNG supply. The facility had previously sustained damage in Iranian missile and drone strikes in March, which had already disrupted supply contracts with customers in Italy, Belgium, South Korea, and China.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: 54 injured workers and 18 people missing after an explosion at Ras Laffan Industrial City
events.alert_summary: An explosion at Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City injured 54 people and left 18 missing, authorities say.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Southern Lebanon
events.body:
An airstrike in southern Lebanon killed seven people, according to reporting from Oz Arab Media. No further details about the specific location, the identities of those killed, or the circumstances of the strike were provided in the available source material.

Southern Lebanon has seen recurring strikes in the context of ongoing hostilities between Israeli forces and armed groups operating in the region. Communities in the area continue to face the risk of further strikes.

Residents in southern Lebanese towns and villages remain in a difficult position, with uncertainty about safety and access to services for those who have stayed in the area.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven people killed in southern Lebanon by an airstrike, and communities living under continued threat
events.alert_summary: An airstrike killed seven people in southern Lebanon, according to reports.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Batangas
events.body:
Three people — including a toddler and an elderly woman — were killed in what authorities described as a family massacre in Batangas, Philippines. The victims were related, pointing to a targeted attack within a household.

Details on the suspected motive and the number of those involved remain limited from available reporting. Authorities are investigating the circumstances of the killings.

Batangas is a province south of Manila. Incidents like this affect entire communities, as neighbors and extended family members are left to cope with the aftermath of sudden household violence.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A family in Batangas, including a toddler and an elderly woman, killed in a massacre
events.alert_summary: Three family members, including a toddler and a grandmother, were killed in a massacre in Batangas.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
An airstrike struck Gaza City, killing a father and his two daughters along with other victims. The attack was reported by ABNA English, though specific details on the total casualty count, the neighborhood struck, and the timing of the strike were not included in the available summary.

The deaths of children alongside a parent reflect the ongoing toll on families in Gaza. Civilians in the area continue to face danger from strikes, with little ability to seek safety.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A father and his two daughters killed in an airstrike on Gaza City
events.alert_summary: An airstrike on Gaza City killed a father and his two daughters among the victims reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in White Nile, North Kordofan
events.body:
Drone strikes hit civilian areas in two regions of Sudan — White Nile and North Kordofan — killing residents, according to reports. At the same time, the Rapid Support Forces claimed to have made advances in the Blue Nile region, widening the footprint of Sudan's ongoing war.

Sudan's conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the RSF has continued to spread into new areas since fighting began in April 2023. Drone attacks on non-combat areas have been a recurring concern throughout the war.

Civilians in all three affected regions face danger from both ground fighting and aerial strikes.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in White Nile and North Kordofan killed in drone strikes amid ongoing fighting
events.alert_summary: Drone strikes killed civilians in White Nile and North Kordofan while RSF forces claimed territorial gains in Blue Nile.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Niamey
events.body:
An armed group known as JNIM carried out an attack on the airport in Niamey, Niger's capital, killing 13 people. The group later claimed responsibility for the strike.

Niamey is the seat of Niger's government, making an attack on its airport particularly significant. Airports and capital-city infrastructure are rarely targeted in the Sahel's ongoing conflict, and this incident signals that violence is reaching areas previously considered more secure.

Details on the wounded or further damage to the airport were not available in the source. Residents in and around Niamey should be aware that the security situation in the capital has deteriorated.
events.source_title: "Sahel OR Burkina Faso OR Mali OR Niger attack OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families of the 13 killed in the Niamey airport attack, and civilians near the site
events.alert_summary: An armed group attacked Niamey's airport, killing 13 people, and later claimed responsibility.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Chibok
events.body:
A Boko Haram attack in the Chibok area of northeastern Nigeria killed a newborn baby who was only 12 hours old. Chibok is a town that has faced repeated armed group violence over the years, and this latest incident shows that attacks continue to affect civilians in the region.

Details on the full scope of the attack, including any other casualties or damage, are not available from the source summary alone. The death of a newborn in the attack underscores the vulnerability of families living in areas where Boko Haram remains active.

Residents in and around Chibok continue to face the threat of armed attacks with little warning, making everyday life, including childbirth and basic family life, dangerous.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families in Chibok facing armed attacks, including a newborn killed hours after birth
events.alert_summary: A Boko Haram attack in Chibok killed a 12-hour-old baby; no total casualty figure was reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Borno
events.body:
Armed fighters believed to be affiliated with Boko Haram attacked a town in Borno State, in northeastern Nigeria, and kidnapped farmers during the raid. The number of people taken and whether any casualties occurred were not reported in available information.

Borno State has long been a center of activity for Boko Haram and affiliated armed groups, and farmers in rural areas remain especially vulnerable to abductions and attacks. Kidnapping of civilians working in fields is a recurring pattern in the region.

Families of those taken face uncertainty about their loved ones' safety and whereabouts.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Farmers in Borno kidnapped by armed fighters, families awaiting their return
events.alert_summary: Suspected Boko Haram fighters attacked a town in Borno and kidnapped an unknown number of farmers.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kerch
events.body:
Russian-backed authorities in occupied Crimea have suspended public fuel sales after a Ukrainian drone strike hit an oil depot in the city of Kerch, killing four people and injuring 28. The regional governor said fuel would only be available to government agencies responsible for the territory's operations, with individuals and businesses turned away from petrol stations.

Ukraine also struck a logistics facility for oil transport in Russia's Krasnodar region, where one person was killed on a passenger ferry. Ukraine's president described the strikes as a response to Russian attacks and said at least seven people were killed in Russian strikes over the weekend, with children among more than 30 injured.

Crimea had already been experiencing fuel shortages due to Ukraine's ongoing campaign against supply routes. The fuel suspension marks the most significant restriction residents have faced so far, leaving some travelers unable to find petrol to return home.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Crimea and Ukraine caught between fuel shortages and ongoing drone strikes
events.alert_summary: A Ukrainian drone strike on an oil depot in Kerch killed 4 and injured 28, prompting authorities to halt public fuel sales across Crimea.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Santa Cruz
events.body:
Bolivia declared a state of emergency after five weeks of anti-government protests over austerity measures, including cuts to fuel subsidies. The decree banned road blockades and authorized the military to support police in restoring order. By Sunday, authorities reported no active blockades in the country.

Early signs of progress emerged: in Santa Cruz, officials and protest leaders signed an agreement to lift a blockade in San Julian, and a federation of rural and Indigenous groups in La Paz announced a pause in demonstrations, though without dropping their demands for the president's resignation.

At least 17 people have died, many from disruptions to medical care caused by blocked roads. Violent clashes resulted in 365 arrests and 37 injuries. Highways still require clean-up and repair, and military and police remained deployed. Rights observers cautioned that unresolved underlying grievances could lead to renewed unrest.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Bolivian communities cut off from food, fuel, and medicine by five weeks of road blockades
events.alert_summary: Bolivia's state of emergency followed five weeks of blockades that killed at least 17 and led to 365 arrests.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Jantar Mantar, New Delhi
events.body:
Dozens of protesters connected to India's "Cockroach Janta Party," a youth-led political movement, have camped overnight at Jantar Mantar, a designated protest site in New Delhi, defying police orders to leave. Police cut off food and water access at points to pressure the crowd, but protesters say they will not leave until the federal education minister resigns.

The demonstrations were sparked by repeated exam paper leaks and score irregularities affecting millions of students. India's top medical entrance exam was cancelled last month after its question paper appeared to have been leaked. Nearly 1.7 million students retook the exam recently. In the days between the two exam dates, more than a dozen students across India died by suicide.

Protesters first gathered in New Delhi on June 6, with demonstrations since spreading to Mumbai, Bengaluru, and Nagpur. Many attending are first-time protesters, some staying against their parents' wishes, and say they no longer trust the fairness of competitive exams.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Young Indian students sleeping on streets demanding accountability after exam paper leaks and student suicides
events.alert_summary: Protesters camped overnight at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar demanding the education minister's resignation; more than a dozen students died by suicide between two exam dates.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Bagudo
events.body:
Police in Kebbi State shot and killed the leader of a kidnapping gang during an operation in Bagudo, a local government area in northwestern Nigeria. One other member of the gang was arrested during the same operation.

Kidnapping gangs, sometimes called bandits, have been active across northwestern Nigeria for several years, targeting civilians for ransom. Bagudo sits near the Niger River and has seen periodic attacks on communities in the area.

No hostages or casualties among civilians were reported in connection with this specific operation. Residents in the region continue to face the threat of abduction, which disrupts daily life, farming, and movement between towns and villages.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Bagudo affected by kidnapping gangs and ongoing insecurity
events.alert_summary: Police killed a kidnap gang leader and arrested one member in Bagudo, Kebbi State.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Delhi
events.body:
A woman working as an intern for a non-governmental organization in Delhi was stripped and molested by a mob after a kidnapping scare triggered the attack. The source does not detail how the scare began, but the incident escalated into a crowd assaulting the woman publicly.

Mob violence linked to rumors or suspicion of kidnapping has occurred periodically in India, where misinformation can spread quickly and lead to crowd attacks before authorities can intervene. The source does not report an arrest count or state whether police arrived during the assault.

The woman's condition and any subsequent police action are not described in the available source material. Residents and workers in the area would be aware of the risk such rumor-driven crowd responses pose to individuals caught in similar situations.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Young woman in Delhi attacked and molested by a mob following a kidnapping scare
events.alert_summary: A mob stripped and molested a woman working as an NGO intern in Delhi following a kidnapping scare; no casualty count reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
An airstrike in southern Lebanon killed seven people, according to reports from Oz Arab Media. No further details about the specific town or district were available from the source.

The casualties add to an ongoing pattern of airstrikes in southern Lebanon. No information was provided about the identities of those killed or whether any were civilians.

Residents in affected areas remain at risk as long as aerial operations continue in the region.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven people killed in an airstrike in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: An airstrike killed seven people in southern Lebanon, according to reports.

events.title: Armed Conflict in central Gaza
events.body:
An Al Jazeera cameraman was killed when an Israeli airstrike hit his home in central Gaza, according to reporting by Anadolu Ajansı. No further details about the location within central Gaza or the timing of the strike were available in the source material.

The killing of a journalist in his home adds to concerns about the safety of media workers and civilians throughout Gaza. Journalists covering the conflict have faced repeated losses since fighting escalated.

No information was available in the source about other casualties at the site or the current conditions for residents in the surrounding area.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Al Jazeera cameraman and family members killed in an airstrike on their home in central Gaza
events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike on a home in central Gaza killed an Al Jazeera cameraman; no casualty figure reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Northern Syria
events.body:
An attack in northern Syria killed two soldiers, according to state media. No further details about the attackers, the exact location, or the circumstances of the assault were included in the report.

Northern Syria has seen ongoing instability involving multiple armed groups, government forces, and foreign military presences. Civilians in the region continue to live under the uncertainty of sporadic violence.
events.source_title: "Syria OR Iraq airstrike OR clashes OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Two soldiers and their families following a deadly attack in northern Syria
events.alert_summary: An attack in northern Syria killed two soldiers, according to state media.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kebbi
events.body:
A man described as a prominent kidnapping leader was killed in Kebbi state, in northwestern Nigeria. The operation appears to mark the end of a figure who had been linked to abductions in the region, though the source does not detail how the operation was carried out or whether others were killed or captured alongside him.

Kebbi state borders Niger state and sits within a broader zone of Nigeria's northwest where armed groups have carried out kidnappings for ransom affecting farmers, students, travelers, and rural families. The removal of one leader does not necessarily signal the end of kidnapping activity in the area, as such groups often continue under other leadership.

Residents in affected communities have lived under the threat of abduction for years, and news of this kind is often welcomed locally as a sign that security forces are active in the region.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities in Kebbi state long affected by kidnapping and armed criminal violence
events.alert_summary: Security forces killed a kidnapping leader in Kebbi state; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oyo
events.body:
Police in Oyo State, Nigeria, arrested one suspect connected to the kidnapping of a farmer, as authorities continue searching for the victim. The arrest marks a development in an active abduction case, though the farmer's location remains unknown.

Kidnapping of farmers has become a recurring problem across several Nigerian states, where rural workers are targeted while working in their fields, sometimes held for ransom. The situation leaves farming families in fear and disrupts local food production.

No details were available about when the abduction occurred, how many suspects may still be at large, or whether a ransom demand has been made. The search for the missing farmer is ongoing.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Abducted farmer in Oyo State and family waiting for news of safe return
events.alert_summary: Police in Oyo State arrested one kidnapper while searching for an abducted farmer; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sidon
events.body:
Lebanon's Health Ministry reported that an airstrike on the southern coastal city of Sidon killed 7 people and wounded 13 others. The figures come directly from official health authorities tracking casualties across the country.

Sidon is one of Lebanon's largest cities and has seen periodic strikes during ongoing hostilities. Casualties of this scale affect not only the victims but also medical staff, families, and residents living near the strike location.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven people killed and 13 wounded in an airstrike on Sidon, Lebanon
events.alert_summary: An airstrike on Sidon killed 7 people and wounded 13, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza
events.body:
Israeli airstrikes in Gaza killed four people, one of whom was a Palestinian journalist. The strikes add to a continuing pattern of casualties among media workers and civilians in the territory.

The death of a journalist raises concerns about the safety of those reporting from inside Gaza, where access for outside media remains restricted. Residents continue to face the risks of ongoing strikes with limited ability to seek safety or leave affected areas.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Palestinian journalist and three others killed in Gaza airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza killed four people, including a Palestinian journalist.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Rakhine state
events.body:
A military airstrike struck Rakhine state in western Myanmar, killing seven civilians, according to reports. The attack is the latest in a series of airstrikes carried out in areas where fighting has continued between the military and armed groups.

Rakhine state has seen ongoing conflict involving multiple armed factions, and civilians in the region have faced repeated displacement and casualties. Communities there have few safe options, as fighting has disrupted access to food, medical care, and shelter for many residents.

The deaths add to a growing toll of civilian casualties across Myanmar since the military took power in 2021. Airstrikes have become a frequent concern in conflict-affected states, and local communities bear the greatest cost.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven civilians in Rakhine state killed in a military airstrike
events.alert_summary: A military airstrike killed seven civilians in Rakhine state, Myanmar.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oyo Forest
events.body:
Fighters believed to be affiliated with Boko Haram have planted improvised explosive devices in a forest in Oyo state, Nigeria, while stepping up attacks against security forces attempting to rescue abducted schoolchildren. The group appears to be using the explosives to slow or block military and rescue operations in the area.

The schoolchildren remain in captivity as security forces work to locate and free them. The use of IEDs adds significant danger to any rescue operation, putting both soldiers and the children at greater risk.

Families of the missing children continue to wait for news as the situation in the forest remains unstable.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Schoolchildren held captive in Oyo Forest while armed groups plant explosives to block their rescue
events.alert_summary: Armed fighters planted IEDs in Oyo Forest and escalated attacks to block rescue efforts for abducted schoolchildren.

events.title: Armed Conflict in near Mexico City
events.body:
A man and a woman from Chicago, Illinois, were found dead in the area near Mexico City after their disappearance went unresolved for weeks. The circumstances of their deaths were not detailed in available reporting, but the case drew attention given the ongoing dangers facing foreign nationals traveling in parts of Mexico affected by cartel activity.

The region around Mexico City has seen periodic violence linked to organized crime. Authorities were involved in the search before the bodies were located, though details on suspects or the investigation's status were not provided in the source material.

Foreign visitors and expatriates in central Mexico face real risks of kidnapping and targeted violence. Families and communities with members living or traveling in the region should remain aware of local conditions and heed any guidance from local contacts and consular services.
events.source_title: "Mexico cartel violence OR kidnapping OR gunmen when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families of two Americans killed near Mexico City, and travelers in the region facing ongoing cartel violence
events.alert_summary: A couple from Chicago were found dead near Mexico City after going missing for several weeks.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mansouri beach
events.body:
A 76-year-old Lebanese environmental activist died after her home on Mansouri beach, near the southern city of Tyre, was struck during Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon roughly two weeks ago. She had spent more than 25 years protecting endangered loggerhead and green sea turtles that nest along Lebanon's southern coast. Friends said she refused to evacuate, believing she was safe as a civilian.

Her conservation work began in 1999 after she encountered a green turtle laying eggs on the same beach she would later dedicate her life to protecting. She helped establish an eco-tourism and conservation center there in 2000 that grew into a hub for environmental education and marine research, drawing volunteers from around the world.

Colleagues said her home had also been damaged in the 2006 war, yet she remained. Her death comes as airstrikes have continued across southern Lebanon, raising concerns for civilians who have stayed in the area despite ongoing conflict.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: A 76-year-old conservationist killed by an airstrike on her home in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike struck a home on Mansouri beach near Tyre, killing a 76-year-old conservationist after she spent several days in hospital.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Edinburgh
events.body:
A 36-year-old man was charged Sunday in connection with a series of attacks that took place across Edinburgh on Friday, June 19. Police responded to reports of violent incidents including threats, robbery, and vandalism at multiple locations across the city. Counterterrorism police launched a probe into the incidents, and a major road connecting the city center to the Leith suburb was closed.

British media reported the attacks appeared to begin near a mosque in western Edinburgh and continued through other parts of the city. Social media footage showed a man carrying a weapon before being restrained by police. Five people were injured; three required hospital treatment.

Britain's prime minister described the attacks as motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred" and condemned them publicly. Scotland's First Minister also condemned the violence. The incidents follow a broader pattern of anti-Muslim and anti-migrant unrest across Britain, including riots in Belfast and other cities.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Five people injured in anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh, including three hospitalized
events.alert_summary: A man was charged after attacks on Friday in Edinburgh left five people injured, three requiring hospital treatment.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Bureij refugee camp
events.body:
A 25-year-old Al Jazeera cameraman was killed when an Israeli airstrike struck a house in Bureij refugee camp in central Gaza. He was the 12th Al Jazeera journalist killed by Israel in Gaza since October 2023. Two other Palestinians also died in the same strike.

The cameraman's older brother, also an Al Jazeera correspondent, had been killed weeks earlier when his car was struck by Israeli shelling. After that loss, the younger brother continued working, filmed coverage of the war, and took on care of his late brother's children. Colleagues remembered him as polite, warm, and dedicated.

At least 260 Palestinian journalists have been killed in Gaza since October 2023, according to the Committee to Protect Journalists. Gaza is now described as the deadliest place in the world for journalists. Airstrikes have continued in the territory despite a ceasefire announced in October.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinian journalists and their families in Gaza amid continuing airstrikes
events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike on a house in Bureij refugee camp killed a 25-year-old Al Jazeera cameraman and two others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Zaporizhzhia
events.body:
An airstrike hit the city of Zaporizhzhia in southern Ukraine, killing five people and wounding ten others. The city has been a recurring target throughout the conflict, as it sits near the front lines and is home to Europe's largest nuclear power plant.

Residents in Zaporizhzhia continue to face the risk of strikes on civilian areas. The wounded are receiving care, though no further details on the specific targets or timing of the strike were included in available reporting.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Five people killed and ten wounded in Zaporizhzhia amid ongoing airstrikes
events.alert_summary: An airstrike struck Zaporizhzhia, killing five people and wounding ten.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes struck southern Lebanon, killing eight people and wounding 17 others, according to reports. The source does not specify the exact towns or villages hit.

Southern Lebanon has seen repeated strikes in recent months. Residents in affected areas continue to face danger, and those wounded will require medical care that may be difficult to access in areas under ongoing threat.

events.prayer: Eight people killed and 17 wounded in airstrikes on southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed 8 people and wounded 17 others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Qennarit
events.body:
An airstrike struck the town of Qennarit, near the southern Lebanese city of Sidon, killing 7 people and wounding 13 others. The strike was carried out by Israeli forces, according to the report.

Sidon is one of Lebanon's larger coastal cities, and the surrounding area has seen recurring strikes during the ongoing conflict. The toll of 7 killed and 13 wounded reflects the continued danger facing civilians in communities across southern Lebanon and areas near major population centers.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven people killed and 13 wounded near Sidon in an airstrike
events.alert_summary: An airstrike killed 7 and wounded 13 in Qennarit, near Sidon, Lebanon.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Aleppo
events.body:
Two Syrian soldiers were killed in an ambush in the Aleppo area, according to reports. No further details about the attackers or the specific location within the region were provided in available reporting.

Aleppo has remained a point of tension since Syrian government forces, backed by allied militaries, retook the city years ago. Sporadic attacks on soldiers and security personnel have continued in and around the area.

The deaths highlight continued instability for those living and working in the region.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Two soldiers killed in an ambush in Aleppo, Syria
events.alert_summary: An ambush killed two Syrian soldiers in Aleppo; no other casualties reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed two soldiers, according to a report from Anadolu Ajansı. The strikes are said to have occurred in violation of an existing ceasefire between the two sides.

Details on the exact location and timing of the strikes were not available from the source summary. No additional casualty figures were reported.

The deaths raise questions about the stability of the ceasefire and what that means for civilians and others still present in the south of the country.

events.prayer: Two Lebanese soldiers and their families after fatal airstrikes in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed 2 Lebanese soldiers, reportedly violating a ceasefire agreement.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Beqaa Valley
events.body:
An Israeli airstrike struck Lebanon's Beqaa Valley, killing four people, according to Lebanese media reports. The Beqaa Valley is an inland agricultural region that has seen repeated strikes during the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

The valley is home to many villages and farming communities, and strikes there affect civilian populations living and working in the area. No further details about the circumstances or specific location of the strike were available in the source material.

events.prayer: Four people killed in an airstrike on Lebanon's Beqaa Valley
events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike killed 4 people in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley, according to Lebanese media.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes killed at least five people in southern Lebanon, according to Vatican News. The strikes occurred even as a ceasefire agreement was reportedly in place, raising concerns about the safety of civilians still in the area.

The source does not specify exact towns, dates, or which parties carried out or violated the ceasefire terms. Residents in southern Lebanon continue to face danger despite the pause in fighting that had been announced.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon caught in airstrikes during a ceasefire period
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed at least 5 people in southern Lebanon despite a ceasefire being in effect.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lower Jubba
events.body:
Somali National Army soldiers captured an al-Shabaab militant during operations in the Lower Jubba region of southern Somalia, according to the state news agency. No additional casualty figures were reported.

Lower Jubba has long been an area of al-Shabaab activity, and Somali forces continue to conduct operations there as part of ongoing efforts to push the group from its strongholds.

Residents in areas where these operations occur often face movement restrictions and uncertainty about safety as fighting or security sweeps take place.

events.prayer: Civilians in Lower Jubba region caught between ongoing military operations and al-Shabaab activity
events.alert_summary: Somali National Army captured 1 al-Shabaab member during operations in Lower Jubba region.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Hiraan
events.body:
Clan fighting has broken out in the Hiraan region of central Somalia, prompting the federal government to call for an immediate ceasefire. Officials issued a public warning against the use of social media to spread content that could inflame the conflict further.

The government's statement reflects concern that online material is making the situation harder to contain. Hiraan has a history of interclan tension, and fighting there can quickly affect civilians in surrounding communities.

No casualty figures were included in the available reporting, but the government's urgent tone suggests the clashes are ongoing and that local residents remain at risk.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians caught in clan clashes in Hiraan as online incitement fuels the violence
events.alert_summary: Somalia's government called for an immediate end to clan clashes in Hiraan and warned against online incitement.

events.title: Displacement in Haiti
events.body:
The United Nations reports that gang violence has forced 1.5 million people from their homes across Haiti. The scale of displacement reflects how much control armed groups have extended across the country, including in and around the capital.

Displaced families are living without reliable access to food, clean water, or medical care. Areas that once provided shelter have become increasingly difficult to reach as gangs block roads and movement.

Humanitarian organizations are working in the country, but access to many affected communities remains limited. For the 1.5 million people uprooted, the immediate concern is finding safety, and for many there is no clear path to returning home.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: 1.5 million Haitians displaced by gang violence with no safe place to go
events.alert_summary: Gang violence has displaced 1.5 million people across Haiti, according to the United Nations.

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Strait of Hormuz
events.body:
Iran announced it had again closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon as a violation of a recent US-Iran agreement. The US military disputed the closure, saying shipping traffic was continuing to move through the waterway. The announcement came as US and Iranian delegations arrived in Switzerland for a new round of direct talks, with Pakistan serving as mediator.

At least 47 people were killed in Lebanon on Saturday following a series of Israeli airstrikes, according to Lebanon's health ministry. The Israeli military said it struck 80 sites linked to Hezbollah and reported four of its own soldiers killed. Lebanon's health ministry says 4,057 people have been killed since fighting between Israel and Hezbollah resumed on March 2.

A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was confirmed Friday, though exchanges of fire had continued since an initial US-Iran deal was announced earlier in the week. The Switzerland talks aim to build on that deal, with a 60-day window to reach a final agreement.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: 4,057 people killed in Lebanon since March as fighting continues amid fragile ceasefire efforts
events.alert_summary: Iran announced closure of the Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran talks opened in Switzerland; 47 people were killed in Lebanon in Israeli airstrikes Saturday.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
At least five Palestinians were killed, including two children, when an airstrike struck an apartment building in Gaza City on Saturday morning. The strike occurred amid ongoing claims about a ceasefire.

Residents of Gaza City continue to face danger even as negotiations over a potential ceasefire are reported. The deaths of children in a residential building underlines the ongoing risk to civilians in the area.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Children and families in Gaza City killed in apartment strike amid ceasefire negotiations
events.alert_summary: An airstrike on an apartment in Gaza City killed at least 5 people, including 2 children, early Saturday.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Southern Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes struck southern Lebanon, killing five people, according to a report from a Jordanian news outlet. No additional details about the specific locations hit or the timing of the strikes were included in the available source material.

Residents in southern Lebanon have faced recurring aerial bombardment in recent months. The deaths add to an ongoing toll on civilians in the region.

events.prayer: Five people killed in airstrikes on southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed five people in southern Lebanon; no further figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes killed at least 20 people in Lebanon even though a ceasefire agreement was in place at the time of the attacks. The strikes represent a breakdown — or disputed interpretation — of the ceasefire terms, though the source does not detail which areas were hit or what prompted the strikes.

Civilians in Lebanon remain at risk as the situation on the ground does not appear to reflect the halt in hostilities that the ceasefire was meant to establish. The gap between the agreement and what is happening on the ground leaves communities uncertain about their safety.

events.prayer: At least 20 people in Lebanon killed by airstrikes despite a ceasefire agreement
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed at least 20 people in Lebanon, occurring despite an active ceasefire agreement.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
An airstrike struck Gaza City and killed four Palestinians, two of whom were girls, according to the Palestine Chronicle. No further details about the location within the city or the circumstances of the strike were provided in the source.

The deaths of two children among the four killed underscores the ongoing danger facing civilians in Gaza. No figures for wounded or displaced were included in the available reporting.

events.title: Armed Conflict in northern Syria
events.body:
A US-led coalition carried out an airstrike in northern Syria, killing a senior militant. The strike appears to be part of ongoing coalition operations targeting armed groups in the region.

Northern Syria has seen repeated coalition airstrikes over the years as part of efforts to counter militant activity. Civilians in the area continue to live amid the risk of military operations from multiple armed actors.
