events.prayer: Civilians in northern Syria caught in ongoing airstrikes and militant activity
events.alert_summary: A US coalition airstrike in northern Syria killed one senior militant; no further casualties reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Niamey
events.body:
Armed gunmen attacked the airport in Niamey, the capital of Niger, with explosions reported during the assault. Few additional details are available from the source at this time.

Niamey's airport serves both civilian and military purposes, and any attack there affects travelers, airport workers, and surrounding neighborhoods. Niger has faced ongoing instability since a military coup in 2023, and armed groups have been active across the broader Sahel region.

Residents near the airport would have been exposed to the sounds and dangers of the exchange. The situation was still developing at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: "Sahel OR Burkina Faso OR Mali OR Niger attack OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians and travelers near Niamey airport caught in an armed attack
events.alert_summary: Gunmen attacked the airport in Niamey, Niger's capital, with explosions reported during the assault.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Chibok
events.body:
A Boko Haram attack in Chibok, the town in Borno State long associated with the 2014 schoolgirl abductions, has claimed the life of a baby who was only about 12 hours old at the time.

Details on the full scale of the attack, including additional casualties or the number of people displaced, were not specified in available reporting. Chibok remains an area where armed group activity continues to threaten civilian life, including the most vulnerable residents.

The death of a newborn in such circumstances reflects the ongoing danger faced by families in communities across northeastern Nigeria where Boko Haram and affiliated groups remain active.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Newborn and families in Chibok caught in an armed attack
events.alert_summary: A Boko Haram attack in Chibok killed an infant reported to be 12 hours old.

events.title: Civil Unrest in La Paz
events.body:
Bolivia's president declared a state of emergency on Saturday, giving the military authority to clear road blockades that have shut off food, fuel, and medical supplies to La Paz for roughly 50 days. At least 17 people have died, most due to disrupted access to medical care, and 37 have been injured. Authorities have made 365 arrests during the unrest.

The protests began after the president eliminated longstanding fuel subsidies and introduced austerity measures. Highland Indigenous groups and rural workers have led the demonstrations, accusing the government of neglecting their communities. Some protesters are demanding the president resign and have refused to negotiate.

The 90-day decree bans road blockades and directs armed forces to support police in reopening highways. The president signed an agreement with one labour union Friday, but other protest groups rejected talks. Hospitals ran short of oxygen and store shelves emptied during the weeks of disruption.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Bolivians in La Paz cut off from food, fuel, and medical care amid ongoing blockades
events.alert_summary: Bolivia's president declared a 90-day state of emergency after 50 days of blockades left La Paz isolated, killing at least 17 and injuring 37.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Lebanese official media reported that Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon on Saturday, killing five people. The strikes came one day after a ceasefire was announced between Israel and Hezbollah, the Iran-backed armed group.

Residents were reported fleeing the area as the strikes continued. It is not yet clear whether the ceasefire has fully broken down or whether these represent isolated violations.

Civilians in the south face an uncertain situation as fighting continues despite the announced halt. The deaths and displacement add to ongoing hardship for communities in the region.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Five people killed in southern Lebanon as residents flee Israeli strikes during ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed five people on Saturday, one day after a ceasefire was announced.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bolivia
events.body:
Bolivia's president declared a state of emergency following weeks of roadblocks by miners, farmers, and indigenous groups demanding his resignation. The blockades have caused shortages of food, fuel, and other basic goods and have cut off access to medical care for many Bolivians. Several people have died and hundreds have been arrested since the unrest began in late April.

The protests were initially triggered by a proposed land reform, which the president has since withdrawn. Demonstrators are also pushing for fuel subsidies to be restored and opposing proposed changes to the constitution. The state of emergency would give the president broader authority to clear blocked roads; Congress must approve or reject it within 72 hours.

A deal was reached with the country's main labor union, but some indigenous groups say they will continue protesting. Police and military personnel were seen in public squares following the announcement.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Bolivian civilians facing shortages of food, fuel, and medical access amid ongoing roadblocks
events.alert_summary: Bolivia's president declared a state of emergency after weeks of protests left hundreds arrested and basic goods scarce.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon on Saturday, killing at least seven people, including two children. The strikes came just hours after reports surfaced of a ceasefire agreement, raising questions about whether fighting would stop.

The continued strikes are putting pressure on a broader diplomatic effort. An interim agreement between the United States and Iran, aimed at ending the wider war in the Middle East, is now reported to be at risk because of the ongoing fighting.

Civilians in the area remain in danger as the situation continues, with no confirmed halt to the strikes at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Seven people, including two children in southern Lebanon, killed hours after ceasefire reports
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after ceasefire reports emerged.

events.title: Bayahibe
events.body:
A fire broke out at the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel in Bayahibe, on the southeastern coast of the Dominican Republic. One woman, an Italian national, died in the blaze. Three people were taken to medical facilities and six others received treatment at the scene.

About 1,690 tourists were evacuated to other hotels and nearby establishments. Authorities say the fire spread quickly because the resort's roof was made of thatch, and wind conditions made it worse. The flames consumed most of the hotel complex, a popular destination for international visitors.

Fire officials brought the blaze under control, and an investigation is underway to find the cause. The hotel franchise had not issued a public statement at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Tourists and staff caught in the Bayahibe resort fire, and the family of the woman who died
events.alert_summary: A fire at a beach resort in Bayahibe killed one woman and forced about 1,690 tourists to evacuate.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh district
events.body:
Israeli air strikes and drone attacks hit southern Lebanon on Saturday, hours after a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was announced. Lebanon's civil defence agency reported 16 people killed and 12 wounded in the Nabatieh district. In the neighboring Tyre district, a strike on the village of Barish killed four members of one family — a father, mother, and their two children. A Lebanese soldier was also killed in the village of Kfar Reman.

The strikes are threatening to derail planned diplomatic talks between the United States and Iran. A ceasefire in Lebanon is described as essential to those negotiations, with Iranian officials saying conditions on the ground could "make or break" the process. An agreed framework explicitly links an end to fighting in Lebanon with a broader regional ceasefire arrangement.

Lebanon's army said the continued strikes are aimed at blocking efforts to restore stability. Talks between Lebanon and Israel are scheduled for late June in Washington, though Hezbollah has not been included in those discussions.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in southern Lebanon killed and wounded amid strikes following a ceasefire announcement
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 21 people and wounded 12 after a ceasefire took effect.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tyre
events.body:
A 77-year-old Lebanese marine ecologist died on Friday after being wounded when an Israeli strike hit her home near Tyre. She had lived and worked on Lebanon's southern coast for decades, protecting the nesting sites of endangered loggerhead and green sea turtles at al-Mansouri beach.

On the same day she died, Israeli air attacks on southern Lebanon killed at least 50 people and wounded dozens more. Environmental volunteers and colleagues mourned her passing, calling it a loss for all of Lebanon.

She had helped establish an eco-tourism project at al-Mansouri beach in 2000 and spent years documenting marine life and advocating against coastal pollution. Both loggerhead and green sea turtles face extinction in the eastern Mediterranean from development, plastic pollution, and fishing nets — work she had championed since 1999.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon killed and wounded amid ongoing airstrikes, including a 77-year-old conservationist
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 50 people on Friday, including a 77-year-old ecologist wounded in an earlier strike near Tyre.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
A family was killed early Saturday morning when strikes hit a building on al-Thalatini Street in Gaza City around 2:00 am. A father and his two daughters — ages four and fourteen — died in the attack. The mother was wounded and died later that day. A relative said there was no warning before the strike hit their home.

Earlier Friday evening, five people were injured when strikes hit a tent sheltering displaced families in the al-Mawasi area of Khan Younis. Naval gunfire was also reported Saturday off the coast of southern Gaza.

Health authorities report that since an October ceasefire, at least 1,007 people in Gaza have been killed and 3,165 wounded. Since the war began in October 2023, at least 73,018 people have been killed. About 2.2 million people in Gaza currently face acute humanitarian needs, with food, medical aid, and shelter materials restricted from entry.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: A family in Gaza City killed overnight, including two young daughters, and thousands facing ongoing strikes
events.alert_summary: Overnight strikes on a residential building in Gaza City killed five people, including a father and his two daughters, aged four and fourteen.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Bannu district
events.body:
Two roadside bombs struck Bannu district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province on Saturday, killing at least seven people. The first bomb hit a passenger pickup truck. When the wounded were being driven to a hospital, a second bomb exploded along the route, killing and wounding more people. No group has claimed responsibility, though armed groups including the Pakistan Taliban are active in the area.

The attacks come amid rising tension along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border. Pakistani airstrikes earlier this month killed fighters near the border, according to Pakistani officials, while Afghan authorities said 12 civilians died in those strikes. The border has been largely closed since violence increased in October, cutting off trade and travel between the two countries.

Residents near the border face ongoing risk from roadside bombs, closed crossings, and the broader breakdown in relations between the two governments.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Wounded survivors and families of seven killed in Bannu district bomb attacks
events.alert_summary: Two roadside bombs in Bannu district killed at least seven people and wounded three, with the second bomb targeting rescuers.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Pakistan
events.body:
Afghan forces launched strikes against targets inside Pakistan, according to reporting by Al Jazeera. The incident has heightened tensions along the Afghanistan-Pakistan border, a region that has seen repeated cross-border incidents in recent years.

No casualty figures, specific strike locations within Pakistan, or dates were available from the source summary. Further details on the scale of the strikes and any civilian impact had not been confirmed at the time of this report.

Residents and communities near border areas between the two countries may face continued risk as the situation develops.

events.body:
A U.S.-led coalition carried out an airstrike in northern Syria, killing a senior militant, according to reports. No further details about the location, the group involved, or the circumstances of the strike were provided in the available summary.

No information was given about civilian casualties or displacement resulting from the strike. Residents in areas where such operations occur regularly face uncertainty about safety, particularly in communities near militant activity.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes hit southern Lebanon, killing 18 people, according to a report from an Iranian news outlet. The strikes occurred while a ceasefire agreement was reported to be in place.

The source does not specify exact locations within southern Lebanon, the dates of the strikes, or whether the casualties were civilians or combatants.

Residents in southern Lebanon continue to face the threat of airstrikes even during periods when a ceasefire is supposed to be holding. The situation raises concerns for communities that had hoped the ceasefire would bring a halt to violence in the area.

events.prayer: 18 people killed in southern Lebanon amid a ceasefire agreement
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed 18 people despite a ceasefire being in effect.

events.title: Displacement in Beirut
events.body:
Crowds of people packed the main highway out of Beirut as residents tried to escape ongoing airstrikes. The scene showed vehicles backed up along the route as families and individuals moved away from areas under attack.

The movement reflects a pattern seen across Lebanon during periods of intensified strikes, where civilians in affected neighborhoods leave quickly with what they can carry. Roads out of the capital became a bottleneck as people sought safer ground.

For those remaining in the city or caught in the surrounding areas, the combination of active strikes and clogged escape routes leaves limited options.

events.prayer: Lebanese civilians jamming highways to flee ongoing airstrikes near Beirut
events.alert_summary: Residents fled Beirut as airstrikes prompted a highway traffic jam; no casualty figure reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Southern Lebanon
events.body:
An airstrike struck southern Lebanon, killing two people. No additional details about the exact location, the identities of those killed, or the circumstances of the strike were provided in the source.

Residents in southern Lebanon have faced recurring strikes in the area. The deaths add to the toll on civilian life in a region that has seen ongoing cross-border violence.

events.prayer: Two people killed in an airstrike in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: An airstrike killed two people in southern Lebanon; no further casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kyauktaw
events.body:
An airstrike carried out by Myanmar's military killed at least seven people in the town of Kyauktaw, according to reporting from Myanmar Now. Kyauktaw is located in Rakhine State, an area that has seen ongoing fighting between military forces and armed groups in recent years.

Airstrikes on populated areas put civilians at direct risk and can force residents to flee their homes. Communities in Rakhine State have faced repeated displacement and disruption to daily life as conflict in the region continues.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Borno
events.body:
Nigerian army troops fought off an attack by ISWAP — the Islamic State West Africa Province — in Borno State in northeastern Nigeria. Following the attack, military forces launched a combined air and ground counter-offensive in the area.

Borno State has long been a center of armed group activity in Nigeria's northeast, and clashes between the military and groups like ISWAP remain ongoing. The source does not report casualty figures from this particular engagement.

Residents in the region continue to live under the threat of armed attacks, and military operations in response can disrupt daily movement and access in affected communities.

events.prayer: Civilians in Borno State amid ongoing armed group attacks and military counter-operations
events.alert_summary: Nigerian forces repelled an ISWAP attack in Borno and launched a follow-up air-ground counter-offensive.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was announced by a US official following a night of airstrikes across southern Lebanon that killed 47 people, including women and children, and wounded 97. In the Nabatieh district, strikes hit the towns of Harouf, Haboush, and al-Duweir. Hezbollah said it had ambushed an Israeli force, destroying three tanks and killing four soldiers, including a battalion commander.

Despite the ceasefire announcement, rescue officials in Nabatieh reported at least 12 airstrikes after it was said to have taken effect. The Israeli military said its forces would "continue to remove immediate threats." Hezbollah had not confirmed the ceasefire as of the report.

Displaced Lebanese residents expressed doubt the agreement would hold, with one resident noting that previous agreements had not been honored. The US State Department said direct talks between Lebanon and Israel would resume in Washington next week.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Displaced Lebanese families skeptical of ceasefire as airstrikes continue in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes killed 47 people and wounded 97 in southern Lebanon before a ceasefire was announced.

events.title: Eastern Congo
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak is spreading in eastern Congo, with UN agencies warning the situation is worsening as the disease crosses into neighboring areas. The spread is being made worse by ongoing armed violence in the region, which limits the ability of health workers to respond.

UN agencies described the risk to the wider region as serious and growing. Conflict in eastern Congo has long disrupted healthcare and displaced large numbers of people, conditions that allow infectious disease to move quickly through communities.

Civilians in affected areas face both the ongoing threat of armed violence and now a disease outbreak that is becoming harder to contain.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians in eastern Congo facing Ebola spread amid ongoing armed violence
events.alert_summary: An Ebola outbreak is accelerating in eastern Congo and spreading beyond its borders, UN agencies warned.

events.title: Bunia, Ituri
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo on May 15 has now killed 232 people and infected 896 others across 31 health zones. Health officials believe the rare Bundibugyo strain had been circulating for months before the government announced it, leaving medical workers unknowingly exposed. Seventeen of the 75 healthcare workers who have contracted the virus have died. Basic protective equipment such as gloves and masks remains in short supply at many facilities.

Particular concern surrounds Kigonze displacement camp in Bunia, Ituri province, where at least 30 people died since early May. The camp houses more than 15,000 people, and relatives had refused Ebola testing until recently, making it difficult to confirm causes of death. Aid workers warn that overcrowding, poor sanitation, and resistance to testing could allow the virus to spread undetected across hundreds of camps sheltering more than five million displaced people in eastern DRC.

Funding cuts have worsened conditions. UN data shows money for toilets and handwashing stations in DRC fell by more than half between 2024 and 2025, and this year's $80 million sanitation appeal is only 21 percent funded. Health officials say the outbreak has not yet reached its peak.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Five million displaced people in eastern Congo living amid an Ebola outbreak with little sanitation or protective supplies
events.alert_summary: Ebola has killed 232 people and infected 896 across 31 health zones in DRC, with 17 of 75 infected healthcare workers dead.

events.title: Bedford
events.body:
Two London-bound passenger trains collided on the same track near Bedford on Friday evening, killing one driver and injuring more than 80 people. Of those taken to hospital, 11 sustained very serious injuries, 32 suffered serious wounds, and 56 others had minor injuries. As of Saturday morning, 28 people remained hospitalized, with nine in critical condition.

More than 20 ambulances, specialist rescue teams, and six air ambulances responded to the scene. British Transport Police and the Rail Accident Investigation Branch have opened an investigation to determine what caused the crash. Officials said it is too early to identify a cause, and have not confirmed whether signalling problems played a role.

Both trains were operated by East Midlands Railway. The investigation is ongoing, and residents in the area can expect continued disruption to rail services while crews work at the scene.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families of the driver killed and 99 people injured in the Bedford train collision
events.alert_summary: Two passenger trains collided near Bedford, killing one driver and sending more than 80 people to hospital.

events.title: Displacement in Kigonze
events.body:
At least 30 people have died since May at the Kigonze displacement camp in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Those who died showed symptoms consistent with Ebola, raising concern that the disease may be spreading through the camp. Testing for Ebola has only recently been put in place.

Displacement camps already face difficult conditions, and the combination of crowded living and a potential Ebola outbreak puts residents at serious risk. The delay in testing means the true scope of the situation is still not fully known.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Displaced people in Kigonze camp facing at least 30 deaths and a possible Ebola outbreak
events.alert_summary: At least 30 people have died since May in the Kigonze displacement camp in Congo amid symptoms suggesting Ebola.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ali al-Taher
events.body:
Four Israeli soldiers were killed and others wounded after Hezbollah ambushed Israeli forces near Ali al-Taher, according to reporting from the Palestine Chronicle. The number of wounded soldiers was not specified in the available source material.

Ali al-Taher is a village in southern Lebanon, an area that has seen repeated clashes between Israeli forces and Hezbollah amid ongoing hostilities along the border.

No figures on civilian casualties or displacement were included in the source material. The incident adds to a pattern of ground-level fighting in southern Lebanon that continues to affect communities on both sides of the border.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Four soldiers killed and others wounded in an ambush near Ali al-Taher
events.alert_summary: An ambush near Ali al-Taher killed 4 Israeli soldiers and wounded others; no civilian casualty figure reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
At least 20 people were killed by Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon less than 24 hours after a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was announced. Local officials reported 16 deaths in the Nabatieh district and 7 in the neighboring Saida area, with others wounded. A family of four — two parents and their two children — was killed in the town of Barich. Warplanes, drones, and artillery targeted several areas across the south.

The Israeli military said it struck what it described as dozens of Hezbollah targets after the group fired more than 50 projectiles at Israeli forces. A Hezbollah official told the BBC that the group does not recognize the ceasefire announced by US officials.

Hospitals in the south are overwhelmed. At one facility in Nabatieh, ambulances have begun bypassing the emergency room and going directly to the morgue. Many residents had returned to their villages after earlier ceasefires, believing the worst of the fighting was behind them.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon killed and wounded as airstrikes continue despite a ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 20 people in southern Lebanon within 24 hours of a ceasefire announcement, with 16 deaths in Nabatieh and 7 in Saida.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Butembo
events.body:
Armed men entered a hospital in Butembo, in eastern Congo, and removed a young girl receiving Ebola treatment along with her mother. Health officials said the men appeared angry, and the incident likely stemmed from widespread distrust of Ebola treatment centers in the region. The child and her mother were found two days later at another Ebola facility about 18 kilometers away, and the girl's condition was described as stable.

The broader outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola for which no vaccine currently exists, has recorded more than 890 confirmed cases and over 230 deaths. Seventy-five health workers have contracted the virus, and 17 of them have died. Facilities have been attacked multiple times — crowds have attempted to reclaim bodies of the deceased, and isolation tents have been burned — as some residents believe the disease is fabricated by outside organizations.

Ongoing conflict in eastern Congo, where an armed rebel group controls parts of North and South Kivu, is hampering the response further.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: A six-year-old Ebola patient and her mother taken from a hospital in eastern Congo amid fear and misinformation
events.alert_summary: Armed men removed a six-year-old Ebola patient and her mother from a hospital in Butembo; both were found 18km away two days later.

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Bissau
events.body:
Guinea-Bissau's political crisis has stretched nearly seven months since soldiers seized power on November 26, 2025, interrupting a presidential election. Armed men stormed the National Electoral Commission building, seized ballots and vote tallies, and destroyed servers storing the results. A National Transitional Council took power and has scheduled new elections for December 6, 2026.

A military court recently ordered the country's leading opposition figure — head of the PAIGC party — to remain under house arrest after questioning over allegations he plotted against the military government. His lawyer says no formal legal justification has been provided for the detention, and prosecutors have not requested pre-trial detention. The opposition contends the measure is illegal under the country's criminal code and is aimed at clearing the field before the next election.

Portugal and other Portuguese-speaking nations are calling for the opposition leader's release, but the military leadership has responded by threatening to leave the Community of Portuguese Language Countries entirely.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Opposition leader and civilians in Guinea-Bissau caught in a prolonged political crisis after a military coup
events.alert_summary: A military court in Guinea-Bissau ordered an opposition leader to remain under house arrest over alleged coup plotting, deepening a post-coup political crisis.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza
events.body:
An airstrike struck a residential apartment in Gaza, killing five civilians. The attack was carried out by Israeli forces, according to the source.

No further details about the specific neighborhood, the identities of those killed, or whether others were wounded are available from this report. Residential buildings in Gaza have frequently been struck during the ongoing conflict, raising ongoing concerns about civilian safety in densely populated areas.

events.prayer: Five civilians in Gaza killed in an airstrike on a residential apartment
events.alert_summary: An airstrike on a residential apartment in Gaza killed five civilians, according to reports.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
events.body:
Afghanistan launched air strikes on Thursday night against sites in Pakistan's Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces, both of which border Afghanistan. Kabul said the targets were bases used by ISIS-K and what it described as hostile intelligence groups planning attacks against Afghanistan. Pakistan's government rejected the claims as false, saying armed groups including ISIS-K are based inside Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

The strikes come after Pakistan carried out its own cross-border strikes last week, which the Afghan Taliban said killed at least 13 people, including 11 children. A ceasefire reached in March collapsed after both sides accused the other of violations, and mediation efforts led by China have not produced a lasting agreement. The United Nations reported in May that cross-border fighting killed at least 372 Afghan civilians and injured 397 in just the first three months of 2026, with no peace deal in sight.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Afghanistan and Pakistan caught in ongoing cross-border strikes and collapsed ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Afghanistan launched air strikes on sites in two Pakistani border provinces on Thursday, amid fighting that has killed at least 372 Afghan civilians in early 2026.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Mexico
events.body:
A journalist in Mexico has been missing for more than two weeks after being kidnapped, prompting an ongoing search. The disappearance has drawn attention to the ongoing danger facing reporters in the country, where attacks on journalists are widespread.

Those who commit violence against journalists in Mexico rarely face legal consequences, a pattern that has long contributed to the country being one of the more dangerous places in the world for press workers.

No further details about the journalist's location or the circumstances of the kidnapping were included in available reports. The search was still ongoing at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Journalist missing in Mexico for more than two weeks, family and colleagues awaiting news
events.alert_summary: A journalist in Mexico has been missing for more than two weeks after being kidnapped; no casualties reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Pavlohrad
events.body:
Russia launched 90 drones at Ukraine between late Thursday and early Friday, killing three people across multiple regions. An eight-year-old girl was killed and one person wounded in Pavlohrad in the central Dnipropetrovsk region. Separate strikes killed one person in the southern Odesa region and one in the eastern city of Kramatorsk. Russian drone attacks also struck civilian ships in the Black Sea late Thursday, killing one person and wounding five others.

The strikes came after Ukraine launched its largest-ever drone attack on Moscow the previous day. Russian officials said an eight-year-old girl was killed at her home and hundreds of drones set fire to a large oil refinery in southeast Moscow for the second time in three days. The Kremlin said its air defenses were holding and that strikes on Ukraine would continue.

Ukraine also struck railway bridges in Russian-held Crimea, which its military said were used to move Russian supplies. Peace talks led by the United States remain stalled, while both sides have been escalating attacks in recent weeks.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine and Russia caught in escalating drone and missile strikes, including children killed in their homes
events.alert_summary: Russian drone strikes killed three in Ukraine, including an eight-year-old girl in Pavlohrad, as Ukraine's drone attack on Moscow the day before killed a child and set fire to an oil refinery.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Moscow
events.body:
One of the largest drone attacks on the Moscow region since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine struck an oil refinery in the south-east of the city, setting it ablaze and sending thick black smoke across the skyline. Residential buildings and shopping centres were also hit. The governor of the Moscow region confirmed that an eight-year-old girl died in a fire caused by one of the strikes.

Residents near the refinery largely went about their day — fishing, shopping, watching children play — while smoke filled the sky. Russian state television gave the attack little coverage, and President Putin made no public statement about it. When Russian newspapers did report the story the following day, coverage stressed that Russian strikes on Ukraine were far more damaging than anything Ukraine was doing to Russia.

The attack is among the clearest signs yet that the conflict has physically reached the Russian capital, a reality that some Moscow residents are finding difficult to process.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Moscow near a burning oil refinery, and an eight-year-old girl killed in the drone strike
events.alert_summary: A Ukrainian drone strike hit an oil refinery, residential buildings, and shopping centres in Moscow, killing one child.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli forces carried out at least 12 air raids and continued artillery shelling in southern Lebanon after a ceasefire was supposed to take effect at 4pm local time on Friday. The Lebanese health ministry reported 47 people killed and 97 wounded in attacks beginning at midnight. The ceasefire had been brokered with help from the United States, Qatar, and Iran.

An Israeli military spokesperson said forces would keep "operational freedom" to respond to what they consider threats in southern Lebanon, effectively leaving room to continue strikes. Residents in the area, including those near Tyre, said the ceasefire did not feel real. A Hezbollah official said the agreement would hold only if Israel abided by it.

The ceasefire was tied to broader efforts to turn a US-Iran interim agreement into a lasting regional arrangement, including keeping the Strait of Hormuz open to shipping. Continued fighting has raised concerns that the larger diplomatic process could collapse.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: 47 people killed and 97 wounded in Lebanon as airstrikes continue despite a ceasefire agreement
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes and artillery killed at least 47 people and wounded 97 others in Lebanon after a ceasefire took effect.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Explosions were reported in southern Lebanon within minutes of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah taking effect, according to footage and reports from the region.

The timing of the strikes — occurring almost immediately after the ceasefire began — raises uncertainty about the durability of the agreement and what it means for people living in the affected areas.

Residents in southern Lebanon remain in a fragile situation as it is unclear whether the ceasefire will hold.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon caught in airstrikes minutes after a ceasefire took effect
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes hit southern Lebanon minutes after a new ceasefire with Hezbollah came into force.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon
events.body:
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was announced by US officials, with the sitting US president stating he had asked Israel to agree to the halt in fighting. The ceasefire was declared in effect on a Friday afternoon local time.

Despite the announcement, fighting did not stop. Attacks and retaliatory strikes from both sides continued after the ceasefire was supposed to take hold, according to reporters on the ground in Jerusalem and Beirut.

Residents in Lebanon remain in a situation where an agreement exists on paper but the actual conditions on the ground have not changed. No casualty figures were provided in the source material, but the continued exchange of fire means civilians in affected areas face ongoing danger even as a formal halt to hostilities has been declared.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon caught between ongoing attacks despite a declared ceasefire

events.title: Armed Conflict in Pakistan
events.body:
Afghan forces launched airstrikes inside Pakistan, according to reporting by NDTV. The strikes come amid growing tensions between the two neighboring countries, though the source does not specify the exact locations hit within Pakistan or provide casualty figures.

Cross-border strikes of this kind put civilians on both sides of the border at risk. The Afghanistan-Pakistan border region is home to many communities, and escalating military activity between the two governments raises concerns about civilian safety in the area.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kholodnogirskyi district, Kharkiv
events.body:
An airstrike hit a residential district of Kharkiv, Ukraine's second-largest city, and rescue workers responding to the scene found human remains. The Kholodnogirskyi district is an urban area within the city.

No further details on casualties, the number of people affected, or the type of munition used are available from this report. Kharkiv has been subject to repeated strikes due to its location close to the Russian border.

events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike hit Kharkiv's Kholodnogirskyi district, where rescuers recovered human remains.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes struck southern Lebanon, killing 18 people and wounding 33 others, according to reports. The strikes were attributed to Israeli forces.

The casualties add to ongoing pressure on civilian communities in the region. Residents in southern Lebanon have faced repeated rounds of strikes in recent months, and the latest deaths will likely deepen concern among families and aid workers operating in the area.

events.prayer: 18 people killed and 33 injured in southern Lebanon airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed 18 people and injured 33 others in southern Lebanon.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kharkiv
events.body:
Firefighters worked to bring a warehouse fire under control in Kharkiv after a Russian airstrike set the buildings ablaze. One person was reported missing in connection with the strike.

Kharkiv, located in northeastern Ukraine close to the Russian border, has faced repeated strikes throughout the war. Attacks on warehouses and infrastructure can affect the storage of goods, supplies, and humanitarian materials that residents and aid organizations depend on.

The full extent of damage to the warehouses and any additional casualties had not been confirmed at the time of the report.

events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike struck warehouses in Kharkiv, sparking a fire with one person reported missing.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ye Township
events.body:
A military airstrike hit a housing complex in Ye Township, destroying five buildings. Among the structures lost was a primary school, along with residential buildings that had been part of a Nippon Foundation housing project.

The loss of a school means children in the area have lost access to their classroom, adding to the disruption many families in Myanmar already face due to ongoing conflict. Communities living near such facilities are increasingly at risk as airstrikes continue to hit civilian areas across the country.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Niamey
events.body:
Witnesses reported explosions and sustained gunfire near the airport in Niamey, Niger's capital. The source does not specify who was involved or what triggered the incident.

No casualty figures or official statements were included in the available reporting. The situation at the airport and surrounding areas remained unclear at the time the report was filed.

Residents near the airport would have been directly affected by the unrest, and travel through the capital could be disrupted.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Diori Hamani airport
events.body:
An attack on Niger's Diori Hamani airport left 35 people dead, according to BBC reporting cited by bdnews24.com. The airport is located in Niamey, Niger's capital city.

No further details about the attackers, the method of attack, or the number of wounded are available from this source summary. It is not clear whether the airport remains operational or whether additional casualties are expected.

Niger has faced ongoing instability since a military coup in 2023, and armed groups have been active across the wider Sahel region. An attack of this scale on a capital city airport would mark a significant escalation for civilians and those traveling through Niamey.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oyo
events.body:
A group of students in Oyo State, Nigeria, were kidnapped and remain in the hands of their captors, according to reporting from Punch Newspapers. Families of those taken are described as being in anguish as the situation continues without a known end.

No specific number of students, date of abduction, or location within Oyo State was available in the source summary. The identities of those responsible have not been confirmed in the available material.

Kidnapping for ransom has affected communities across Nigeria in recent years, and families in these situations often face prolonged uncertainty with little information about the condition or whereabouts of their loved ones.

events.prayer: Students from Oyo held in captivity while families wait for their return

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kebbi
events.body:
Authorities in Kebbi State, Nigeria arrested 12 people connected to several armed groups, including Lakurawa, Mamudawa, and Boko Haram. A suspect in a rape case was also among those detained in the operation.

The arrests point to the presence of multiple armed factions operating in Kebbi, a state in northwestern Nigeria that has faced growing insecurity in recent years. Lakurawa is a relatively recently identified group that has drawn attention for attacks in the region.

No casualties were reported in connection with the arrests themselves. Residents in areas where these groups are active continue to face risks from armed violence and criminal activity.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities in Kebbi State affected by armed group activity and violence
events.alert_summary: Police arrested 12 suspects linked to Lakurawa, Mamudawa, and Boko Haram groups in Kebbi State.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Kaduna
events.body:
A soldier is fighting for his life in a hospital after a crowd in Kaduna attacked him, reportedly believing he was a bandit. The incident highlights the level of fear and tension in communities where armed banditry has become widespread in northwestern Nigeria.

The soldier survived the attack but remains in serious condition. No additional casualties or arrests are reported in the available source material.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A soldier in Kaduna hospitalized after being attacked by a crowd who mistook him for a bandit
events.alert_summary: A mob in Kaduna attacked a soldier after mistaking him for a bandit; he remains hospitalized in critical condition.

events.title: Displacement in Haiti
events.body:
The United Nations reports that gang violence has forced approximately 1.5 million people from their homes across Haiti. This figure marks a continuing deterioration of conditions for ordinary Haitians caught in the middle of widespread armed gang activity.

Displaced families face shortages of food, shelter, and basic services. Many have fled their neighborhoods with little or nothing, and the scale of displacement makes return difficult for most.

The situation continues to affect communities across the country, leaving large numbers of civilians without stable housing or reliable access to aid.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: 1.5 million Haitians displaced by gang violence with no safe place to return
events.alert_summary: Gang violence has displaced 1.5 million people across Haiti, according to the United Nations.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Middle East
events.body:
Official figures show at least 3,468 Iranians killed since US and Israeli strikes began in late February, including nearly 1,500 civilians. A separate count by a US-based human rights group puts the number higher, at 3,636, and says those figures should be treated as minimum counts. Investigators found that a strike on the first day of the conflict hit a school in the town of Minab, killing 168 people, including 110 children. Another strike hit a sports hall during a girls' volleyball match in Lamerd, killing 20 people.

In Lebanon, health authorities report 3,912 people killed in Israeli attacks since fighting resumed on March 2, among them 247 children. Analysts warn that the true death toll across the region may be significantly higher than official numbers suggest, due to internet shutdowns, restricted media access, and political pressure on families not to speak publicly about deaths.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Thousands of civilians, including hundreds of children, killed across Iran and Lebanon since February
events.alert_summary: More than 7,300 people have been killed in Iran and Lebanon since strikes began on February 28, including hundreds of children.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh district
events.body:
Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon's Nabatieh district overnight killed at least 18 people and wounded 33 others, according to Lebanon's health ministry. The Israeli military said it struck 80 targets linked to Hezbollah and that four of its soldiers were killed in a Hezbollah ambush involving guided missiles, rockets, and artillery.

The strikes came one day after the United States and Iran signed an agreement calling for an end to hostilities across the region, including Lebanon. Both sides have continued fighting since the deal was announced, raising uncertainty about whether any ceasefire can hold. Israel has stated it considers its conflict with Hezbollah separate from the broader US-Iran agreement and has no plans to withdraw forces from Lebanese territory.

Since the current conflict began, Lebanon's health ministry reports at least 3,912 people have been killed and nearly 12,000 wounded. Around one million people remain displaced, and dozens of communities in the south have been completely destroyed.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon's Nabatieh district killed and wounded in overnight airstrikes, and the one million people still displaced
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes on the Nabatieh district killed at least 18 and wounded 33; four Israeli soldiers were also killed in a Hezbollah ambush.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan
events.body:
As Sudan's conflict moves into a fourth year, civilians are caught not only in frontline fighting but also in a growing pattern of fear, forced disappearance, and detention. A UN-appointed fact-finding mission presented these findings to the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva.

The update highlights that the dangers facing ordinary people go beyond direct violence. Disappearances and arbitrary detention have become additional threats pressing down on civilian life across the country.

No specific casualty figures were included in the report summary, but the picture drawn by rights experts points to a population under pressure from multiple directions at once.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians in Sudan trapped by frontline violence, disappearances, and detention as conflict continues into a fourth year
events.alert_summary: Rights experts reported Sudan's civilians face frontline violence, disappearances, and detention as conflict enters its fourth year.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Harouf
events.body:
Residents of southern Lebanon woke just after midnight Thursday to airstrikes on their villages and cities, hours before scheduled talks between the United States and Iran were set to begin in Switzerland. At least 18 people were killed and dozens wounded. The largest number of casualties came from a residential building struck in Harouf village.

Israel has carried out near-daily attacks on Lebanon since early March, and more than 3,000 people have been killed since fighting intensified. Over one million people have been displaced from their homes. Israel currently occupies roughly one-fifth of Lebanese territory.

The overnight strikes prompted Iran to say its negotiators were not ready to travel for talks until the existing agreement — which includes Lebanon in a ceasefire — showed signs of being put into effect. Switzerland's Foreign Ministry confirmed the talks have been postponed, with no new date set, even as the 60-day window for reaching a formal agreement has already begun.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of southern Lebanon amid ongoing airstrikes, with over one million already displaced
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 18 people and wounded dozens, with bodies recovered from a residential building in Harouf village.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Edo
events.body:
Officers in Edo state, Nigeria, shot and killed a man believed to be involved in kidnapping activities, while a second suspect was taken into custody. The incident appears to be part of ongoing police operations targeting criminal networks in the area.

Kidnapping has remained a concern across several Nigerian states, with residents and travelers at risk. The arrest of the second suspect may lead to further investigation into the network involved.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families in Edo state threatened by kidnapping and ongoing criminal violence
events.alert_summary: Police shot and killed one suspected kidnapper and arrested an accomplice in Edo state.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Abuja
events.body:
Police in Nigeria's capital, Abuja, arrested a pregnant woman suspected of cooking meals for members of a kidnapping gang, according to a report from a Nigerian news outlet. Authorities described the group as a known criminal network operating in the Federal Capital Territory.

No further details about the gang, the number of suspects, or any rescued victims were available from the source. It is unclear whether other arrests were made in connection with the operation.

Kidnapping for ransom remains a persistent problem in and around Abuja, and arrests of suspected support personnel signal continued police efforts to disrupt criminal networks in the region.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Pregnant woman arrested in Abuja for alleged ties to a kidnapping gang
events.alert_summary: Police in Abuja arrested a pregnant woman accused of providing support to a kidnapping gang.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sumy region
events.body:
Warehouses storing sulfur-based substances caught fire near the Sumy region of Ukraine after a Russian airstrike. The source does not specify an exact town or date, but the incident raised immediate concern because of the nature of the materials involved.

Sulfur-based substances can release toxic fumes when they burn, creating a health hazard for anyone nearby. Residents in the surrounding area may face risks from smoke or air contamination depending on wind conditions and how long the fire burned.

The Sumy region sits in northeastern Ukraine near the Russian border and has experienced repeated strikes throughout the conflict. No casualty figures were included in the available reporting.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents near Sumy region affected by airstrikes and chemical warehouse fires
events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike ignited warehouses holding sulfur-based substances near the Sumy region of Ukraine.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Rakhine state
events.body:
A military airstrike struck Rakhine state in Myanmar, killing seven civilians, according to reports. The attack is the latest in a pattern of aerial strikes that have targeted areas across the country since the military takeover in 2021.

Rakhine state has seen ongoing conflict between military forces and armed groups, leaving civilians caught in the middle. Airstrikes in populated areas have repeatedly resulted in civilian deaths throughout Myanmar in recent years.

The deaths add to a rising toll of civilian casualties in a conflict that has displaced hundreds of thousands of people across Myanmar's states and regions.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Seven civilians in Rakhine state killed in a military airstrike
events.alert_summary: A military airstrike killed seven civilians in Rakhine state, Myanmar.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kyauktaw
events.body:
Military aircraft struck the town of Kyauktaw, killing eight civilians and destroying at least 30 buildings, according to the Arakan Army. The strikes hit a populated area, leaving residents without shelter and causing casualties among the local civilian population.

Kyauktaw is located in Rakhine State, a region that has seen ongoing fighting between the military and the Arakan Army. Airstrikes on towns in this area have been a recurring pattern, with civilians bearing much of the impact.

Thirty buildings destroyed represents a significant loss of housing and structures in a town setting, which can displace many families even among those who survive.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Eight civilians in Kyauktaw killed in airstrikes, families left without homes
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed 8 civilians and destroyed 30 buildings in Kyauktaw, Myanmar.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Kumasi
events.body:
Police in Kumasi, Ghana, confronted a carjacking syndicate in a shootout that left two gang members dead and one suspect in custody. The operation appears to be part of efforts to break up an organized group responsible for vehicle theft in the area.

Kumasi is Ghana's second-largest city, and criminal syndicates operating there can disrupt daily life for large numbers of residents and commuters. No police casualties were reported in the available information.

The arrest may give authorities further leads into the network's operations, though the full size of the syndicate is not yet clear from available reports.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Kumasi residents living under threat from a carjacking syndicate, families of those killed
events.alert_summary: Police in Kumasi killed 2 gang members and arrested 1 suspect in a shootout with a carjacking syndicate.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sumy
events.body:
A Russian airstrike set fire to warehouses storing sulfur residues near the city of Sumy in northeastern Ukraine. Local authorities stated that the situation does not pose a danger to residents in the surrounding area.

Sumy region has faced repeated cross-border attacks due to its proximity to the Russian border. Strikes on industrial or storage facilities raise concern among civilians even when officials report no immediate threat, as fires involving chemical residues can produce smoke that affects air quality.

Residents in the area were monitoring the situation following official assurances that no evacuation or emergency measures were required.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents near Sumy living close to warehouses set alight by an airstrike
events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike ignited warehouses containing sulfur residues near Sumy, Ukraine; authorities report no danger to the public.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
The Civil Defence headquarters in Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, was destroyed in an airstrike. The facility serves as a base for emergency responders who assist civilians during attacks and other crises.

The loss of this building removes a key coordination point for civil emergency response in the region. Emergency workers operating out of Nabatieh would normally be among the first to respond to injuries, fires, and rescue needs caused by ongoing strikes in southern Lebanon.

No casualty figures were available from the source material. Residents of Nabatieh and surrounding areas who depend on civil emergency services now face reduced capacity for emergency response.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Emergency responders and civilians in Nabatieh after the Civil Defence headquarters was destroyed
events.alert_summary: An airstrike destroyed Lebanon's Civil Defence headquarters in Nabatieh; casualty figures not reported in source.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Borno
events.body:
Military personnel in Borno state detained a person believed to be passing information to Boko Haram, according to a report from Idoma Voice Newspaper. No further details about the location of the arrest within Borno or the circumstances leading to the detention were available in the summary.

Borno state in northeastern Nigeria has long been a center of Boko Haram activity, and military operations to identify and detain suspected collaborators are ongoing in the region. Residents in affected communities continue to live under the pressure of insurgent presence and counterinsurgency operations.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities in Borno state living under the threat of Boko Haram activity
events.alert_summary: Troops arrested a suspected Boko Haram informant in Borno state; no casualties reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in English Channel
events.body:
A Russian-flagged oil tanker entered the English Channel on Wednesday evening, the first sanctioned vessel to do so since UK forces boarded and seized another shadow fleet ship, the Smyrtos, the previous Sunday. The tanker, which left a Baltic Sea port on June 12 after loading oil, is broadcasting a destination of a port in China.

The vessel has been sanctioned by the UK, the US, and the EU, and has changed its name twice since being accused of smuggling Russian oil. A Russian navy frigate that earlier fired warning shots toward a British yacht in the Channel may be operating in the area, though its exact position relative to the tanker was unclear as of Wednesday evening.

Analysts say the legal basis for boarding this tanker is weaker than for the Smyrtos, since it is openly flying a Russian flag rather than sailing without registration. The shadow fleet carries around 75% of Russia's sanctioned oil exports, according to the UK Ministry of Defence.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians and sailors near the English Channel as Russian sanctioned tankers and warships transit the waterway
events.alert_summary: A sanctioned Russian tanker entered the English Channel on Wednesday as a Royal Navy warship operated nearby.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kfar Tebnit
events.body:
Israeli forces launched strikes in southern Lebanon amid a surge in fighting with Hezbollah. Witnesses reported Lebanese citizens killed and Israeli soldiers wounded during what sources describe as a new Israeli offensive in the area.

Social media footage showed rockets and helicopters over the towns of Kfar Tebnit and Ali al-Taher. The scale of casualties beyond initial reports remains unclear.

Residents in the area face danger from both air strikes and ground activity. The situation is developing and conditions for civilians in southern Lebanon remain uncertain.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese civilians and Israeli soldiers caught in renewed fighting in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon, killing Lebanese citizens and wounding Israeli soldiers in renewed clashes.

events.title: Khan Younis
events.body:
More than 18,500 patients in Gaza require medical care that cannot be provided inside the territory, according to the World Health Organization. Israel has kept the Rafah crossing closed since February 28, halting all medical evacuations. Gaza's Ministry of Health says 6 to 10 patients die each day while waiting to travel abroad for treatment, and roughly 1,200 have died since Israel seized the Rafah crossing in May 2024.

Before October 2023, between 50 and 100 patients left Gaza daily for medical care. That flow has now stopped almost entirely. Save the Children estimates that at the current pace, evacuating all patients in need could take more than a year.

Among those waiting is a 15-year-old girl in Khan Younis whose eye pressure has reached more than double the normal range. She has held a medical evacuation referral for nearly a year, but permission to leave has not been granted. Specialists and the supplies she needs do not exist inside Gaza.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: More than 18,500 patients in Gaza blocked from medical evacuations, losing health and life while waiting
events.alert_summary: Gaza's Health Ministry says 6 to 10 patients die daily waiting for blocked medical evacuations, with roughly 1,200 deaths since May 2024.

events.title: Displacement in southern Lebanon
events.body:
People who had fled their homes are beginning to return to southern Lebanon after a ceasefire agreement took hold. The ceasefire follows a deal involving the United States and Iran, though the source notes that tensions remain in the area despite the agreement.

Conditions are described as fragile, meaning the situation could change quickly. Returning residents face uncertainty about what they will find when they arrive home.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents returning to southern Lebanon under a fragile ceasefire, uncertain of what they will find
events.alert_summary: Residents began returning to southern Lebanon as a fragile ceasefire took hold following a US-Iran deal.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Rimal neighbourhood, Gaza City
events.body:
An Israeli drone struck a vehicle near Abu Khadra Mosque in the Rimal neighbourhood of western Gaza City on Thursday, killing at least three Palestinians and wounding several others. Residents said the vehicle was a jeep driven by civilians. It was described as the first explosion in the area after several quiet days.

Earlier the same day, Israeli fire wounded a fisherman off Gaza's coast and a man near Khan Younis. A girl was also killed by Israeli gunfire in Beit Lahiya in northern Gaza.

Gaza's Health Ministry says at least 1,007 Palestinians have been killed by Israeli fire since a US-brokered ceasefire took effect, bringing the total death toll in Gaza to 73,018 since October 7, 2023. Israeli forces now control about 64 percent of the Gaza Strip, and a rights group reports that recent boundary shifts in the Tuffah neighbourhood east of Gaza City have forced dozens of residents from their homes.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Gaza City killed and wounded in ongoing drone strikes amid a ceasefire that has not stopped the violence
events.alert_summary: An Israeli drone strike near Abu Khadra Mosque in Gaza City killed at least 3 Palestinians and wounded several others on Thursday.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
An attack on a vehicle in Gaza City killed at least three Palestinians, according to recent reports. The strike is the latest in a series of attacks that have continued following a US-brokered ceasefire reached in October 2025.

Since that ceasefire, more than 1,000 people have been killed in Gaza. The ongoing strikes raise serious concerns for civilians still living in the territory, where people face continued danger despite the ceasefire agreement.

Those living, working, or serving in Gaza remain in a difficult and unpredictable situation as attacks continue.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Over 1,000 Palestinians killed in Gaza since a ceasefire, with families continuing to lose loved ones
events.alert_summary: An attack on a vehicle in Gaza City killed at least 3 Palestinians, bringing the post-ceasefire death toll past 1,000.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Haddatha, Bint Jbeil district
events.body:
A drone carried out an airstrike on the town of Haddatha, located in the Bint Jbeil district of southern Lebanon. No injuries were reported following the strike.

Haddatha lies in a part of southern Lebanon that has seen recurring airstrikes in recent months. The Bint Jbeil district sits close to the border and has been affected by ongoing cross-border activity.

Residents in the area remain exposed to the possibility of further strikes, even when casualties are not immediately reported.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Haddatha in the Bint Jbeil district following a drone strike on their town
events.alert_summary: A drone airstrike hit the town of Haddatha in southern Lebanon's Bint Jbeil district; no injuries were reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Niamey
events.body:
Gunmen attacked the airport in Niamey, the capital of Niger, with explosions reported during the assault. Few additional details are available from the source about casualties or the extent of damage to the facility.

Niamey's airport serves as a key point of entry and exit for the country. An attack on this location would affect travelers, airport workers, and surrounding neighborhoods.

Niger has faced ongoing instability since a military takeover in 2023, and armed groups have been active across the broader Sahel region. Residents near the airport would have been directly exposed to the violence.
events.source_title: "Sahel OR Burkina Faso OR Mali OR Niger attack OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians and airport workers in Niamey caught in an armed attack
events.alert_summary: Gunmen attacked the airport in Niamey, Niger's capital, with explosions reported during the assault.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Guayaquil
events.body:
A man police identified as the leader of a gang called Los Águilas was shot dead outside the arrivals terminal at Guayaquil's airport. Two young men concealed handguns behind stuffed toys and flowers while waiting for him, then fired at close range. One bystander was struck during the attack, and passengers scattered as shots rang out. Police detained two teenagers in connection with the killing.

The attack came one day after Ecuador's president declared a state of emergency across ten provinces, including Guayas, where Guayaquil is located. That declaration gives security forces expanded powers, including the ability to search homes without a warrant under reasonable suspicion.

Los Águilas was designated a terrorist organization by the government in 2024 and is accused of drug trafficking and extortion. Ecuador sits between the world's two largest coca-producing countries, making it a key smuggling corridor. The country's murder rate reached a record high in 2025.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Guayaquil and ten provinces under a state of emergency amid rising gang violence
events.alert_summary: Gunmen shot dead a suspected gang leader outside Guayaquil's airport, wounding one bystander.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Moscow
events.body:
Close to 200 Ukrainian drones struck the Moscow region in what officials described as the largest Ukrainian drone attack since the full-scale war began. The Kapotnya oil refinery in south-east Moscow was hit, catching fire and sending thick black smoke into the sky. A nearby shopping centre also burned after drone debris fell on it. Seventeen people were wounded in the Moscow region, and one person was killed when an oil depot was struck in the southern Rostov region.

Residents in the south-east reported a fine drizzle leaving black spots on their clothes and coating car parks in an oily sheen. Moscow authorities told residents in the affected district to keep windows closed, and advised families with children, elderly people, and asthmatics to leave the area. Moscow's four airports were temporarily shut, and more than 500 flights were cancelled or delayed.

Russia's defence ministry said almost 1,000 drones and four cruise missiles were intercepted across the country in 24 hours. Russian officials warned strikes on Ukraine would follow "on a mass scale."
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Moscow and across Russia affected by drone strikes and air pollution
events.alert_summary: Ukraine launched close to 200 drones at Moscow, hitting a refinery and wounding 17 people in the region.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Biala Podlaska
events.body:
A Russian artist living in exile in Poland was shot and killed in Biala Podlaska, a town east of Warsaw near the Ukrainian border. The victim, known for satirical caricatures of Russian, Soviet, Belarusian, and Chechen leaders, was shot five times, including once in the head. The attacker then approached and fired two more shots at close range.

Polish authorities, including the national security agency, have detained at least one suspect traveling on a Georgian passport, along with two Belarusian nationals who were held but not charged. The prime minister described the killing as a "political murder" and said investigators are still trying to identify who ordered it.

Officials have not ruled out involvement by Russian or Chechen actors. The victim had been living in Poland since 2021 and had previously declined a government offer of protection. Three days before his death, he staged a protest outside the Russian embassy in Berlin.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Russian exile artist shot dead in Poland; family and community mourning amid unresolved questions
events.alert_summary: A Russian dissident artist was shot five times and killed in Biala Podlaska, Poland; one suspect detained.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Moscow
events.body:
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow oil refinery for the second time in one week, with the facility reported to still be burning after emergency services worked to contain the fires. The refinery supplies about 40 percent of Moscow's fuel. Russia said its air defenses shot down 555 Ukrainian drones overnight, with nearly 200 intercepted near the capital. A shopping center, a high-rise residential building, an industrial facility, and private homes in the surrounding region were also damaged. Moscow's busiest airport suspended flights and evacuated passengers.

Russia struck back at Kyiv with ballistic missiles and drones overnight. Ukrainian officials said at least 239 drones and seven ballistic missiles were launched at Ukraine, hitting nine locations. One person was killed in a drone attack on the northeastern city of Sumy.

Earlier this week, a Russian attack on Kyiv killed 11 people and damaged a centuries-old monastery. Civilians in both countries continue to face repeated overnight strikes with no ceasefire in sight.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Kyiv and Moscow caught in overnight drone and missile exchanges
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow oil refinery twice this week while Russian missiles and drones hit Kyiv and Sumy, killing at least one person.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Cleveland, Gauteng
events.body:
A shooting in the Cleveland area of Gauteng, South Africa left 12 people dead. Authorities have described the incident as a massacre, though details about suspects, motive, or the exact circumstances have not been provided in available reporting.

Cleveland is a township area within the Johannesburg metro. Incidents of this scale involving multiple victims are relatively uncommon and have drawn significant attention from local media and officials.

Residents in the area are affected by the violence, and families of those killed are now dealing with loss. No information on arrests or suspects was available at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Twelve people killed in a shooting in Cleveland, Gauteng, and their grieving families
events.alert_summary: A shooting in Cleveland, Gauteng killed 12 people in what authorities are calling a massacre.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Mudug region
events.body:
Somali military forces carried out an operation in the southern Mudug region, resulting in the reported deaths of 200 Al-Shabaab fighters, according to the country's national news agency. The report did not specify the exact dates of the operation or provide details on how the figure was confirmed.

No information was given about civilian casualties, displaced residents, or damage to towns in the area. The source did not describe the methods used in the operation or whether any soldiers were killed or wounded.

Mudug is a region in central Somalia where Al-Shabaab has maintained a presence for years. Residents in areas where such operations occur often face disruption to movement and daily life.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities in southern Mudug caught in ongoing military operations against Al-Shabaab
events.alert_summary: A military operation in Somalia's southern Mudug region killed 200 Al-Shabaab militants, according to the Somali National News Agency.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Haiti
events.body:
Gang violence in Haiti has claimed more than 2,000 lives so far this year, according to reports. The toll reflects the ongoing grip that armed groups have maintained across much of the country, particularly in and around the capital.

Civilians across Haiti continue to face danger from gang-controlled territories, with many neighborhoods effectively cut off from basic services, aid, and free movement. Displacement remains widespread as residents flee areas where gangs operate.

The violence has also made it difficult for humanitarian workers and local organizations to reach people in need.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Haitian families and communities enduring ongoing gang violence that has killed over 2,000 people this year
events.alert_summary: Gang violence in Haiti has killed over 2,000 people so far this year, according to reports.

events.title: Big Cypress Natural Preserve
events.body:
All immigrant detainees have been moved out of a Florida detention facility known as Alligator Alcatraz, located on an abandoned airstrip inside Big Cypress Natural Preserve near the Everglades. Authorities said the transfers were made for safety reasons as the Atlantic hurricane season began, with the first named storm of 2026 forming in the Gulf of Mexico.

The facility, which could hold up to 3,000 people, operated for about a year before closing. During that time, detainees reported being denied access to lawyers, receiving inadequate medical care, and finding worms in their food. Indigenous leaders from the Miccosukee and Seminole nations also opposed the site, saying its construction harmed their homes and ceremonial sites nearby.

It remains unclear whether the closure is permanent, where the transferred detainees were sent, or how many were moved. Rights groups have called for a permanent shutdown and say the harm caused during its operation cannot be undone.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Immigrant detainees transferred out of a Florida facility amid reports of neglect and denied legal access
events.alert_summary: U.S. authorities transferred all detainees from Florida's Alligator Alcatraz facility, which was designed to hold up to 3,000 people, citing hurricane season safety concerns.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Abuja
events.body:
Police in Abuja arrested four people suspected of involvement in kidnapping and banditry, according to Nigerian outlet TheCable. Those detained include a pregnant woman who worked as a cook, along with three other suspects.
