No further details about the specific incidents, victims, or locations within Abuja were available from the source summary. It is not clear whether the arrests are connected to a single case or multiple criminal operations in the area.

Abuja and its surrounding regions have seen ongoing security concerns related to kidnapping and armed groups. Residents in affected communities continue to face uncertainty about safety.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Four suspects in Abuja linked to kidnapping and banditry, including a pregnant woman
events.alert_summary: Police in Abuja arrested four suspects linked to kidnapping and banditry, including a pregnant cook.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Arakan
events.body:
Military aircraft carried out airstrikes in the Arakan region of western Myanmar, killing two civilians and wounding eight others. The strikes were attributed to the Myanmar military junta.

Arakan — also known as Rakhine State — has seen ongoing fighting between junta forces and the Arakan Army, an ethnic armed group that has gained significant territory in the region over the past year. Civilian casualties from airstrikes have been a recurring pattern in areas where ground fighting is active.

Residents in affected areas continue to face risk from aerial attacks, with limited access to medical care or safe routes out of conflict zones.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Ten civilians in Arakan wounded or killed in airstrikes, including two who lost their lives
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed 2 civilians and wounded 8 others in the Arakan region of Myanmar.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kyauktaw
events.body:
A military airstrike struck the town of Kyauktaw in Rakhine state, killing at least seven civilians. Among those killed was a five-year-old child.

Myanmar's air force has been widely used in the country's ongoing armed conflict, particularly against pro-democracy armed groups. Rights organizations have raised concerns that these strikes regularly hit civilian areas without distinction.

Rakhine state has seen prolonged fighting, and civilians there continue to face danger from aerial attacks.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Seven civilians, including a five-year-old child, killed in an airstrike in Rakhine state
events.alert_summary: A military airstrike in Kyauktaw, Rakhine state killed at least seven civilians, including a five-year-old child.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Kebbi
events.body:
Security forces in Kebbi state, northwestern Nigeria, have arrested 11 people suspected of ties to the Lakurawa militant group and Boko Haram. Those detained include both suspected fighters and individuals believed to have been passing information to the armed groups.

Kebbi state sits near Nigeria's borders with Niger and Benin, an area where Lakurawa — a relatively newer armed group with reported links to jihadist networks in the Sahel — has been expanding its presence alongside longer-established groups like Boko Haram. The arrests suggest security forces are actively working to disrupt local support networks that help these groups operate.

Residents in the region have faced growing pressure from militant activity, including raids and recruitment efforts in rural communities.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities in Kebbi state threatened by Lakurawa and Boko Haram militant networks
events.alert_summary: Police arrested 11 suspected Lakurawa and Boko Haram members and informants in Kebbi state.

events.title: Displacement in Lebanon
events.body:
More than 100 days of conflict in Lebanon have left children bearing some of the heaviest costs, according to a UNICEF report released Wednesday. The agency found that an average of 12 children were killed or wounded every day, even after a ceasefire took hold between Hezbollah and Israel.

Beyond direct harm, the war forced large numbers of families from their homes, adding displacement to the dangers children already faced. The UN agency described the overall picture as a shattering look at civilian life under the strain of strikes and forced movement.

UNICEF's findings point to children continuing to be harmed despite the truce, raising concerns about how protection measures are holding on the ground for Lebanon's most vulnerable residents.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Children in Lebanon harmed daily amid ongoing conflict and displacement
events.alert_summary: UNICEF reports an average of 12 children killed or maimed daily in Lebanon over more than 100 days of war.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza
events.body:
Israeli forces have killed more than 1,000 Palestinians in Gaza since a ceasefire was brokered by the United States, according to reporting by Al Jazeera. Palestinians described the arrangement as a ceasefire in name only, saying that violence on the ground has continued despite the formal agreement.

The report profiles individuals among those killed during this period, giving faces and stories to the death toll that has accumulated since the truce was declared.

Civilians in Gaza continue to face lethal danger even under the terms of a ceasefire, raising serious concerns for residents, families, and anyone with ties to people in the territory.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinians in Gaza killed during a declared ceasefire period, families mourning more than 1,000 deaths
events.alert_summary: More than 1,000 Palestinians have been killed in Gaza since a ceasefire agreement took effect.

events.title: Displacement in Tulkarem
events.body:
Families displaced for more than a year from Tulkarem refugee camp in the West Bank were allowed a brief return, where they found their homes and streets in ruins following months of Israeli military operations in the area.

Residents carried out what little they could salvage from the wreckage while under the watch of soldiers. The extent of the destruction left many in shock.

The visit appears to have been temporary, leaving it unclear when or whether residents will be able to return permanently to the camp.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinian families returning to Tulkarem refugee camp after more than a year of forced displacement
events.alert_summary: Palestinian families briefly returned to Tulkarem refugee camp to find homes and streets destroyed after months of military operations.

events.title: Civil Unrest in India
events.body:
The founder of a recently formed Indian political movement known as the Cockroach Janta Party was attacked, according to a report from Al Jazeera. The incident has drawn renewed attention to the party, which has been active for roughly a month.

The source does not provide details about the location of the attack, the number of people involved, or the condition of the founder afterward. It also does not specify who carried out the attack or the motive behind it.

The report raises questions about the future of the party following the incident.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Founder of India's Cockroach Janta Party following a reported attack
events.alert_summary: The founder of India's Cockroach Janta Party was attacked; no casualty figures are given in the source.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Jumpers
events.body:
The death toll from a massacre in Jumpers has risen to 13, according to reporting from the Daily Sun. No further details about the circumstances, perpetrators, or timing of the attack are available from the source material provided.

Because the source summary contains only a headline and outlet name, specific details about how the attack occurred, who carried it out, or what community was affected cannot be confirmed. The Daily Sun is a Nigerian publication, suggesting the event likely occurred in Nigeria.

Residents in the affected area face uncertainty as the situation continues to develop. The rising toll suggests the initial reports undercounted the number of people killed.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Thirteen people killed in the Jumpers massacre and their grieving families
events.alert_summary: A massacre in Jumpers killed at least 13 people, according to reports.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Damascus
events.body:
ISIS has claimed responsibility for an attack targeting a government official near Damascus, Syria. The group has continued to carry out sporadic attacks inside the country even after losing its territorial hold in the region years ago.

The attack is a reminder that ISIS remains an active threat in Syria, particularly as the country navigates a fragile and shifting political situation. Officials and civilians in areas around the capital remain vulnerable to such strikes.

No casualty figures were available from the source summary. Field workers and ministry personnel operating in or around Damascus should be aware that targeted violence against officials can affect surrounding neighborhoods and civilian movement.
events.source_title: "Syria OR Iraq airstrike OR clashes OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians near Damascus living under threat of continued ISIS attacks
events.alert_summary: ISIS claimed responsibility for an attack targeting an official near Damascus, Syria; casualty figures unknown.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Baidoa
events.body:
A suicide bomber attacked Baidoa town in Somalia, killing four people and wounding six others, according to the Somali National News Agency.

Baidoa is a regional hub in southwestern Somalia and has experienced previous attacks linked to the armed group al-Shabaab, which has carried out bombings across the country. No group claimed responsibility in the available source material.

The wounded were taken for medical care, though conditions for civilians in the area remain uncertain following the blast.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Baidoa wounded and killed in a suicide bombing attack
events.alert_summary: A suicide bomb attack in Baidoa killed 4 people and wounded 6 others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in northern Nigeria
events.body:
A wave of attacks across northern Nigeria left at least 50 people dead within one week, according to reports. The violence affected multiple areas across the region, though specific locations and the groups responsible were not detailed in the available summary.

Northern Nigeria has faced ongoing insecurity from armed groups, including bandits and militants, who regularly target farming communities, travelers, and villages. Residents in affected areas often have little warning before attacks occur.

The scale of the week's violence — 50 deaths across the region — points to conditions that remain dangerous for civilians living and working in the north.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Communities across northern Nigeria grieving 50 killed in one week of attacks
events.alert_summary: Attacks across northern Nigeria killed at least 50 people within a single week.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Butembo
events.body:
Armed men stormed Wanamahika Hospital in Butembo, in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, and took a six-year-old Ebola patient and her mother from the facility. A local health official described the men as "very angry" and carrying knives. Health authorities are urging the child and her mother to return to a care center, warning they risk worsening their condition and spreading the virus to family members.

This incident is part of a broader pattern of attacks on Ebola health facilities during the current outbreak. Last month, police fired warning shots in Mongbwalu after crowds tried to reclaim bodies of patients, and isolation tents were burned in Rwampara after families were prevented from taking a body. Misinformation has fueled the violence, with some communities believing Ebola does not exist and that health organizations are fabricating the outbreak for profit.

The current outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola for which no vaccine yet exists, has confirmed roughly 840 cases and nearly 200 deaths. Conflict in eastern Congo, including areas held by the M23 rebel group, is complicating efforts to trace contacts and contain the spread.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Six-year-old Ebola patient and her mother missing after armed men removed her from hospital care
events.alert_summary: Armed men with knives stormed a hospital in Butembo and removed a six-year-old Ebola patient; she and her mother remain missing.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Poland
events.body:
A Russian artist known for caricatures mocking political figures, including Vladimir Putin, was shot and killed in Poland. The killing came just three days after he staged a performance protest near the Russian embassy in Berlin.

The artist, who went by the stage name Semyon Skrepetsky, was a vocal critic of the Kremlin and had drawn attention through public demonstrations and satirical artwork targeting both Russian and opposition political figures.

No suspect or motive has been confirmed in the source. His death raises concern among Russian dissidents and artists living in exile across Europe.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Family and colleagues of a Russian artist shot dead in Poland following a public protest
events.alert_summary: A Russian artist and political caricaturist was shot dead in Poland three days after a protest near the Russian embassy in Berlin.

events.title: Armed Conflict in NIPSS
events.body:
Armed gunmen stormed the National Institute for Policy and Strategic Studies (NIPSS) in Nigeria, killing at least some soldiers and a police officer during the assault. The attack represents a direct strike on a high-profile government institution.

Details remain limited based on available reporting, including the exact number of attackers, the full casualty count, and whether any civilians inside the facility were harmed or taken. It is also unclear whether the gunmen were repelled or withdrew on their own.

Staff, students, and others present at the institute at the time of the attack may have been caught in the crossfire or placed in danger during the incident.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Soldiers, a police officer, and civilians at NIPSS following an armed attack
events.alert_summary: Gunmen attacked NIPSS, killing soldiers and a police officer; full casualty count unknown.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mansouri
events.body:
Lebanese state news reported that Israeli drones struck the town of Mansouri. No details about casualties, damage, or the intended target were included in the report.

Mansouri is a town in southern Lebanon, an area that has seen repeated strikes in the context of ongoing conflict between Israel and armed groups operating in the region.

Residents in and around the area face continued risk from aerial activity.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Mansouri, Lebanon, under drone airstrike
events.alert_summary: Israeli drones carried out an airstrike on the town of Mansouri, Lebanon; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Duk
events.body:
A road ambush in the Duk area of South Sudan left six people dead, according to a report from Radio Tamazuj. No further details about the attackers or the victims were available in the summary provided.

Road ambushes remain a serious danger for civilians and travelers in parts of South Sudan, where armed violence continues to disrupt daily life. The Duk area has seen periodic unrest, and incidents like this make movement along rural roads dangerous for local communities.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Six people killed in a road ambush in Duk, South Sudan
events.alert_summary: An ambush on a road in Duk killed six people, according to Radio Tamazuj.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Baidoa
events.body:
A suicide bombing in Baidoa, a city in southwestern Somalia, killed four people and wounded six others. No group has claimed responsibility based on the available source material.

Baidoa is the capital of South West State and has been a recurring target for al-Shabaab attacks. The city serves as a regional hub, and violence there affects civilians, aid workers, and local government operations.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Four people killed and six wounded in a suicide bombing in Baidoa
events.alert_summary: A suicide bomb attack in Baidoa killed 4 people and wounded 6 others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oyo
events.body:
Armed bandits carried out an attack in Oyo State, Nigeria, leaving one person dead and several others wounded. Photos from the scene have begun circulating as additional details about the incident come to light.

Bandit attacks have become an increasing concern across various Nigerian states, with rural and semi-rural communities often bearing the brunt of the violence. Residents in affected areas face threats to their safety and livelihoods.

The injured are receiving care, though the full extent of casualties and the specific location within Oyo State had not been fully confirmed at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Oyo State wounded and grieving after an attack by armed bandits
events.alert_summary: Armed bandits attacked a community in Oyo State, killing 1 person and injuring others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in English Channel
events.body:
A Russian frigate fired warning shots near a UK-flagged civilian yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday, about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight. The shots were fired outside British territorial waters in foggy conditions. No injuries were reported.

Both the British and Russian defence ministries confirmed the incident. Russia's account states the frigate crew fired after the yacht appeared on a collision course and repeated attempts to reach the vessel by radio failed. The UK government assessed the Russian ship was drifting at the time.

Britain's prime minister, speaking at the G7 summit in France, called the action "reckless" and said the couple aboard the yacht "must've been terrified." The UK defence ministry described it as an isolated incident with no link to a separate British commando boarding of a tanker suspected of being part of Russia's shadow fleet the previous weekend.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Couple aboard a British yacht after a Russian warship fired warning shots nearby in the English Channel
events.alert_summary: A Russian warship fired warning shots near a British yacht in the English Channel, about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight; no injuries were reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in south Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli forces struck multiple locations in south Lebanon on June 17, even as a broader peace agreement for the region — one that includes Lebanon — has reportedly been reached. The details of that agreement have not yet been made public.

The United States has confirmed that under the deal, Israel will not be required to withdraw to the internationally recognized border with Lebanon. Israeli officials indicated strikes are likely to continue until there is clarity on the terms of the US-Iran agreement.

Residents in south Lebanon remain in an uncertain position — caught between ongoing airstrikes and a peace process whose terms have not yet been disclosed.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Civilians in south Lebanon under airstrikes amid unresolved ceasefire terms
events.alert_summary: Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on several areas in south Lebanon on June 17.

events.title: Displacement in south Lebanon
events.body:
Fresh Israeli strikes have hit south Lebanon even as a ceasefire agreement was announced by the United States and Israel. Despite the ongoing attacks, thousands of residents have been driving back toward their homes in the south, unwilling to wait any longer to return.

The Lebanese government has warned people not to go back yet, but many civilians are choosing to ignore those warnings out of desperation to see their homes and communities. At the same time, many of those returning say they remain uncertain about what they will find and whether the situation is truly safe.

Civilians in the area face a difficult choice between the hardship of displacement and the risk of returning to a region still under attack.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Thousands of south Lebanese civilians returning home amid ongoing Israeli strikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes continued in south Lebanon as thousands of displaced residents attempted to return home despite government warnings.

events.title: Balochistan
events.body:
Thousands of people in Balochistan, Pakistan's largest and poorest province, are risking their lives transporting smuggled Iranian fuel on overloaded motorbikes through temperatures that can reach 50°C (120°F). Plastic fuel canisters swell and soften in the heat, and smugglers are regularly killed when containers split or ignite. Many say they have no other option after drought ended farming livelihoods.

The smuggling has grown in recent months as fuel prices have risen due to disruptions to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. A leaked Pakistani intelligence report estimated that fuel worth $1 billion is smuggled from Iran into Pakistan each year, with around 2.4 million of Balochistan's 15 million residents involved. Pakistan's official petroleum sales have dropped to a 27-year low, partly due to the increase in smuggling.

Balochistan also faces a long-running conflict between Pakistani security forces and separatist insurgents, adding another layer of danger for those traveling its roads.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: 2.4 million fuel smugglers in Balochistan risking death from heat, fire, and armed conflict
events.alert_summary: An estimated 2.4 million people in Balochistan smuggle fuel from Iran amid drought, poverty, and ongoing insurgency.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Durban
events.body:
Malawian migrants gathered at the Sherwood Hall transit site in Durban clashed with police while demanding to be deported home. The migrants are among a larger group of stranded Malawian nationals seeking repatriation.

South African authorities have moved to fast-track deportation hearings in response to the situation. Meanwhile, the Malawian government is actively seeking donations to fund the return of its citizens still stranded in South Africa.

The transit site has become a flashpoint as migrants, unable to leave on their own, have grown frustrated with delays. Families and individuals caught in the situation face uncertainty about when they will be able to return home.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Malawian migrants stranded at Durban transit site seeking to return home
events.alert_summary: Police clashed with Malawian migrants at a Durban transit site demanding deportation back to Malawi.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh al-Fawqa
events.body:
Israeli jets struck the Nabatieh al-Fawqa area and the outskirts of the nearby village of Kfar Tebnit in southern Lebanon on Wednesday, according to Lebanon's state news agency. The Israeli military had not commented at the time of reporting but has previously said its operations target Hezbollah.

The strikes came despite public criticism from US President Donald Trump, who said at the G7 summit in France that Israel had been fighting Hezbollah "too long and too many people are being killed." Trump said Israel's prime minister needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon."

Both Israel and Hezbollah have continued exchanging attacks since the US-Iran agreement was announced on Sunday. Iran's foreign minister warned that any continued Israeli military action in Lebanon would be treated as a violation of that agreement. A formal signing was expected Friday in Switzerland.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of southern Lebanon near Nabatieh al-Fawqa and Kfar Tebnit under renewed airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli jets struck the Nabatieh al-Fawqa area and the outskirts of Kfar Tebnit in southern Lebanon; no casualty figures were reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Israeli strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon, prompting Iran to warn of a "harsh response" if attacks continue. Iran has accused Israel of repeatedly violating the Lebanon truce that was linked to its agreement with the United States.

The situation is straining diplomatic efforts on multiple fronts. The US president publicly criticized the Israeli prime minister, urging him to be "more responsible" in Lebanon. Iran's foreign minister stated that any final deal with Washington must include sanctions relief, the release of frozen Iranian assets, and an Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon.

For civilians in southern Lebanon, the strikes add uncertainty to an already fragile truce, and it remains unclear how ongoing diplomatic tensions between Tehran, Washington, and Jerusalem will affect conditions on the ground.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon killed and at risk amid ongoing Israeli strikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli strikes killed 4 people in southern Lebanon amid tensions over a ceasefire truce.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Port-au-Prince
events.body:
More than one in ten Haitians have been forced from their homes as gang violence continues to grip the country. The United Nations reports 2,300 people killed and 100 kidnapped so far this year, with 1.5 million displaced — including over 300,000 across Port-au-Prince alone, a record number. A government official was among those recently abducted.

Over 30 people were killed, injured, or went missing last weekend in Cité Soleil, a seaside slum in the capital. A gang federation estimated to control 70% of Port-au-Prince has been designated a foreign terrorist organization by the United States.

A new international gang-suppression force, approved by the UN Security Council in September, is due to begin operations in coming weeks. Fewer than 1,000 troops from four countries have deployed so far. Haiti has not had a president since its last head of state was killed in July 2021, and elections have yet to be scheduled.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: 1.5 million Haitians displaced by gang violence, thousands killed or kidnapped this year
events.alert_summary: Gang violence in Haiti has killed 2,300, kidnapped 100, and displaced 1.5 million people so far this year.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Zaporizhzhia
events.body:
A drone struck a residential building in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, injuring at least seven people. Emergency services responded to a fire burning through the structure following the strike.

The attack is described as suspected Russian in origin. No deaths were reported in the source, but the strike caused direct harm to civilians in their homes.

Zaporizhzhia has been a recurring target during the ongoing conflict. Residents in the city continue to face risk from aerial attacks on civilian infrastructure.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: At least seven residents in Zaporizhzhia wounded in a drone strike on their home
events.alert_summary: A drone strike hit a residential building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring at least seven people and starting a fire.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Arakan
events.body:
Military airstrikes in the Arakan region of Myanmar killed two civilians and left eight others wounded, according to Narinjara News. The strikes are attributed to Myanmar's military junta, which has continued aerial operations across the country since seizing power in 2021.

Arakan, also known as Rakhine State, has seen ongoing fighting between junta forces and the Arakan Army, a regional ethnic armed group. Civilians in the area have faced repeated displacement and casualties from airstrikes targeting contested areas.

The injured have been left to seek care in a region where access to medical facilities is already limited by years of conflict.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Arakan wounded and killed in airstrikes, families grieving losses
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed 2 civilians and injured 8 others in Arakan, Myanmar.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan
events.body:
According to the United Nations, drone strikes in Sudan killed more than 1,000 people in the first five months of 2026. The figure points to a sustained and widespread pattern of aerial attacks affecting civilians across the country.

Sudan has been caught in an ongoing war between the national army and a rival paramilitary force since April 2023. Drone attacks have become an increasingly common feature of the conflict, striking residential and civilian areas.

The toll from these strikes alone — separate from ground fighting, shelling, and other violence — underscores the scale of harm civilians continue to face. Aid organizations and the UN have repeatedly raised concerns about access to those injured or displaced by the fighting.
events.source_title: "Sudan RSF OR fighting OR airstrike OR El Fasher when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Sudan killed by drone strikes in the first five months of 2026
events.alert_summary: UN reports drone strikes killed over 1,000 people across Sudan in the first five months of 2026.

events.title: Displacement in Haiti
events.body:
The number of people forced from their homes in Haiti has reached 1.5 million as armed gangs continue to expand their control across the country. The UN Secretary-General traveled to Haiti in response to the worsening situation, signaling the scale of international concern over conditions on the ground.

Gangs now control large portions of the capital and surrounding areas, making movement dangerous for ordinary residents. Displaced families often have nowhere safe to go, and humanitarian workers face serious risks trying to reach people in need.

The visit draws attention to how much the situation has deteriorated, but the 1.5 million displacement figure reflects a crisis that has been building for years and shows no clear signs of slowing.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: 1.5 million people displaced in Haiti as gang violence continues to spread
events.alert_summary: Gang violence in Haiti has displaced 1.5 million people, prompting a visit from the UN Secretary-General.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Isle of Wight
events.body:
A Russian Navy frigate fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday. Russia said the yacht made a "dangerous approach" to the vessel. The incident took place about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight, just outside British territorial waters.

The UK government said it is investigating what happened. No injuries or damage to the yacht were reported in the initial accounts.

The frigate involved is the Admiral Grigorovich, a Russian naval vessel operating in waters close to the English coast. The presence of a Russian warship in the Channel and its use of warning fire against a civilian vessel raises concern for recreational and commercial boat traffic in the area.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Crew of a UK-registered yacht fired upon by a Russian warship in the English Channel
events.alert_summary: A Russian warship fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight; no casualties reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in English Channel
events.body:
A Russian warship fired warning shots near a British couple sailing through the English Channel. The couple described hearing five horn blasts from the Russian frigate, a signal asking whether they had been seen. They adjusted their course to show they were aware of the vessel's presence.

Russia's defence ministry said the yacht had come on a "dangerous approach" toward the warship, and crew fired rifles into its path after attempts to make radio contact and after launching warning flares went unanswered.

Britain's Ministry of Defence described the encounter as an "isolated incident" and said it was not connected to the seizure of a Russian shadow fleet tanker in the same waterway days earlier. The Channel, a busy shipping route, has seen increased attention in recent days following that earlier vessel seizure.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Retired British couple caught in a warning fire incident with a Russian warship in the English Channel
events.alert_summary: A Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British couple's yacht in the English Channel; no injuries reported.

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Hebron
events.body:
Israel's finance minister announced Monday that planning and construction powers at the Ibrahimi Mosque in Hebron — also known as the Cave of the Patriarchs — had been transferred from Palestinian authorities to Israel. The move scraps portions of the 1997 Hebron Agreement, which had given the Palestinian municipality civil control over planning and construction throughout the city, including at the mosque and the adjacent Jewish Tomb of the Patriarchs.

Israel's Foreign Ministry pushed back on the announcement, saying only planning authority over Jewish settlement areas and heritage sites had changed, not the agreement as a whole. The Palestinian Authority condemned the decision as a violation of signed agreements and international law, and called on the international community and the United States to intervene.

Hebron's mayor warned the change amounts to a serious breach of the political framework governing the city's administration. The Ibrahimi Mosque was inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage in Danger list in 2017. Residents in a city already divided between Israeli and Palestinian-controlled zones now face uncertainty over how the site will be governed.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinians and residents of Hebron amid disputed control over a shared holy site
events.alert_summary: Israel's Higher Planning Council transferred construction authority at the Ibrahimi Mosque away from Palestinian control, scrapping parts of a 1997 agreement.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kyiv Pechersk Lavra
events.body:
A Russian drone attack struck Kyiv on Monday, setting fire to the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, a monastery that is more than 1,000 years old. The attack caused damage to the historic site and was described as part of a broader strike on the Ukrainian capital.

Repairs at the monastery have begun following the attack. The monastery is one of the most significant religious and cultural sites in Ukraine.

No specific casualty figures for the monastery were given in the source, though the attack on the capital was described as deadly. Residents in Kyiv continue to face the threat of drone and missile strikes on civilian and historic areas.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Worshippers and community at Kyiv's ancient monastery damaged by drone strike
events.alert_summary: A Russian drone attack set fire to the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery and caused damage across the capital; repairs have begun.

events.title: Democratic Republic of Congo
events.body:
The head of Africa's Centre for Disease Control and Prevention warned on June 16 that tens of thousands of people who had contact with sick patients in the Democratic Republic of Congo are not being tracked. Officials say the outbreak could surpass the 2014–2016 epidemic in West Africa, which killed more than 11,000 people across Guinea, Liberia, and Sierra Leone.

Contact tracing is one of the most important tools for stopping Ebola from spreading. When it breaks down at this scale, the virus can move through communities largely unseen before health workers can respond.

Healthcare workers on the ground have been raising concerns alongside international health officials. The warning reflects how difficult it has become to keep up with the pace of new cases, and the consequences for civilians — especially in areas where health services are already limited — could be serious.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Tens of thousands in Congo exposed to Ebola with no contact tracing underway
events.alert_summary: Africa's CDC head warned that tens of thousands of Ebola contacts in Congo are untraced, risking the worst outbreak in history.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Moscow
events.body:
A Ukrainian strike produced smoke visible near the Kremlin in Moscow, while Russian forces carried out an attack on a notable church inside Ukraine. Both strikes represent an escalation in the exchange of cross-border attacks between the two countries on day 1,573 of the conflict.

The church strike drew attention because of the building's cultural and religious significance to Ukrainians. Details on casualties or structural damage from either strike were not included in the available source material.

Residents near both strike locations face ongoing risk as the conflict continues to bring attacks into populated and historically significant areas on both sides of the border.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine and Russia caught in ongoing cross-border strikes
events.alert_summary: Ukraine struck a target visible from the Kremlin; Russia hit an iconic church in Ukraine.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kanji Lake
events.body:
Armed attackers described as terrorist-kidnappers raided a village near Kanji Lake in Nigeria, killing residents. The attack follows a pattern of armed group violence in rural communities across the country's northwest and central regions.

No specific casualty numbers were available from the source summary. Kanji Lake sits in an area that has seen repeated raids by armed groups who kill, loot, and abduct villagers. Survivors and neighboring communities are left exposed, with little immediate protection.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of villages near Kanji Lake attacked by armed kidnappers
events.alert_summary: Armed attackers stormed a village near Kanji Lake, killing residents; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Santa Marta
events.body:
Rural areas around Santa Marta, a coastal city in northern Colombia, are experiencing rising tensions following clashes involving armed groups. Residents in surrounding communities are caught in the middle as groups compete for control of territory in the region.

The source does not specify which armed groups are involved or provide figures on casualties or displacement. Colombia has long seen competition among guerrilla factions, paramilitary offshoots, and criminal networks, and rural communities often bear the heaviest burden when fighting breaks out.

Civilians in affected areas face uncertainty about movement and safety, and local communities may be cut off from markets, schools, and services during periods of active conflict.
events.source_title: "Colombia OR Ecuador armed group OR massacre OR violence when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Rural communities in Santa Marta caught between armed group clashes and rising tensions
events.alert_summary: Armed group clashes have raised tensions in rural areas around Santa Marta, Colombia; casualties and displacement figures unknown.

events.title: Displacement in Western Cape
events.body:
One month after storms struck South Africa's Western Cape, hundreds of people remain without homes. The floods killed 11 people and damaged tens of thousands of homes across the region.

Many of those displaced are farm workers who lived in housing provided by their employers on agricultural land. That accommodation was completely destroyed, leaving workers with no home and no workplace at the same time. The Western Cape is a major wine-producing region, and the affected communities are closely tied to farm labor.

An outreach organization has been working to support displaced residents, but needs remain high weeks after the storms. With housing wiped out rather than simply flooded, many families face longer timelines to recovery than typical flood survivors.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Thousands of farm workers in Western Cape left without homes after flooding
events.alert_summary: Flooding in South Africa's Western Cape killed 11 people and damaged tens of thousands of homes last month.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Pemba
events.body:
A journalist and opposition activist vanished on January 7, 2025, while traveling by public transport between the cities of Pemba and Nacala in northern Mozambique. He had been in Pemba for several days before his disappearance, working on political organizing connected to the aftermath of the October 2024 general elections.

In the days before he disappeared, he told family members he felt threatened and warned them that "the situation wasn't good." He had publicly denounced the killing of fellow opposition party members and was investigating the Islamist insurgency in Cabo Delgado, its effects on gas development projects, and alleged human rights abuses by both armed groups and soldiers.

His friends and family suspect that security forces were involved, partly because he had been in contact with an opposition lawyer who was assassinated shortly after the 2024 elections. No evidence has been made public confirming what happened to him or where he is.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Family and friends of a journalist missing since January 2025 in Cabo Delgado, seeking answers
events.alert_summary: A journalist and opposition activist disappeared on January 7, 2025, while traveling between Pemba and Nacala, Mozambique.

events.title: Displacement in Qah camp
events.body:
In northwest Syria, hundreds of thousands of people who were displaced during the civil war are still living in makeshift shelters at camps including Qah camp. When the Assad government fell, many hoped to return home, but found their villages destroyed and unlivable.

Most have stayed in the camps, waiting for reconstruction that aid workers say could take years. In the meantime, living conditions are getting worse. Cuts to global humanitarian aid — particularly from the United States — have reduced support for the region, and hospitals and other key services are at risk of shutting down.

The people in these camps remain largely out of sight of international attention, with little clarity on when or whether they will be able to return to their communities.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Hundreds of thousands of displaced Syrians in makeshift camps waiting for reconstruction and facing deteriorating conditions
events.alert_summary: Hundreds of thousands of displaced people remain in camps in northwest Syria as aid cuts threaten hospital closures.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Havana
events.body:
Cuba is experiencing worsening shortages of fuel, food, and medicine following tightened US sanctions. Fewer than 360,000 people visited the island in the first five months of 2026 — a drop of 58% compared to the same period last year — as airlines and hotel chains have pulled out. Air Canada suspended flights indefinitely, and two Spanish hotel groups halted operations at multiple properties ahead of a US government deadline.

The fuel crisis has paralyzed large parts of the economy. Garbage is piling up in city streets, and power cuts have triggered rare public protests. A state-run news outlet reported that the survival rate for children with cancer has fallen from 85% to 65% since January.

Catholic churches are also affected. Nuns at a monastery in Havana are producing communion wafers with as little as two hours of electricity per day, and priests have been asked to ration the wafers used in Mass.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Cuban civilians — including children with cancer — facing fuel shortages, food scarcity, and rising poverty
events.alert_summary: Fuel shortages and sanctions have cut Cuba's foreign visitors by 58%, while children's cancer survival rates have dropped from 85% to 65%.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
Israeli drones struck multiple towns in southern Lebanon on Wednesday, including areas around Nabatieh, Kfar Tebnit, and Tyre, wounding residents and killing at least four people in earlier attacks. Five Israeli soldiers were wounded when two Hezbollah drones detonated near their positions in the south. Hezbollah also launched at least 10 rockets toward Israeli forces near Kfar Tebnit.

A United Nations spokesperson noted that the number of projectiles fired between Israeli forces and Lebanon dropped from 705 the previous Sunday to 174 on Sunday, with 169 attributed to Israel and five to Hezbollah. Violence has slowed since a US-Iran framework agreement was announced, but has not stopped.

Families have begun returning to some villages, but residents expressed doubt about whether the ceasefire would hold. Security sources cited in reports believe Israeli forces are moving to secure strategic high ground around Nabatieh.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of southern Lebanon caught between ongoing strikes and uncertain ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Air strikes on towns in Nabatieh district killed at least 4 and wounded at least 5 soldiers amid a fragile US-Iran framework agreement.

events.title: Armed Conflict in English Channel
events.body:
A Russian naval frigate fired warning shots near a small, motor-less yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday morning. The yacht had drifted toward the warship in foggy conditions after departing from the UK. The Russian vessel sounded an audible warning before firing from roughly 500 yards away. No injuries or damage to the yacht were reported. The UK Ministry of Defence confirmed it is investigating the incident.

The event occurred about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight, outside UK territorial waters, between the Isle of Wight and Normandy. A British naval vessel was already shadowing the Russian frigate at the time. UK officials say they are treating Tuesday's incident as isolated and unconnected to a separate operation days earlier, in which Royal Marine Commandos intercepted a Russian-linked tanker carrying sanctioned oil in the same waterway.

The Russian frigate has been operating in the Channel for some time, resupplied by a repair vessel, and has reportedly escorted other vessels through the area while under Royal Navy monitoring.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians aboard a yacht fired upon by a Russian warship in the English Channel
events.alert_summary: A Russian warship fired warning shots near a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel; no injuries or damage were reported.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Washington, D.C.
events.body:
Federal authorities arrested five men from Ohio, California, Missouri, and Nebraska after uncovering an alleged plot to attack a UFC event held on the White House South Lawn on Sunday. According to court documents, the group planned to use explosive-laden drones to cause panic, then direct fleeing crowds toward a waiting sniper team. A "second wave" was allegedly intended to storm the White House gate.

The plot was uncovered after the mother of a 19-year-old suspect in Ohio contacted local authorities about her son's firearms purchases and online activity. During an FBI interview, the suspect said the group had been communicating since around March through a TikTok group, with vetted members moving to encrypted messaging. Prosecutors said the group expressed antigovernment views and aimed to target wealthy individuals and politicians at the event.

About 4,300 people attended the invite-only event, with another 85,000 watching nearby. All five suspects have been charged with conspiracy to commit murder.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Five men arrested after plotting an attack on a White House public event attended by thousands
events.alert_summary: The FBI arrested five men across four states for allegedly plotting a drone and sniper attack on a UFC event at the White House attended by about 4,300 people.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Albuera, Leyte
events.body:
An ambush in Albuera, in the province of Leyte, left two people dead and three others wounded. Few additional details are available from the source material beyond the confirmed casualty figures.

Albuera is a municipality on Leyte island in the Eastern Visayas region of the Philippines, an area that has seen periodic armed incidents in past years. The circumstances of the attack and those responsible had not been identified in the available report.

Residents in the area may face continued uncertainty following the incident.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Two people killed and three wounded in an ambush in Albuera, Leyte
events.alert_summary: An ambush in Albuera, Leyte killed 2 people and wounded 3 others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kismayo
events.body:
Security forces in the Jubland regional state of Somalia arrested a number of Al-Shabaab militants in Kismayo, according to the Somali National News Agency. No additional details were given about the circumstances of the arrests, the specific location within the town, or what led to the operation.

Kismayo is a port city in southern Somalia and the administrative center of Jubland State. Al-Shabaab has long maintained a presence and carried out attacks across southern Somalia, and security operations against the group are ongoing.

No casualties or injuries were reported in connection with these arrests. Residents in the area continue to live with the risk of militant activity and security operations in and around the city.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Kismayo living near ongoing Al-Shabaab militant activity
events.alert_summary: Jubland State security forces arrested several Al-Shabaab militants in Kismayo town; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Rakhine State
events.body:
Military airstrikes in southern Rakhine State struck a residential area, damaging at least one school and one house and killing livestock, according to a local news report.

No casualties among civilians were reported in the available summary, but the strikes destroyed property and animals that communities depend on for food and income.

Rakhine State has seen ongoing fighting between the military and armed groups, and civilians in rural areas continue to bear the cost of that conflict through damage to homes, schools, and livelihoods.
events.source_title: "Myanmar military OR airstrike OR clashes when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Villagers in southern Rakhine State whose school, home, and livestock were destroyed in airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Military airstrikes in southern Rakhine State damaged a school and house and killed livestock.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kordofan
events.body:
Fighters from the Rapid Support Forces and allied rebel groups attacked communities in Sudan's Kordofan province, killing 14 people. The attack adds to a long pattern of violence across Sudan since fighting between the RSF and the Sudanese Armed Forces broke out in April 2023.

Kordofan has seen repeated episodes of fighting and has been among the regions affected by displacement and food insecurity as the broader conflict continues. Civilians in the area remain caught between armed groups with little ability to leave or find safety.
events.source_title: "Sudan RSF OR fighting OR airstrike OR El Fasher when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Fourteen people killed in Kordofan as RSF and allied rebels continue attacks
events.alert_summary: RSF forces and allied rebels attacked Kordofan province, killing 14 people.

events.title: Mongbwalu
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak centered in Ituri province in the Democratic Republic of Congo has killed more than 140 confirmed people since being declared about a month ago. Authorities believe the Bundibugyo strain of the virus may have been spreading undetected for months before the outbreak was officially confirmed, and it has killed roughly one in five known cases.

Healthcare workers at a treatment center in Mongbwalu have celebrated the recovery of at least two patients, with staff hoping the visible survivals will encourage more sick residents to seek care rather than rely on traditional medicine. The hospital's medical director says patient numbers are increasing since the first recovery.

The response has been complicated by local mistrust and misinformation. A treatment tent at the hospital was set on fire on May 21 by people who had been told that burning centers would end the outbreak, echoing similar attacks during the 2018–2020 outbreak in North Kivu. Officials say a burial event in early February may have triggered a large cluster of infections.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Communities in Ituri province facing an Ebola outbreak that has killed more than 140 people
events.alert_summary: An Ebola outbreak in Ituri province, Congo, has killed more than 140 people, with a treatment center in Mongbwalu attacked by arson on May 21.

events.title: Canberra
events.body:
Four women who were part of a Gaza-bound humanitarian flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in May met with Australia's Foreign Minister and senior police officials on Monday. The eleven Australians were among hundreds of activists detained when Israeli forces stopped the vessel on May 18. The activists say they were kidnapped, raped, and tortured while in custody.

Australia's Federal Police confirmed they had begun inquiries into the allegations, describing a "victim-centred, trauma-informed approach." The Foreign Minister said she believes the activists' accounts and has raised the allegations with Israel on multiple occasions, calling for an independent and transparent investigation.

Israel's embassy in Australia denied the claims, saying no credible evidence had been presented and no formal complaints had been submitted to them. The activists say they intend to pursue the matter fully.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Australian activists alleging assault and torture after flotilla detention in May
events.alert_summary: Australian Federal Police opened an inquiry into assault and torture allegations made by 11 activists detained after a Gaza-bound flotilla was intercepted on 05/18/2025.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Jonglei
events.body:
In early February, fighting between government forces and opposition armed groups swept through Jonglei State. A bomb struck a hospital in Lankien hours after staff evacuated the last patients, including women in labor. Residents fled into surrounding marshland on February 7 as mortar fire hit the town. When some returned, they found the hospital looted and burned, water systems dismantled, vehicles stripped, and homes on the outskirts destroyed.

Satellite imagery analyzed by the Centre for Information Resilience identified destruction across more than 23 incidents between late January and February, with civilian structures including homes, health facilities, and markets appearing burned and looted. Researchers noted that troop movements from west to east followed a path of burning, though satellite data alone cannot confirm intent or responsibility. Both military and opposition forces have been accused of attacking civilians.

Humanitarian observers say the destruction has pushed tens of thousands of people toward famine conditions. Access to the area remains restricted, limiting independent verification of witness accounts.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Tens of thousands of civilians in Jonglei facing displacement, burned villages, and destroyed hospitals

events.title: Armed Conflict in Haris
events.body:
An Israeli airstrike struck a motorcycle in the southern Lebanese town of Haris, according to MTV Lebanon. No further details about casualties or the circumstances of the strike were included in the report.

Haris is a town in southern Lebanon, a region that has seen repeated strikes in recent months. Residents in these areas continue to face the risk of sudden attacks.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Haris, Lebanon, following an airstrike on a motorcycle in their town
events.alert_summary: An Israeli airstrike targeted a motorcycle in the southern Lebanese town of Haris; casualties unknown.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Raqqa
events.body:
An ISIS attack in the Raqqa region of northern Syria killed one member of the Syrian security forces. The attack is the latest in a series of ISIS operations in the area, where the group has maintained a low-level insurgency despite losing territorial control years ago.

Raqqa was once the self-declared capital of the ISIS so-called caliphate and was retaken by Kurdish-led forces in 2017. Since then, ISIS fighters have continued to carry out hit-and-run attacks, ambushes, and assassinations targeting security personnel across northeastern Syria.

Residents and security forces in the region remain vulnerable to these ongoing attacks, which have continued to disrupt stability and daily life across the area.
events.source_title: "Syria OR Iraq airstrike OR clashes OR militants when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Syrian security personnel and civilians in Raqqa amid ongoing ISIS attacks
events.alert_summary: An ISIS attack in Raqqa killed one Syrian security member; no further casualties were reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Zamfara State
events.body:
Armed attackers struck in Zamfara State in northwestern Nigeria, killing 17 people and wounding others. The state has experienced repeated attacks by armed groups over recent years, and communities there remain among the most exposed to rural violence in the country.

No further details about the specific location within the state, the identity of those responsible, or the circumstances of the attack were available in the source.

Residents in Zamfara have long faced disruption to daily life, farming, and movement as a result of ongoing insecurity.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families of the 17 killed and wounded survivors of the armed attack in Zamfara State
events.alert_summary: An armed attack in Zamfara State, Nigeria, killed 17 people and wounded others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ondo
events.body:
Armed men abducted a traditional ruler and wounded his wife during an attack in Ondo State, southwestern Nigeria. The attackers, described as bandits, carried out the raid at the ruler's residence, shooting his wife before taking him away.

The incident reflects a pattern of kidnapping-for-ransom attacks that have spread beyond Nigeria's northwest and north-central regions into areas that previously saw fewer such incidents. Traditional rulers are sometimes targeted because of their community standing and perceived wealth.

No information on the wife's condition, the number of attackers, or any ransom demand was available in the source. Security forces had not publicly commented at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A traditional ruler kidnapped and his wife shot by bandits in Ondo
events.alert_summary: Bandits in Ondo State kidnapped a traditional ruler and shot his wife; no casualty figures reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Katsina
events.body:
A retired senior Nigerian military officer was abducted by bandits in Katsina state and later found dead. Nigeria's president publicly mourned the loss of the officer, who had reached the rank of major general during his military career.

Katsina state, in Nigeria's northwest, has faced ongoing banditry for years. Armed groups in the region regularly carry out kidnappings, raids on villages, and attacks on travelers and residents.

The killing of a retired senior officer highlights that even high-profile individuals are not safe from bandit activity in the region. Civilians living in affected communities continue to face the threat of abduction and violence.
events.source_title: "Nigeria attack OR kidnapping OR bandits OR Boko Haram when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Family and colleagues of a retired Nigerian general killed after abduction by armed bandits in Katsina
events.alert_summary: Armed bandits in Katsina abducted and killed a retired Nigerian major general; 1 person dead.

events.title: Rio de Janeiro
events.body:
Two helicopters collided in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, killing six people. Among those killed was a 32-year-old American musician known for several widely shared songs.

No further details about the cause of the collision or the identities of the other five people killed were provided in the source. It is unclear whether the collision occurred over a populated area or what risk, if any, was posed to people on the ground.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Six people killed in a mid-air helicopter collision over Rio de Janeiro, including families left behind
events.alert_summary: Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, killing six people, including one American musician.

events.title: Edwards Air Force Base
events.body:
A US Air Force B-52 bomber crashed immediately after take-off from Edwards Air Force Base in southern California on Monday morning, killing all eight people on board. The crew included military personnel, government civilians, and contractors — among them two Boeing employees. The aircraft burst into flames and the crash was confined to the runway area of the base.

Officials said the crash was deemed "unsurvivable" after reviewing initial footage. No cause has been determined yet, and investigations are expected to take up to 30 days, with further analysis potentially running more than six months. The base temporarily grounded flight operations and suspended non-commercial visitor access while emergency response continued.

The victims' names will be released 24 hours after their next of kin are notified. Families of the eight crew members are now receiving notification from the base.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Eight crew members — military, civilian, and contractor — killed in a bomber crash at Edwards Air Force Base
events.alert_summary: A B-52 bomber crashed on take-off at Edwards Air Force Base in California, killing all eight people on board.

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
A journalist for Press TV was struck by shrapnel while filming a report on Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon. The journalist said he was wearing a clearly marked "Press" vest at the time of the strike and was taken to a hospital for treatment.

Press-identified reporters working in conflict zones rely on visible markings to signal their non-combatant status. The wounding of a journalist during active reporting highlights the ongoing danger facing civilians and media workers in southern Lebanon.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Journalist wounded by shrapnel while reporting in southern Lebanon
events.alert_summary: A journalist was wounded by shrapnel from an Israeli strike while filming in southern Lebanon.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Abuja
events.body:
Police in Nigeria's capital, Abuja, have arrested four people connected to kidnapping and banditry operations in the area. Those detained include a pregnant woman who worked as a cook, along with three other suspects.

Authorities did not release details on the number of victims involved or the scale of the criminal activity. The arrests suggest ongoing efforts by Nigerian police to disrupt criminal networks operating in and around the capital.

Abuja and its surrounding areas have seen periodic kidnapping incidents in recent years. Residents in affected communities remain at risk while investigations continue.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Four suspects in Abuja detained amid kidnapping and banditry investigations
events.alert_summary: Police in Abuja arrested four suspects, including a pregnant woman, linked to kidnapping and banditry.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sloviansk
events.body:
An airstrike struck Sloviansk, a city in eastern Ukraine, killing three members of the same family and wounding five people.

Sloviansk is located in the Donetsk region, an area that has experienced repeated attacks since the early stages of the broader conflict. Residential areas in the city have been hit on multiple occasions, and many civilians who remain do so with limited access to safe shelter.

The wounded were reported to number five, though no further details about their condition or the exact location of the strike within the city were included in the source.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Three family members killed and five wounded by an airstrike in Sloviansk
events.alert_summary: A Russian airstrike in Sloviansk killed three family members and wounded five others.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Colombia-Venezuela border
events.body:
A journalist was killed near the border between Colombia and Venezuela, according to reports. Authorities have opened an investigation into the case, but no further details about the circumstances or suspects have been made public.

The Colombia-Venezuela border region has long been affected by the presence of armed groups, making it one of the more dangerous areas in the country for civilians and media workers alike.

No additional figures on casualties or arrests were available at the time of reporting. The investigation is ongoing.
events.source_title: "Colombia OR Ecuador armed group OR massacre OR violence when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Colombian journalist and family members near the Venezuela border following a killing
events.alert_summary: A journalist was killed near the Colombia-Venezuela border; authorities are investigating.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Port-au-Prince
events.body:
A senior Haitian security official and members of their family were kidnapped in Port-au-Prince, according to reports. No further details about the number of people taken or the circumstances of the abduction were available from the source.

Kidnapping has been a persistent threat in Haiti, where armed gangs have extended their reach across much of the capital and surrounding areas. Targeting a government security official signals that gang activity continues to affect people at all levels of Haitian society.

Residents of Port-au-Prince face ongoing risks of abduction, violence, and displacement as armed groups maintain control over large portions of the city.
events.source_title: "cartel OR gang massacre OR kidnapping OR shootout when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: A senior security official and family members taken by kidnappers in Port-au-Prince
events.alert_summary: Armed kidnappers abducted a senior Haitian security official and family members in Port-au-Prince; no casualty figure reported.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Haiti
events.body:
Gang violence in Haiti has killed at least 2,300 people so far this year, according to the United Nations human rights chief. The announcement signals that conditions on the ground remain dangerous for civilians across the country.

Haiti has struggled with gang activity for several years, with armed groups controlling significant portions of the capital and other areas, making movement and daily life difficult for residents. Kidnappings and killings have driven many families from their homes.

The UN figure represents deaths recorded within the year to date, though the actual toll may be higher given how difficult it is to document casualties in areas controlled by armed groups. Civilians, including aid workers and church personnel operating in affected areas, continue to face risks related to gang activity throughout the country.
events.source_title: "Haiti gang violence OR kidnapping when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Over 2,300 Haitians killed this year amid ongoing gang violence across the country
events.alert_summary: Gang violence has killed at least 2,300 people in Haiti so far this year, according to the UN human rights chief.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Oaxaca
events.body:
A mayor in the Mexican state of Oaxaca was shot and killed, according to reports. The official had sought protection from authorities weeks before the attack, suggesting awareness of a threat to their life. The state governor publicly condemned the killing.

The murder highlights the ongoing danger faced by local elected officials in parts of Mexico where armed groups operate. Mayors and municipal leaders in rural and semi-rural areas are frequently targeted, particularly when they resist or are perceived to stand in the way of criminal organizations.

Communities in these areas are left with disrupted local government and a climate of fear that makes public service increasingly difficult. Residents in affected areas often have little recourse when violence reaches into local institutions.
events.source_title: "Mexico cartel violence OR kidnapping OR gunmen when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Residents of Oaxaca grieving the killing of a local mayor who sought protection
events.alert_summary: A mayor in Oaxaca, Mexico was shot and killed weeks after requesting protection from authorities.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Geneva
events.body:
Protesters clashed with police in Geneva ahead of the G7 summit taking place in neighboring France. Officers deployed tear gas and water cannon to disperse the crowd. During the demonstration, some protesters smashed windows and set a car on fire.

One protester, who said they were disappointed by the violence, described the demonstration as an attempt to send a message about what they called oppression through money and power.

The G7 summit was scheduled to begin on June 15 in Évian-les-Bains, France, bringing together leaders from Britain, France, Canada, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United States, and the European Union. Residents near the protest site in Geneva would have faced disruption from the clashes and dispersal measures.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Protesters and police in Geneva amid property damage and crowd dispersal
events.alert_summary: Police used water cannon and tear gas on anti-G7 protesters in Geneva after windows were smashed and a car was set on fire.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Crimea
events.body:
Ukrainian drone strikes have targeted fuel depots, supply routes, and military infrastructure across Russian-occupied Crimea, causing widespread fuel shortages and panic among residents.

Long lines of hundreds of cars stretched for kilometers at gas stations near Simferopol, with one resident waiting nearly seven hours to buy 20 liters of fuel at high prices. Russian tourists cut their holidays short and fled across the Crimean Bridge, and residents expect shortages to worsen.

Since mid-May, Ukrainian drones have attacked trucks carrying fuel and supplies along the land route through occupied Ukrainian territory into Crimea. The Chongar bridge, a key entry point into the peninsula, was damaged and can only handle light vehicles. Cargo ships bringing food and fuel have also been targeted. Analysts quoted in the source say Ukraine has effectively turned Crimea into an isolated zone encircled by the fighting, leaving civilians dependent on shrinking and disrupted supply lines.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Crimean residents facing fuel shortages, supply disruptions, and daily drone strikes across the peninsula

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
A ceasefire agreement announced between the United States and Iran has raised cautious hopes in Lebanon, where fighting has killed at least 3,783 people and wounded 11,699 since March 2. More than 1.2 million people have been displaced from southern Lebanon, Beirut's southern suburbs, and the Bekaa Valley. Israeli forces continue to occupy parts of southern Lebanon, and officials are warning residents of border villages not to return until conditions are clearer.

Some displaced residents have already begun traveling back to their villages, though many have little to return to. Homes have been destroyed, and residents report serious financial hardship. Two of the south's most populated areas, Tyre and Nabatieh, suffered widespread destruction from recent bombing and evacuation orders.

Lebanese officials have welcomed the announcement but expressed uncertainty about its implementation. A previous ceasefire in November 2024 failed to hold, with neither a full Israeli troop withdrawal nor a halt to hostilities following. Residents say they are waiting to see whether this agreement will translate into actual calm on the ground.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Over 1.2 million Lebanese displaced from the south, Beirut suburbs, and Bekaa Valley awaiting ceasefire implementation
events.alert_summary: A US-Iran ceasefire announced Monday has raised hopes in Lebanon, where at least 3,783 have been killed and 1.2 million displaced since March.

events.title: Displacement in southern Lebanon
events.body:
People are beginning to return to their homes in southern Lebanon following a ceasefire agreement. The mood among residents is described as cautiously optimistic, though the situation remains fragile.

No casualty figures or displacement numbers are provided in the source. The ceasefire appears to be holding, allowing movement back into areas that had been evacuated, but the stability of the agreement is uncertain.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of southern Lebanon returning home under a fragile ceasefire
events.alert_summary: Displaced residents began returning to southern Lebanon as a ceasefire took hold.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan
events.body:
The United Nations reports that drone strikes in Sudan have killed more than 1,000 people over a five-month period. The scale of casualties points to an ongoing pattern of aerial attacks affecting civilian populations across the country.

Sudan has been gripped by fighting between rival military factions since April 2023, and aerial strikes have become a recurring feature of the conflict. The UN figure underscores the toll the war continues to take on ordinary people caught in areas of active fighting.

With millions already displaced and humanitarian access limited in many parts of the country, the addition of widespread drone strikes raises further concern for communities with little ability to move to safety.
events.source_title: "Ethiopia OR Somalia al-Shabaab OR clashes OR drone when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Civilians in Sudan killed by drone strikes over five months of ongoing conflict
events.alert_summary: Drone strikes in Sudan killed over 1,000 people across five months, according to the UN.

events.title: Civil Unrest in London
events.body:
A British court found two men guilty of conspiracy to commit arson after a series of fires targeted properties and a vehicle connected to Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The attacks took place over five days in May last year. One of the men was also convicted on counts of damaging property by fire and endangering lives.

Prosecutors said one of the convicted men was directed by an unidentified Russian-speaking contact online, using the alias "El Money" via the Telegram messaging app, and was offered around $4,000 in cryptocurrency to carry out the attacks. Officials said there is no confirmed evidence the attacks were state-sponsored, though Counter Terrorism Policing London said the online handler aimed to create "unrest" in the UK.

On the day of the verdict, the BBC published an investigation claiming the attacks were part of a broader campaign linked to the Russian state, naming a specific individual — claims Al Jazeera could not independently verify. The two convicted men are due to be sentenced Friday.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians and public officials in the UK amid arson attacks and suspected foreign interference
events.alert_summary: A London court convicted two men for arson attacks targeting properties linked to the UK prime minister in May 2025.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Beirut's Southern Suburbs
events.body:
An Israeli airstrike struck Beirut's southern suburbs, killing three people. The source does not provide additional details about the location of the strike, the identities of those killed, or whether others were wounded.

The southern suburbs of Beirut have seen repeated strikes in recent months as part of broader conflict between Israel and armed groups in Lebanon. Residents of the area continue to face risk from aerial attacks.
