Fighting has continued despite a ceasefire agreed in April and a conditional truce announced the previous week. Lebanese authorities say Israeli attacks since March have killed nearly 3,700 people. The conditional truce deal, which called for a halt to Hezbollah fire but not Israeli strikes, was rejected by Hezbollah.
events.source_title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon and Sidon killed and displaced amid ongoing airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes across southern and eastern Lebanon killed 12 people on Wednesday, including nine in Tayr Debba and three in Sidon.

events.title: Armed Conflict in south Lebanon
events.body:
Airstrikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon, according to Lebanese sources cited by Reuters. The strikes are part of continued military activity in the south of the country.

Residents in the affected areas face ongoing danger from aerial bombardment. The report gives no further details on the specific locations struck, the identities of those killed, or whether any additional casualties were reported.
events.source_title: "massacre OR coup OR airstrike OR ambush when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Thirteen people killed in south Lebanon airstrikes and the communities living under ongoing bombardment
events.alert_summary: Airstrikes killed 13 people in south Lebanon, according to Lebanese sources.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gulf of Oman
events.body:
US forces fired on the Palau-flagged oil tanker Settebello in the Gulf of Oman on Tuesday evening, striking a vessel carrying 24 Indian sailors. The tanker reported an engine room fire about 37 kilometers northeast of the Omani port of Sohar. The Omani navy responded to the distress call and rescued 21 crew members; three remain missing.

India's government summoned the US Embassy's deputy chief of mission in New Delhi to protest the strike. India's Foreign Ministry condemned the attack and called for an end to strikes on commercial shipping in the region, saying its embassy in Oman is coordinating with Omani authorities on the ongoing search and rescue effort.

The UN's shipping agency secretary-general also condemned the incident, saying endangering seafarers is "simply unacceptable." Families of the three missing sailors are still awaiting news.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Three Indian seafarers missing after a strike on a commercial tanker in the Gulf of Oman
events.alert_summary: US forces struck the tanker Settebello off Oman, leaving 3 of 24 Indian crew members missing after 21 were rescued.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Bandar Abbas
events.body:
The United States carried out strikes on Iranian targets for the second straight night on Wednesday, with explosions reported in Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Gorgan, and Hengam. Air defenses were also activated in the Fars region. The US Secretary of Defense described the strikes as targeting "key facilities" and framed them as pressure to advance ceasefire negotiations.

The attacks broke a temporary ceasefire that had been in place since April 8. The current escalation followed the downing of a US military helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz earlier in the week, which US officials attributed to Iran. No US service members were reported hurt in that incident.

Officials have offered varying reasons for continuing the campaign, including halting Iran's nuclear program and pressing for a negotiated settlement. Concerns have been raised by human rights groups after statements about potentially targeting civilian infrastructure such as bridges and energy systems.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Iranian cities facing a second night of US airstrikes amid stalled ceasefire talks
events.alert_summary: US forces launched a second consecutive night of strikes on Iran, with explosions reported in at least four locations including Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Gorgan, and Hengam.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Mexico City
events.body:
A teachers' union has occupied Mexico City's main square for more than a week, blocking the plaza set to host the country's opening World Cup fan festival. Protesters have toppled World Cup statues and blocked roads as part of an annual push for better working conditions. Families of Mexico's more than 130,000 missing people also gathered, hanging flyers of loved ones and calling on authorities to address humanitarian needs instead of investing in the tournament.

Officials acknowledged the plaza may not be available and noted 18 alternative venues where fans could watch for free. The government says everything is under control. More than 100,000 soldiers, marines, and police officers are deployed across the three Mexican host cities.

In Guadalajara, heavily armed security forces have been patrolling streets following cartel violence in February. Local officials say calm returned within 48 hours of that outbreak and are urging international visitors to attend without concern.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families of 130,000 missing people in Mexico seeking justice amid World Cup celebrations
events.alert_summary: Teachers' union protesters blocked Mexico City's main plaza, threatening the World Cup opening fan festival.

events.title: Displacement in South Africa
events.body:
Nigerian migrants in South Africa are returning home after a campaign group gave people living in the country without documents until June 30 to leave. Migrants say they are living in fear, and Nigeria's diplomatic mission in South Africa says many of those departing no longer feel safe to live or work there.

The departures follow a spike in xenophobic protests. Nigeria's mission has acknowledged the scale of people choosing to leave rather than risk staying past the deadline.

Migrants who leave lose not only their homes but also their income and livelihoods, with consequences reaching families both in South Africa and back in Nigeria.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Nigerian migrants in South Africa fleeing xenophobic threats and losing livelihoods
events.alert_summary: Nigerian migrants are leaving South Africa after a campaign group set a June 30 deadline for undocumented people to leave.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Bondi Beach
events.body:
A man accused of killing 15 people in a shooting at a Jewish festival at Bondi Beach in December now faces 78 charges in total. Authorities confirmed 19 additional charges this week, including counts of shooting with intent to murder, firing at officers, and causing grievous bodily harm. The accused has not yet entered a plea and is due back in court in August.

Prosecutors told the court that investigators are working through roughly 230,000 CCTV images and content from multiple devices, some requiring translation. Court documents allege the attack was planned over months, with the two shooters visiting the location two days before. A video from October reportedly shows them in front of an image linked to the Islamic State group.

The accused's father, who also fired on the crowd, was killed by police at the scene. The attack was described as Australia's worst mass shooting in nearly 30 years and prompted new gun laws and a royal commission into antisemitism.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Survivors and families of the 15 people killed in the Bondi Beach attack awaiting justice
events.alert_summary: A man accused of killing 15 people at a Sydney Jewish festival now faces 78 total charges after 19 new offences were filed.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kunar, Khost, and Paktika
events.body:
Pakistan launched air strikes against targets along the Afghanistan border, hitting sites in the provinces of Kunar, Khost, and Paktika. Pakistan's information minister said 26 militants were killed in strikes on four targets described as hideouts, a training centre, and an ammunition cache. The strikes came a day after an attack near Peshawar killed six Pakistani security officers.

Afghanistan's Taliban government said 13 people were killed — 11 children, one woman, and one elderly man — and 14 women and children were wounded. The Taliban denied that Afghan territory is being used to threaten neighboring countries.

The strikes broke months of relative calm following a ceasefire agreed last October. Earlier this year, clashes in late February left dozens dead, and a March air strike on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul killed at least 269 people. Civilians in border areas remain exposed to continued cross-border violence.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Children and families in Kunar, Khost, and Paktika caught in cross-border air strikes
events.alert_summary: Pakistani air strikes in three Afghan provinces killed at least 13 people, including 11 children, according to Taliban officials.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Cleveland, Johannesburg
events.body:
At least 10 armed men entered the Jumpers Informal Settlement in the Cleveland suburb of Johannesburg late Tuesday night, opening fire on residents at multiple locations before fleeing in a white vehicle. Eight men and three women died at the scene; a fourth man died later in hospital. Nine others were taken to medical facilities with gunshot wounds.

Police have launched a manhunt and say the motive is still under investigation. Residents believe the attack may be connected to a dispute between groups of illegal miners in the area. A provincial police official said investigators could not rule out a link to illegal mining, noting the settlement sits next to a known illegal mining zone.

Illegal mining has grown across South Africa, with groups targeting disused gold mines and selling ore on the black market. The trade has a history of violence, and the national government earlier this year deployed the military to help shut down some operations.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Twelve people killed and nine wounded in a Johannesburg informal settlement shooting
events.alert_summary: Armed gunmen killed 12 and wounded 9 residents at the Jumpers Informal Settlement in Cleveland, Johannesburg.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Cheboksary
events.body:
Ukrainian forces struck a military plant in Cheboksary, a city in Russia's Chuvash Republic about 900 kilometers from the front line. The targeted facility reportedly supplies components for drones and missiles used by Russian forces. Local officials confirmed three people were injured; it was not confirmed whether the plant itself was damaged.

In the same overnight period, Ukraine also reported hitting the port of Mariupol, an oil refinery in Samara, and an oil tanker in the Black Sea. Russia said its air defenses intercepted 326 Ukrainian drones across several regions. Ukraine's air force reported shooting down 181 of 207 Russian drones, while acknowledging 21 direct hits across 14 locations.

At least two people were killed and 26 wounded, including two children, across four Ukrainian regions in the past 24 hours from Russian drone strikes. Long-range missile strikes inside Russia remain relatively rare, though Ukraine has been increasing drone attacks on key Russian facilities in recent months.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine and Russia caught in overnight drone and missile exchanges, including two children among the wounded
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian missiles struck a drone and missile plant in Cheboksary, Russia, injuring 3; Russian drone strikes killed 2 and injured 26 in Ukraine.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tayr Debba
events.body:
Israeli airstrikes killed at least 17 people across southern Lebanon, Lebanese media reported. Nine of those deaths occurred in Tayr Debba, east of the port city of Tyre, where Israeli jets and drones carried out at least four strikes. Three more people died in nearby Deir Qanoun el-Nahr, two in Seddiqin, two in Sidon when a car was targeted by a drone, and one in the Massaken al-Shaabiya area of Tyre.

Hezbollah said its fighters responded with rocket and shellfire against Israeli troops in the southern Bayada and Yohmor areas. The group has been present in parts of southern Lebanon since Israeli forces moved in following the start of the conflict.

Lebanon's health ministry reports at least 3,696 people have been killed in Lebanon since the conflict began. A UN-brokered ceasefire reached on 16 April has not held. Close to one million people — about one-fifth of Lebanon's population — remain displaced, and 1.4 million need humanitarian aid, according to the UN.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon, nearly one million displaced, caught in ongoing airstrikes
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 17 people across several towns in southern Lebanon, with 9 dying in Tayr Debba alone.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Strait of Hormuz
events.body:
The United States and Iran continued trading strikes on Wednesday, a day after the US hit Iranian defense systems, radar sites, and ground control stations near the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian state media reported that two water reservoirs near the southern port town of Sirik were struck, leaving thousands of residents without drinking water. Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it launched strikes on 21 targets at US bases in Bahrain and Jordan; nearly all Iranian missiles and drones were intercepted, with no reported casualties.

At sea, the US military fired on a commercial vessel, the Settebello, in the Gulf of Oman, saying the ship was attempting to transport Iranian oil in violation of an ongoing blockade. Three Indian sailors remain missing and 21 crew members were rescued. The Indian government called for an end to attacks on commercial shipping.

The conflict began in late February after US and Israeli strikes on Iran. A two-week ceasefire agreed in April has largely broken down, with both sides exchanging fire intermittently since then.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Iran and sailors in the Gulf of Oman caught in ongoing US-Iran strikes
events.alert_summary: US and Iranian forces exchanged strikes for a second day; Iranian attacks on US bases were nearly all intercepted with no reported casualties.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mariupol
events.body:
Ukrainian forces are striking Russian military supply routes through occupied southern Ukraine, targeting the land corridor connecting occupied territory to the Crimean peninsula — roughly 160 kilometers long. Ukrainian drones have been conducting surveillance and attacks near Mariupol, and the military has published videos showing burned-out Russian military vehicles along the route. Civilians are reportedly no longer permitted to use the corridor.

Military analysts say Ukrainian drone technology, including AI-equipped drones from a domestic manufacturer, has made attacks more effective at medium range. However, experts caution that full control of the supply routes is still far off, and that Russia is expected to deploy mobile defense units and protective nets to counter the drone campaign.

Analysts note that forcing Russia to commit resources to defending the corridor is itself a strategic burden. The window of opportunity depends on surprise, which will diminish as Russian countermeasures develop. Attacks on fuel stations and repair facilities along the route could further disrupt logistics for Russian forces stationed in the south.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Civilians in occupied southern Ukraine living under ongoing drone strikes and supply route attacks
events.alert_summary: Ukrainian forces are attacking Russian military supply routes through occupied southern Ukraine toward Crimea using AI-equipped drones.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Strait of Hormuz
events.body:
A US Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz in the early hours of Tuesday after an Iranian drone struck it. Both crew members were rescued uninjured from the water by an uncrewed surface vessel — the first known rescue of its kind — and were later picked up by a second helicopter. The US Central Command described the incident as an act of Iranian aggression and launched what it called proportional self-defence strikes in response.

US strikes hit several locations along Iran's southern coast, including Sirik, Jask, Minab, Qeshm Island, and the port of Bandar Abbas. The US military said it targeted communications and radar sites. Iranian officials said civilian infrastructure, including water facilities, was also damaged.

A ceasefire brokered by Pakistan in April remains nominally in place. Both governments have signalled they do not want a return to full-scale conflict, but the exchange shows how quickly the truce can break down. Civilians in the affected coastal areas face continued uncertainty as both sides weigh their next moves.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Two US aviators and civilians near Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island caught in US-Iran strikes
events.alert_summary: A US Army Apache helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz after an Iranian drone strike, prompting US retaliatory strikes on multiple Iranian coastal sites.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Muzaffarabad
events.body:
A military helicopter went down near Muzaffarabad, the capital of Pakistan-administered Kashmir, killing everyone on board. The army's media wing said the Mi-17 aircraft crashed during takeoff because of a technical fault, and a board of inquiry has been ordered to find the exact cause. No survivor count was given.

The crash comes during a stretch of unrest in the region. At least 11 people were killed during clashes between police and protesters in Rawalakot city on Sunday after a protest movement announced demonstrations following a government ban on the group under antiterror laws.

In response, authorities have deployed federal paramilitary troops across Pakistan-administered Kashmir and issued a strict travel advisory for the area. A heavy security presence remains in Muzaffarabad.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Military personnel and civilians in Pakistan-administered Kashmir amid a helicopter crash and ongoing unrest
events.alert_summary: A military helicopter crashed near Muzaffarabad, killing all on board, during a period of unrest that has already left 11 dead.

events.title: Displacement in West Bank
events.body:
A new Amnesty International report says the displacement of Palestinians from the occupied West Bank is part of a deliberate government strategy, not the work of isolated individuals. According to the report, settler violence is "an integral part of an organized state policy" aimed at expanding settlements and annexing land in Area C, which makes up more than 60 percent of the West Bank and falls under full Israeli military and administrative control.

The report details one Bedouin village where residents faced repeated attacks on their homes, destruction of water supplies, and sewage pumped onto farmland. Israel's Supreme Court twice ordered authorities to protect returning residents, but those orders were not enforced. In April, far-right ministers distributed weapons to settlers in the Hebron area.

Israel's Security Cabinet approved 34 new settlements in a single session in April — the largest single-session approval recorded — bringing the total approved since late 2022 to 103. Attacks on Palestinian communities in the West Bank have increased since October 2023.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: About 5,910 Palestinians forced from homes in the West Bank amid ongoing settler attacks
events.alert_summary: Amnesty International reports settler attacks displaced roughly 5,910 Palestinians across at least 117 West Bank villages between 2023 and 2025.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sirik
events.body:
Fighting between the United States and Iran escalated after a U.S. Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday. The U.S. described its response as self-defense strikes, saying it targeted communications and radar facilities. The U.S. military said two pilots from the downed helicopter were safe and uninjured.

Iranian officials said civilian infrastructure was also hit. Two concrete water storage reservoirs in the Bamani district of Sirik County, in southern Iran's Hormozgan Province, were destroyed. Those reservoirs supplied drinking water to more than 20,000 people in the city of Kouhestak and ten surrounding villages. Estimated damage to the reservoirs runs between $780,000 and $830,000.

Iran launched retaliatory strikes on U.S. military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. The strikes come as Iran was already classified as facing extremely high water stress, with last year marking its fifth consecutive year of drought.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: More than 20,000 residents in Sirik and surrounding villages losing access to drinking water amid ongoing fighting
events.alert_summary: U.S. strikes on southern Iran hit two water reservoirs serving over 20,000 residents, with Iran retaliating against U.S. bases in the Gulf.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Strait of Hormuz
events.body:
US forces began striking targets inside Iran on Tuesday after an American helicopter was downed over the Strait of Hormuz. The US president said the strikes may continue, adding that Iran had missed its chance to reach a negotiated agreement. Iran responded by firing missiles at bases hosting US forces in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

Questions are now being raised about whether an April 8 ceasefire has collapsed. The US president also renewed threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges, though he stopped short of confirming those plans. Iranian officials said they will not sign any deal that fails to recognize their country's rights and signaled they are willing to return to open conflict if needed.

Iran has maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz throughout the fighting, pushing energy prices higher. Civilians and workers in the region face continued uncertainty as both sides signal the conflict is not over.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Iran, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan caught between ongoing US-Iran strikes and missile attacks
events.alert_summary: US strikes inside Iran continued after a helicopter was downed over the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran launched missiles at US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Fleurance
events.body:
An 11-year-old girl named Lyhanna was found dead at a farm near the town of Fleurance in south-west France after going missing for six days. A 41-year-old man — the father of one of her friends — was taken into custody three days after her disappearance. He has denied involvement but admitted driving her to a local swimming pool. Investigators have since learned the man had been named in multiple allegations of sexual abuse, including one filed nine months earlier by the mother of another young girl.

The case has sparked widespread anger because the suspect was never questioned in those nine months despite medical evidence supporting the earlier complaint. More than 60,000 people joined protests across France on Monday, many calling for the justice minister's resignation. Officials have acknowledged serious failures and ordered a review of around 70,000 unprocessed complaints involving sexual abuse of minors. Proposals to extend maximum sentences for serial rapists of children to life imprisonment are now moving through parliament.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: French families and child abuse survivors amid grief over an 11-year-old girl's murder and failures to act
events.alert_summary: More than 60,000 people protested across France after the murder of an 11-year-old girl whose alleged killer had been reported to police nine months earlier.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tyre
events.body:
Satellite images compared between January and June 2026 show large sections of Tyre, a coastal city in southern Lebanon, reduced to rubble. Residential blocks, historical quarters, and Palestinian camps have been bulldozed and levelled. Israel has established a 10-kilometer buffer zone along the border, and Lebanese residents have been barred from returning to areas within that zone. Tyre sits just 11 kilometers from that line.

At least six people were killed in strikes on the nearby town of Tayr Debba, one day after 20 people were killed and dozens wounded across southern Lebanon. A US-brokered ceasefire is reported to be in place.

The destruction has reached the edges of Tyre's UNESCO World Heritage archaeological site, with strikes recorded just meters from protected structures. Educational institutions and religious sites have also been hit. Lebanon's Ministry of Culture has condemned the attacks, calling the protection of the ancient city a global obligation under international law.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Tyre and southern Lebanon facing ongoing airstrikes, displacement, and destruction of their communities
events.alert_summary: Israeli airstrikes have killed at least 3,600 people and displaced 1.2 million across southern Lebanon since March 2, with satellite images confirming widespread demolition of civilian and historical areas in Tyre.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sudan
events.body:
Drone strikes on bridges, roads, and other civilian infrastructure across Sudan are blocking the delivery of humanitarian aid, the United Nations warned. The attacks are cutting off supply routes that aid organizations depend on to reach people in need.

The UN said the damage is putting civilians at further risk in a country already experiencing one of the world's most serious humanitarian crises. Aid workers are struggling to find alternate ways to reach affected communities.

No specific casualty figures were reported in connection with the infrastructure strikes, but the disruption leaves many people without access to food, medicine, and other essential supplies.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians in Sudan cut off from aid as drone strikes destroy roads and bridges
events.alert_summary: Drone attacks damaged bridges and roads in Sudan, disrupting humanitarian aid routes, the UN reported.

events.title: Displacement in Gaza
events.body:
Gaza's Health Ministry says more than 16,500 Palestinians requiring medical treatment abroad have been prevented from leaving the territory, despite a ceasefire nominally in place since October. Officials say delays are costing lives, as most of Gaza's healthcare infrastructure has been destroyed and cannot treat patients locally.

The Rafah crossing between Gaza and Egypt was partially reopened in February after nearly two years of closure, but has been temporarily shut several times since. Authorities say Israel allows medical evacuations only three days a week through Rafah and one day a week through the Karem Abu Salem crossing. Officials called on human rights organizations to pressure Israel to allow free movement of patients.

The UN Secretary-General on Tuesday called on Israel to reopen all crossings and allow humanitarian aid to enter without restriction. A joint UN and EU assessment found human development in Gaza had been set back 77 years, with more than $71 billion needed for recovery. Almost 90 percent of Gaza's population remains displaced, with most experiencing food insecurity.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: 16,500 Palestinians in Gaza unable to reach medical treatment while healthcare infrastructure remains destroyed
events.alert_summary: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel is blocking 16,500 patients from leaving for medical care, with 73,000 killed since October 2023.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Pakistan-administered Kashmir
events.body:
Protests have broken out in Pakistan-administered Kashmir over a rule that reserves legislative seats for refugees from India-administered Kashmir who live in Pakistan. Demonstrators say this arrangement gives those refugees outsized political influence in the region. The government has responded that changing the rule would require constitutional reform.

The protests are being led by a group called the Joint Awami Action Committee. Authorities have accused the group of stoking unrest. The dispute has roots in long-running political debates about representation and governance in the divided Kashmir region.

At least 11 people were killed in clashes between protesters and police. The deaths have raised concerns about whether tensions will continue to grow as the two sides remain at odds over the contested policy.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: At least 11 people killed and families affected by clashes in Pakistan-administered Kashmir
events.alert_summary: Clashes between protesters and police killed at least 11 people in Pakistan-administered Kashmir.

events.title: Civil Unrest in western Afghanistan
events.body:
Authorities in western Afghanistan arrested and detained more than a dozen women for violating dress code rules. When residents gathered to protest the detentions, police opened fire on the crowd. Eyewitnesses reported at least three people were wounded.

The protest reflects ongoing tension between the population and authorities over enforcement of dress regulations for women. Those detained had reportedly been held for not complying with required dress standards.

The situation raises concern for civilians in the area, particularly women at risk of detention and those who gathered to speak out on their behalf.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Protesters wounded and women detained in western Afghanistan over dress code enforcement
events.alert_summary: Police opened fire on protesters in western Afghanistan, injuring at least 3 people.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Belfast
events.body:
A night of unrest in Belfast left multiple houses burned and families displaced, including a Ukrainian family and a man who lost his home of 13 years on Lendrick Street in east Belfast. The violence followed a knife attack the night before. Cars belonging to foreign nationals were set alight, and at least one neighboring home caught fire as a result.

Residents described the scenes as terrifying. One family with a two-month-old baby was forced to flee. A 19-year-old Ukrainian woman escaped through her back door while her neighbor's dog remained trapped. An Indian resident of north Belfast said he was leaving Northern Ireland immediately after the violence, describing the streets as "like a war zone."

Healthcare workers from immigrant communities expressed fear that colleagues may leave Northern Ireland over safety concerns. A Turkish-owned barbershop was also reported among the businesses targeted during the disorder.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Immigrant families and longtime residents in Belfast whose homes were burned or who fled in fear
events.alert_summary: Fires and disorder in Belfast destroyed homes and forced families to flee, following a knife attack the previous night.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Strait of Hormuz
events.body:
The United States carried out a second day of strikes on targets inside Iran, hitting air defence, radar, and other sites near the Strait of Hormuz. Iran responded by attacking US military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait — the same targets struck the day before — and Iranian state media reported the Strait of Hormuz was closed to all vessels, though US Central Command said commercial ships were still transiting the waterway.

Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they struck two oil tankers passing through the strait, though no immediate confirmation was available. Oil prices rose sharply after the closure announcement, with benchmark crude climbing above $95 a barrel.

The exchanges have strained a ceasefire agreed between the two countries in April. A US helicopter was also downed earlier in the week in an attack blamed on Iran. The UN Secretary-General warned the region was being "pulled deeper into crisis," saying the ceasefire had become "more like a lesser-fire" and calling on all parties to stop attacks and pursue a diplomatic settlement.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians and shipping crews caught between ongoing US and Iranian strikes across the Gulf region
events.alert_summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes for a second consecutive day; Iran attacked US bases in Bahrain and Kuwait and claimed to hit two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bolivia
events.body:
Bolivia's president has authorized the use of military force against protesters following weeks of unrest described as the country's worst economic crisis in 40 years. Roadblocks set up by demonstrators have brought movement across the nation to a standstill, cutting off communities and disrupting supply chains.

At least 10 people have been killed since the protests began. The decision to deploy the military marks a sharp escalation in the government's response to what had been largely civilian-led demonstrations.

Residents in affected areas face disrupted access to goods and services due to the roadblocks. With military forces now authorized to act against protesters, the risk of further casualties remains a serious concern for communities across the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Bolivian civilians caught in nationwide protests and military crackdown, at least 10 killed
events.alert_summary: Bolivia's president authorized military force against protesters after roadblocks paralyzed the country, killing at least 10 people.

events.title: Civil Unrest in West Bank
events.body:
A United Nations report states that Israeli security forces routinely accompany settlers and act as a shield during attacks on Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. The report describes a pattern of security forces present during these incidents rather than intervening to stop them.

Israeli officials rejected the findings, saying the report relied on unsubstantiated allegations.

The report's conclusions raise ongoing concerns for Palestinian civilians living in West Bank communities, where such incidents affect daily movement and safety.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank facing settler attacks and security force presence
events.alert_summary: A UN report says Israeli security forces routinely accompany settlers during attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank.

events.title: Armed Conflict in Crimea
events.body:
Ukraine has carried out hundreds of drone strikes on trucks and supply roads in Russian-occupied southern Ukraine, targeting a key highway linking the Russian city of Rostov to Crimea through the port city of Mariupol. According to Ukraine's drone forces commander, military cargo traffic on that road dropped by 71% between late May and early June. Analysts describe the road as the backbone of Russian control in the south.

The campaign has triggered a severe fuel shortage across Crimea. Residents report waiting up to ten hours in line at petrol stations, and most stations now limit purchases to 20 litres per person using prepaid vouchers — when fuel is available at all. Hundreds of buses have been grounded, and tourists who traveled to Crimea before the crisis began are struggling to find fuel to leave.

Sea and bridge routes to the peninsula have also been restricted by previous Ukrainian strikes. Russia's energy ministry publicly acknowledged the supply difficulties for the first time on June 8, noting that attacks on fuel infrastructure had caused what it called "temporary difficulties."
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Crimea facing fuel shortages and disrupted daily life amid ongoing drone strikes

events.title: Civil Unrest in Herat
events.body:
A rare protest broke out in the western Afghan city of Herat after authorities began arresting women accused of wearing the hijab improperly. Both men and women joined the demonstration in the streets, witnesses said.

Taliban police moved to break up the crowd, and multiple witnesses reported that officers used sticks, whips, and firearms against protesters. Medics told the BBC that two people died, though details were not given. A number of others were injured. Police acknowledged responding to the protest but denied any deaths occurred, saying officers acted to restore public order. The BBC could not independently verify all accounts, though gunfire is audible in videos circulating online.

The UN's special rapporteur on human rights in Afghanistan said he was alarmed by what he described as excessive force against seemingly peaceful protesters. Residents say markets in Herat have since emptied, and women report that morality police are stopping vehicles to check whether passengers are dressed in compliance with Taliban rules.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Women and protesters in Herat facing violent crackdown over dress code enforcement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Libya
events.body:
More than 300 young men from Iraqi Kurdistan were kidnapped by a militia in Libya last summer while attempting to reach the United Kingdom. The militia held them in a guarded compound, cramping nearly 180 people into a single cell, and demanded $5,000 from each family as ransom. Families who could not pay quickly were threatened that the debt would be collected "with a kidney." At least one hostage is known to have died.

Some of those released have shown evidence of torture, including burn scars. Several returned with surgical-looking scars that families and Kurdish authorities suspect may be from forced organ removal, though this has not been confirmed. A UK medical consultant who reviewed one photo said the scars were consistent with kidney surgery.

The men had paid a smuggling network to guide them through Libya toward Europe. A dispute between the smuggler and the militia over payment is believed to have triggered the kidnappings. Around 110 hostages were flown home to Iraqi Kurdistan in January on a plane arranged by the Iraqi government. It remains unclear how many are still being held.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Over 300 young Kurdish men kidnapped, tortured, and threatened with forced organ removal in Libya

events.title: Civil Unrest in Gauteng
events.body:
South Africa is facing a wave of anti-migrant protests, with foreign nationals caught in street confrontations and some being repatriated to their home countries. One young Ghanaian footballer on holiday in the country was among a group recently sent back to Ghana after being caught up in the unrest. An estimated 3.3 million immigrants live in South Africa, with many settling in Gauteng and the Western Cape.

President Cyril Ramaphosa announced new measures to tighten border security, crack down on undocumented migrants, and curb corruption in the immigration system. He also warned that no one has the right to stop people in the street and demand proof of nationality. Authorities say they have deported more than 100,000 undocumented migrants over the past two years.

Anti-migrant groups say the plan is unworkable and that the government is not listening to South Africans' concerns. With over 4,400 kilometers of land border and many unofficial crossing points, enforcing the new measures will be difficult.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Migrants and foreign nationals in South Africa caught in rising anti-foreigner violence and uncertainty

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kharkiv region
events.body:
Russian strikes overnight killed five people across Ukraine's northeastern Kharkiv region and the Donetsk region. A pregnant woman and two others died in the Kharkiv region, with missile attacks on the town of Chuhuiv injuring six more and damaging homes and shops. In the city of Kharkiv, a drone attack wounded 16 people, including children. Two more people were killed in the towns of Bilozerske and Druzhkivka in Donetsk, with 11 others injured nearby.

The attacks come amid an exchange of strikes, with Ukraine targeting Russian oil facilities using long-range drones, causing fuel shortages inside Russia, while Russia has continued large air attacks on Ukrainian cities and towns.

Ukraine's president described recent phone calls with US envoys as "very positive" and said diplomacy aimed at ending the war remains a priority. European leaders reaffirmed support for Ukraine, and discussions are ongoing ahead of upcoming G7, NATO, and Coalition of the Willing summits. Direct talks between the two countries' leaders have not yet taken place.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Kharkiv and Donetsk regions wounded and killed in overnight missile and drone strikes

events.title: Civil Unrest in Pretoria
events.body:
Nigeria has begun flying hundreds of its citizens home from South Africa after a new wave of violent anti-immigration protests left at least two Nigerians dead in April. One man was beaten to death by South African military personnel, and another was found dead in a mortuary after being arrested in Pretoria. Nigeria's foreign minister says Nigerians with legal status are being harassed, their businesses looted, and their children kept from school.

Diplomatic relations between the two countries have sharply deteriorated. Nigeria's government accused South Africa of failing to strongly condemn the violence, and threatened retaliatory measures. At least 1,000 Nigerians were initially scheduled for repatriation, with more requesting to return.

South Africa's president addressed the nation, announcing tougher penalties for employers who hire undocumented workers, dedicated deportation courts, and a national biometric register. He also warned citizens not to take immigration enforcement into their own hands. South Africa has experienced three waves of violent anti-immigration unrest since 2008, with migrants from several African countries affected each time.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Nigerian nationals in South Africa facing violence, harassment, and forced displacement amid anti-immigrant unrest

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sde Teiman
events.body:
Former Palestinian detainees, including people from Gaza, the West Bank, and Jerusalem, have described severe abuse during imprisonment in Israeli detention facilities. Accounts gathered by Al Jazeera for an investigative documentary include allegations of stripping, blindfolding, binding of hands and legs, starvation, sleep deprivation, and sexual assault — including assaults involving dogs. Multiple former detainees say the pattern repeated across several facilities, including Sde Teiman, Ofer, Negev, and Ashkelon.

One family in Hebron describes Israeli forces entering their home in July 2023 and ordering women to undress and walk naked in front of soldiers. A former detainee arrested at age 17 from Jenin says he was held for five months. Another from Jerusalem describes humiliation as routine.

As of April 2026, a Palestinian human rights group reported roughly 9,600 Palestinians held in Israeli custody, including over 3,500 under detention without charge or trial, along with 342 children and 84 women.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinian detainees — including children and women — describing systematic abuse, humiliation, and sexual violence in Israeli prisons

events.title: Civil Unrest in Nanyuki
events.body:
Protests in the Kenyan town of Nanyuki turned deadly again as demonstrators clashed with police over a planned US Ebola quarantine centre at a nearby military base. Witnesses told journalists that a protester was shot and killed, though police had not confirmed this at the time of reporting. The previous week, two people also died when police dispersed similar demonstrations. Officers used tear gas and running battles broke out in the streets.

The proposed 50-bed facility is intended to treat Americans affected by the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Kenya's High Court ordered construction halted after a rights group raised public health concerns, but satellite images show work has continued at the airbase regardless.

Many residents fear cross-border infection risk and say the government has not been open about the plans. Kenya has recorded no Ebola cases, and the nearest outbreak area in eastern Congo is nearly 800 kilometres away across Uganda.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Nanyuki and Laikipia county amid deadly protest violence over proposed Ebola facility

events.title: Civil Unrest in Nairobi National Park
events.body:
Police in Nairobi broke up a protest near Nairobi National Park on Monday, detaining ten demonstrators — including a former chief justice — who were marching against development plans on protected land. The group had gathered to present a petition opposing a proposed 1,300-vehicle car park and other construction within the 117-square-kilometer wildlife sanctuary. Protesters were filmed sitting in the middle of a road before officers dispersed the crowd and loaded them onto a lorry.

The former chief justice was later released but refused to leave the police station until the remaining activists were freed. Police have not issued an official statement about the arrests. The Kenya Wildlife Service has defended its plans to build an expanded animal orphanage within the park, saying it will benefit animal welfare and visitor experience, and insisting the public was consulted. The agency has not addressed the car park allegation directly.

Amnesty International, Greenpeace Africa, and several environmental groups jointly condemned the arrests, calling the police response a violent dispersal of peaceful assembly.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Peaceful protesters and conservationists in Nairobi facing arrests over national park dispute

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mahshahr
events.body:
Iran and Israel have both announced a halt to strikes against each other following a fresh exchange of fire. Israel's prime minister said his country was holding fire "at the moment" but warned the struggle was "not finished." Iran said it had stopped operations after delivering what it called a painful response, while threatening harsher action if Israel struck again, including in Lebanon.

The latest round of fighting included Israeli airstrikes on a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, in southwestern Iran, where officials said 14 people were injured. Iran launched missiles toward Jerusalem and other parts of Israel. In Lebanon, an Israeli strike on Tyre killed five people and wounded eight, including four Red Cross rescuers.

US officials said a phone call between the US president and Israel's prime minister helped bring the strikes to a pause, with the US pressing both sides to stop while nuclear negotiations continue. The broader conflict, which began in late February, has drawn in Lebanon and disrupted regional shipping routes.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Iran, Israel, and Lebanon caught in ongoing missile strikes and airstrikes as both sides warn of further escalation

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tel Aviv
events.body:
A day of missile exchanges between Israel and Iran prompted both sides to announce a halt to strikes. Residents in Tel Aviv reacted with mixed feelings — some wanted a stronger response against Iran, while others felt their side had come out worse from the episode.

Those expressing frustration pointed to disruptions to daily life, closed schools, and damage to tourism as signs that ordinary Israelis were bearing the real cost of the confrontation. The ceasing of strikes brought a pause to the immediate danger, but uncertainty remained among the population about what comes next.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Tel Aviv residents enduring missile exchanges and disruptions to daily life, schools, and tourism

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nuseirat refugee camp
events.body:
Survivors from Nuseirat refugee camp in Gaza are speaking out about an Israeli military raid that took place in June 2024. The operation was aimed at freeing four captives held in the camp. At least 274 Palestinians were killed during the raid, which involved disguised forces and air strikes.

Witnesses describe watching family members killed in front of them, with some residents saying they were threatened during the assault. The accounts were gathered two years after the events took place.

The raid remains one of the deadliest single incidents reported in Gaza during the ongoing conflict, and survivors continue to live with its impact.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in Nuseirat refugee camp who lost loved ones in the June 2024 raid

events.title: Armed Conflict in northern Israel
events.body:
A serious flare-up between Israel and Iran began on Sunday when Israeli forces carried out a deadly bombardment of Beirut, Lebanon's capital. Iran responded with a wave of missiles aimed at northern Israel. Despite a U.S. request to hold back, Israel launched strikes early Monday on Iranian air defense systems and a petrochemical facility. Iran then hit a similar facility in the Israeli port city of Haifa and targeted two Israeli airbases. Many missiles were intercepted over the occupied West Bank. No deaths were reported on either side.

The exchanges strained a ceasefire that had paused major fighting since April. The U.S. president publicly called on both sides to stop and reportedly warned Israel's leader that continued attacks could leave Israel without American support.

Diplomatic contacts are continuing through Pakistan as an intermediary. Iranian officials said they remain at the negotiating table, and Pakistan's prime minister called for restraint as talks move toward a possible agreement.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in northern Israel and Lebanon caught in missile exchanges as fragile ceasefire fractures

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tyre
events.body:
Israeli forces carried out air and artillery strikes across southern Lebanon, killing at least 13 people on Tuesday. Eight people were killed and 32 injured in the southern city of Tyre, where strikes hit several buildings and rescuers were still searching rubble for victims. Two more people were killed in a pre-dawn drone strike near the major town of Nabatieh.

The Israeli military ordered residents of Tyre and surrounding areas to leave immediately and move north of the Zahrani River. For the first time, the order included the city's Christian quarter, which tens of thousands of people fled, causing heavy traffic on roads heading north. The military said it was acting because of ceasefire violations and attacks on northern Israel.

Hezbollah said its fighters launched rockets and drones at Israeli military positions. Iran warned it could resume direct strikes on Israel if attacks on Lebanon did not stop, but Israel said it would continue its campaign. Lebanon's health ministry says at least 3,666 people have been killed in the country since the conflict began.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Tyre, including the Christian quarter, fleeing airstrikes and evacuation orders in southern Lebanon

events.title: Mindanao
events.body:
A magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck off the coast of Mindanao island in the southern Philippines early Monday morning, killing at least 35 people and injuring more than 134 others. Around 10,000 families have been displaced. Buildings collapsed and landslides were recorded across affected areas. The quake coincided with the first day of the school year, and videos showed terrified children at schools during the shaking.

The hardest-hit area was the Soccsksargen region, which recorded 31 of the deaths. The remaining four fatalities were in Davao. Twelve people were reported missing. More than 130 aftershocks followed, including one measuring 6.7. The earthquake also triggered tsunami warnings across the Philippines, Indonesia, Japan, and Australia, though these were later cancelled or downgraded after small waves — the largest measuring 1.4 meters — were detected along nearby coastlines.

Mindanao is home to around 26 million people. Authorities are still verifying the full casualty count, and the national disaster agency is expected to release an official update in the coming days.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Tens of thousands of families in Mindanao displaced and mourning after a devastating earthquake

events.title: Armed Conflict in Dahieh, Beirut
events.body:
Iran launched roughly 30 ballistic missiles at targets in northern Israel, including an airbase near Nazareth, after Israel struck a Hezbollah command center in Beirut's southern suburbs. The strike in Beirut killed two people and injured 20. Israel responded with two waves of airstrikes inside Iran. Both sides later announced they had halted strikes on each other, with an Israeli official saying the pause came at the US president's request.

The exchange broke a ceasefire that had been in place since early April. Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon continued despite the pause, and a separate Lebanon ceasefire deal collapsed after Hezbollah rejected its terms and demanded a full Israeli withdrawal. The US president publicly called on both Iran and Israel to stop shooting, saying final negotiations toward a broader peace deal were still moving forward.

Civilians in Lebanon's south and in northern Israel remain exposed to ongoing fire even as diplomacy continues.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Israel, Lebanon, and Iran caught in escalating missile strikes and airstrikes

events.title: Armed Conflict in Itamar, West Bank
events.body:
Iran launched missiles at Israel, and at least one struck near the settlement of Itamar in the northern occupied West Bank. Residents filmed a crater left by the impact, and Israeli media reported that a house was damaged. Smoke was seen rising from the area following the strike.

No casualties were mentioned in the available reporting, but the physical damage points to a direct hit in a populated area.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents in the northern West Bank caught in missile strike damage

events.title: Civil Unrest in Rawalakot
events.body:
At least 11 people were killed and more than 70 injured during clashes between police and protesters in Rawalakot, the capital of Poonch district in Pakistan-administered Kashmir. Four police officers and a bystander were killed after protesters opened fire, while six protesters also died. The unrest began before a major demonstration organized by a civil society umbrella group that local authorities had recently banned under anti-terrorism legislation.

The group, which represents traders and civil society organizations, has led protest movements in the region since 2023. The current protests center on a dispute over reserved legislative seats for refugees from Indian-administered Kashmir who now live elsewhere in Pakistan. Authorities deployed federal paramilitary troops, issued travel advisories urging visitors to leave the area, and internet access remained severely restricted for at least three days, according to a monitoring group.

Pakistan-administered Kashmir is a semi-autonomous region of more than four million people governed under its own assembly but ultimately under Islamabad's authority.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Pakistan-administered Kashmir amid deadly clashes between protesters and security forces

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tehran
events.body:
Israel launched overnight airstrikes on multiple cities in Iran, including Tehran, Tabriz, Karaj, and Isfahan — the most serious escalation between the two countries since a fragile ceasefire took hold in April. The strikes came hours after Iran fired ballistic missiles toward northern Israel, which Tehran said was retaliation for Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs that killed at least two people and wounded 20. Most of the Iranian missiles were intercepted, though debris fell as far as Jordan and the West Bank.

Iran has launched roughly 30 ballistic missiles toward Israel since Sunday night, according to Israeli media. Missiles were also fired from Yemen, and Hezbollah remained engaged against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, where Israel has occupied about one-fifth of the country. Saudi Arabia issued a shelter-in-place alert in one of its provinces before officials disputed reports of an Iranian strike there.

The US president called on both sides to stop fighting and insisted Washington holds decision-making authority over any final agreement. Whether the gap between US and Israeli positions reflects a genuine split remains unclear. The April ceasefire technically remains in place, though both sides continue testing its limits.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Iran, Israel, Lebanon, and surrounding regions caught in escalating missile exchanges

events.title: Civil Unrest in Ghezel Hesar
events.body:
Since a conflict began in late February 2026, rights groups say Iran has sharply increased the use of the death penalty against people accused of political and security-related offenses. At least 40 people have been executed in political or security cases since the war started, and at least 78 more remain on death row. In the six weeks leading up to the end of April, one political execution was recorded roughly every two days.

More than 6,000 people have been arrested since the war began, including protesters, journalists, lawyers, teachers, artists, and members of ethnic and religious minorities. Amnesty International reports that Iran executed at least 2,159 people in 2025 alone — more than double the previous year — accounting for 80% of a worldwide rise in executions. The group says documented patterns of killings, torture, enforced disappearances, and mass arrests could amount to crimes against humanity.

Prisoners have described preparing for execution. Amnesty International has documented torture practices including mock hangings, severe beatings, and prolonged solitary confinement. Trials have reportedly lasted as little as ten minutes, with no independent legal representation.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Prisoners, protesters, journalists, and minorities in Iran facing executions and torture amid wartime crackdown

events.title: Armed Conflict in Zémio
events.body:
Night fell over Zémio, a town in eastern Central African Republic, as the country prepared for its December 2025 presidential election. Fighters from the "Azande Ani Kpi Gbe" (AAKG) militia launched an offensive aimed at seizing the town and disrupting the vote.

UN peacekeepers were present on the ground as the attack unfolded, working to hold back the armed group and protect the electoral process. The situation placed both civilians and peacekeeping personnel in direct danger during what should have been a democratic milestone for the country.

Elections in the Central African Republic have long been complicated by the presence of multiple armed groups operating across its territory, and Zémio's experience during this vote reflects the difficult conditions many communities face when trying to participate in civic life.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Residents of Zémio caught in militia fighting during a national election

events.title: Civil Unrest in San Julian
events.body:
Bolivia's legislature has passed a law giving the president authority to deploy soldiers to clear roadblocks set up by antigovernment protesters. The measure passed the Chamber of Deputies on Sunday after an overnight debate, following earlier Senate approval. It grants soldiers the power to use force and a "presumption of legality" in conflict situations, meaning their actions will be considered lawful unless proven otherwise. This came after lawmakers last month repealed a 2020 law that had restricted military use against protests.

Farmers, miners, and transportation unions have led weeks of demonstrations calling for the president to resign over rising inflation, low wages, and the removal of fuel subsidies. About 100 roadblocks have been reported across the country, which authorities say have caused shortages of food and medicine.

On Saturday, riot police backed by military vehicles fired tear gas while trying to clear a road in San Julian. Protesters threw stones and burned tires in response. The new law signals that confrontations between security forces and demonstrators could intensify in the days ahead.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Bolivian civilians caught between ongoing protests, roadblocks, and the threat of military force

events.title: Displacement near Malta
events.body:
A boat carrying nearly 60 refugees and migrants capsized about 83 kilometers east-southeast of Malta after departing Libya. A fishing boat rescued 48 survivors from the water, while Italian coastguard crews recovered 10 bodies. Search operations for remaining missing persons were continuing, coordinated by Maltese authorities.

The UN's International Organization for Migration reports that at least 990 people have died crossing the Mediterranean this year, making the early part of this year among the deadliest since 2014. At least 2,180 people died or went missing on the same route last year.

The incident comes as EU member states are discussing a proposal to establish deportation centers outside the bloc for rejected asylum seekers, though the plan still needs formal approval. Separately, hundreds of people protested outside the UN refugee agency headquarters in Tripoli, Libya, this week, expressing opposition to the resettlement of undocumented migrants in the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Dozens of refugees and migrants drowned or missing in Mediterranean waters near Malta

events.title: Armed Conflict in Beirut
events.body:
Iran launched a new wave of missile attacks on Israel, with videos showing projectiles over Israeli territory. The Israeli military confirmed the strikes. The escalation followed an Israeli strike on Beirut that was described as deadly.

Iran warned that the attack would lead it to abandon ongoing negotiations and return to what it called "direct confrontation." The sequence of strikes between Israel and Iran, with Beirut also struck, marks a sharp intensification of hostilities across the region.

Civilians in Israel and Lebanon face immediate danger from the exchanges, and the breakdown of any diplomatic track raises concerns about further escalation in the days ahead.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Israel, Lebanon, and the wider region caught in escalating missile exchanges

events.title: Armed Conflict in Ramat David airbase
events.body:
Iran launched a barrage of ballistic missiles toward Israel late Sunday, targeting a military airbase in northern Israel. The Israeli military said it intercepted all incoming missiles, and residents sheltering in protected spaces were told they could leave about an hour after the attack began. No casualties from the missile barrage were reported.

Iran's Revolutionary Guard described the attack as a direct response to Israeli strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut, which it said had killed and displaced large numbers of civilians in the Tyre and Nabatieh regions of southern Lebanon. The Guard warned that any further Israeli action would bring broader strikes against targets across the region.

The exchange came just days after Israel and Lebanon agreed to a ceasefire. Israeli strikes on a densely populated neighborhood in Beirut on Sunday killed at least two people and injured eleven more before the Iranian response. The United States urged Israel not to retaliate, with officials suggesting Washington would not back a new Israeli strike on Iran, leaving the situation deeply uncertain for civilians across the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Israel, Lebanon, and the wider region amid escalating missile strikes and ongoing urban bombardment

events.title: Armed Conflict in northern Israel
events.body:
Iran launched missiles toward Israel, with footage released by Iranian media showing the launches. Separately, videos captured missiles striking areas in northern Israel.

Iranian officials described the attack as a response to an Israeli strike on Beirut, which Iran says violated a ceasefire in Lebanon brokered by the United States.

Residents in affected areas of northern Israel face immediate danger from the incoming strikes. The exchange marks a significant escalation, with strikes now crossing international borders and drawing in multiple countries across the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in northern Israel under incoming missile fire, and communities across the region amid escalating strikes

events.title: Armed Conflict in Natanz, Bandar Abbas, Siri Island, Naqoura, Bint Jbeil
events.body:
Satellite images released by Al Jazeera's Open Source Unit document damage across at least 15 sites in Iran and Lebanon following months of US and Israeli strikes. The images, captured before a US-requested blackout on commercial satellite photography took effect, show destruction at Iranian nuclear facilities, oil terminals, naval bases, and air bases, as well as towns in southern Lebanon.

In Iran, the Natanz uranium enrichment complex was struck twice in June 2025. Oil storage facilities on Siri Island were hit, including a tank holding one million barrels. The Bandar Abbas naval and port complex shows damage at 11 locations, and a warship docked there suffered direct hits. An air base near Tehran used by drone and helicopter units shows collapsed hangars and burn marks.

In Lebanon, more than 100 buildings were destroyed around Naqoura, which hosts a UN peacekeeping headquarters. The town of Bint Jbeil saw roughly 725 buildings damaged or destroyed through ground and air operations. Satellite imagery captured before commercial blackout restrictions took hold provided the primary record of this damage.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Iran and Lebanon amid widespread destruction of military, port, and residential areas

events.title: Armed Conflict in Mogadishu
events.body:
Somalia's nearly two-decade Islamist insurgency pulled thousands of young people into combat, and many who survived are still living with the damage. One man, now 34, shared with BBC News what it was like to be recruited as a teenager during the Ethiopian military intervention that began in late 2006. He describes night shelling in civilian neighborhoods, watching a girl his age die under rubble, and then taking up arms himself by age 16 — fighting street by street through Mogadishu.

He joined a resistance coalition called the Muqawama, trained in small arms and hit-and-run tactics, and was given a gun but no pay. Some of the soldiers he faced were also teenagers. The fighting, he says, left marks that never healed.

Mogadishu has physically rebuilt over the years, but mental health resources for former fighters and civilians who lived through the conflict remain very limited. Many people carry those years silently, without access to support.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Former child soldiers in Mogadishu carrying psychological wounds from years of urban warfare

events.title: Armed Conflict in St Petersburg
events.body:
Ukrainian drones struck the St Petersburg area on the opening and closing days of Russia's International Economic Forum this week. A large plume of black smoke rose over the city skyline, visible to all attendees arriving at the venue. Local officials acknowledged that drones had damaged "infrastructure" but gave no details on what was hit.

The strikes came as Ukraine's president publicly proposed direct peace talks with Russia's leader in a neutral country. Russia's president dismissed the offer, criticized its tone, and told his soldiers to "keep at it."

Inside the forum, officials and business leaders heard reassurances that Russia's economy remains stable, but Russian economists describe stagnation and decline in many sectors. High interest rates, war spending, and ongoing sanctions are straining growth. Small business owners in at least one region say they are struggling to stay afloat. Long-range drone attacks now regularly reach deep inside Russian territory, adding to the pressure on ordinary life.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in St Petersburg and surrounding regions caught between drone strikes and a grinding wartime economy

events.title: Armed Conflict in Dahieh district, Beirut
events.body:
Israel carried out two airstrikes on apartment buildings in the Dahieh district of southern Beirut, killing two people and injuring at least 17. The attacks tore open the lower floors of a residential building, scattering concrete and metal across the street. Crowds rushed to the scene to help the wounded.

Israel said the strikes targeted Hezbollah infrastructure and were carried out in response to projectiles fired into Israeli territory from Lebanon, though Hezbollah has not admitted launching them. An Israeli military spokesman suggested further strikes could follow.

The attack is the third on Beirut since a ceasefire took effect on 17 April, which both sides have repeatedly violated. A separate US-brokered truce was announced just days earlier. Iran's parliament warned of a "decisive and painful response." Lebanon's parliament speaker has already rejected the US-brokered deal, calling it a trap, while Hezbollah says disarming is off the table, leaving civilians caught between ongoing military exchanges and stalled diplomacy.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Beirut's Dahieh district wounded and displaced by fresh airstrikes amid a fragile ceasefire

events.title: Armed Conflict in Chornobyl
events.body:
A drone strike on Sunday significantly damaged a fuel-reception building near Ukraine's Chornobyl nuclear power plant, just metres from where large amounts of nuclear material are stored. Radiation monitors showed no spike above normal background levels, and a fire at the site was put out with no injuries reported. An international nuclear watchdog said a team would visit soon to inspect the damage.

The same day, at least four people were killed in separate aerial attacks elsewhere in Ukraine. Two people died when a guided bomb hit a bus stop in the southern Zaporizhia region, a minibus driver was killed nearby in a drone strike, and a man died in the central Dnipropetrovsk region as drones and bombs struck two districts. Ukraine's air force said Russia launched 236 drones overnight, intercepting 215 of them.

The strikes came as European leaders gathered in London to discuss the ongoing war, now in its fourth year.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians across Ukraine facing ongoing drone and missile strikes, including near nuclear sites

events.title: Armed Conflict in international waters near Cyprus
events.body:
Israeli naval forces intercepted a fleet of more than 50 sailboats in international waters near Cyprus on May 18, detaining 428 activists from more than 45 countries. The vessels were part of the Global Sumud Flotilla, which had departed from Marmaris, Türkiye, on May 14, carrying activists in a symbolic show of solidarity with people in Gaza and challenging what organizers called Israel's naval blockade.

Those aboard the intercepted boats were transferred to two Israeli military landing craft converted for the operation.

The total detention lasted more than 50 hours.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: 428 activists from more than 45 countries detained and reportedly beaten aboard Israeli military vessels

events.title: Armed Conflict in Khan Younis
events.body:
An Israeli strike destroyed a home and damaged nearby tents housing displaced people in Khan Younis. Residents inspected the destruction afterward, with some questioning the value of a ceasefire that remains officially in effect but has not stopped the violence.

The strike occurred while Egypt was hosting a new round of ceasefire talks. Israeli attacks have been intensifying across Gaza even as the formal ceasefire agreement stays on paper. Many displaced people in the area had been sheltering in temporary tents, which were among the structures hit.

Civilians in the area are caught between an ongoing military campaign and stalled negotiations, with little relief in sight for those living in makeshift shelters.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Displaced Palestinians in Khan Younis sheltering in tents amid ongoing strikes during a nominal ceasefire

events.title: Armed Conflict in Beirut
events.body:
Israel carried out airstrikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut, with multiple explosions reported. Videos circulating online showed the aftermath of the attack. Israeli officials said the strikes targeted Hezbollah headquarters, while Lebanese media reported that residential apartment buildings were hit.

The attack came just days after the United States had signaled to Israeli leadership that Beirut should be off limits while Washington pursues a diplomatic agreement with Iran.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents in Beirut's southern suburbs caught in airstrikes on residential neighborhoods

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Fighting continued in southern Lebanon on June 5, with Hizbollah reporting around 20 attacks on Israeli troops in the area. Israeli military figures stated that more than 650 Hizbollah targets had been struck and over 125 fighters killed during the previous week alone.

Lebanon's president publicly warned that Iran was treating Lebanon as a bargaining chip in its negotiations with the United States, calling the situation unacceptable. The statement points to outside pressures shaping conditions on the ground in ways that Lebanese leaders say are beyond their control.

The combination of active fighting and competing international interests leaves communities in southern Lebanon exposed to continued danger.
events.source_title: International Crisis Group
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon caught in ongoing strikes and armed clashes

events.title: Normandy
events.body:
At a D-Day anniversary ceremony in Normandy, France, the US Defense Secretary used his speech to criticize European governments over migration, comparing the arrival of people by boat to an "invasion" of European beaches. He called out Spain, Italy, Greece, and Bulgaria by name, and suggested European leaders have grown too comfortable with the freedoms secured during World War II.

The remarks are part of a broader pattern of criticism from senior US officials toward European immigration policy. Earlier that same week, the US Vice President also blamed migration for a fatal stabbing in the United Kingdom, drawing a sharp response from the British government. Officials noted that the attacker in that case was born in Britain.

Sea arrivals into Europe have dropped significantly since their peak in 2015, when over a million people crossed the Mediterranean. Between January and early June 2026, around 9,100 people crossed the English Channel by small boat — down 38% from the year before.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Europeans and migrants caught in the middle of intensifying political disputes over migration policy

events.title: Armed Conflict in Khardali-Nabatieh road
events.body:
At least 12 people were killed in Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon, including a brigadier general, a captain, and a soldier whose military vehicle was hit on the Khardali-Nabatieh road. It was the first time a Lebanese army officer of that rank had been killed since the conflict began. More than 50 Lebanese army personnel have been killed since fighting started in March.

The Israeli military described the area as an "active combat zone" and said the incident was under investigation. Lebanese officials condemned the strike as a violation of sovereignty and international law. Regional governments including Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and Qatar also denounced the attack, as did the UN peacekeeping force in southern Lebanon.

The strikes occurred days after the two countries agreed to a conditional ceasefire through US-mediated talks. Despite repeated incidents involving Lebanese army personnel, Lebanese officials say the government has limited ability to respond. Over 13 national security personnel were killed in a separate incident weeks earlier with no clear government action following.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese army families and civilians in southern Lebanon amid continued Israeli strikes during ceasefire talks

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
An airstrike hit a tent camp in Gaza City, killing at least six Palestinians and wounding a dozen others. Children were among those hurt in the attack.

Hamas described the strike as "a massacre of women and children" and accused Israel of undermining ceasefire negotiations taking place in Cairo. The attack came while diplomatic talks related to a potential ceasefire were ongoing.

Displaced civilians sheltering in tent camps remain at risk as the conflict continues.
