events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in a Gaza City tent camp struck by an attack, including women and children among the casualties

events.title: Armed Conflict in Abu Zaeima
events.body:
A drone strike hit the main market in Abu Zaeima, a town in North Kordofan state in central Sudan, killing at least 11 people and wounding dozens more on Saturday. A local rights group, Emergency Lawyers, documented the strike but could not confirm who carried it out. Neither armed side claimed responsibility.

The strike came less than 24 hours after similar drone attacks hit nearby villages and a civilian vehicle. Later the same day, another drone struck a fuel station in el-Obeid, North Kordofan's capital, wounding at least four more civilians. Emergency Lawyers says nearly 70 people were killed in drone strikes across West and North Kordofan in the past week alone.

The UN reported at least 880 civilians killed by drone strikes nationwide between January and April of this year. Sudan's war, now in its fourth year, has killed tens of thousands and displaced nearly 13 million people — a scale the UN calls the world's largest displacement and hunger crisis.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in North Kordofan killed and wounded by repeated drone strikes on markets and villages

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tunis
events.body:
Hundreds of people marched in Tunisia's capital calling for press freedom and the release of political prisoners. The demonstration came amid a broad crackdown on dissent under President Kais Saied, which has resulted in the jailing of opposition figures.

Among those detained is the leader of the Ennahda party, one of the country's most prominent opposition movements. Protesters are pushing for those jailed during the crackdown to be freed and for restrictions on journalists and the press to be lifted.

The protest reflects growing concern among Tunisians about civil liberties and political space in the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Hundreds of Tunisian protesters and detained opposition figures amid a government crackdown on dissent

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tirana
events.body:
For six consecutive days, thousands of people have gathered in the Albanian capital to protest a proposed luxury beach resort tied to American investors. Demonstrators are calling on authorities to be more open about how the project was approved.

Environmental groups say the development would damage protected natural areas along Albania's Adriatic Coast. They accuse government officials of lacking transparency in the approval process and allowing harm to biodiversity that is supposed to be under legal protection.

The protests show no sign of stopping, and the dispute has drawn international attention given the high-profile figures connected to the resort project.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Thousands of Albanian protesters and communities along the Adriatic Coast affected by disputed resort development

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Fighting continued in southern Lebanon on June 5, with Hizbollah reporting around 20 attacks on Israeli troops in the area. The Israeli military separately stated that over the previous week, more than 650 Hizbollah targets had been struck and more than 125 fighters killed.

Lebanon's president publicly warned that Iran was treating Lebanon as a bargaining chip in its negotiations with the United States, calling the situation unacceptable. The statement signals growing concern among Lebanese leaders that outside powers are shaping the conflict's trajectory with little regard for Lebanon itself.

Southern Lebanese communities remain caught in the middle of active fighting, with no ceasefire reported.
events.source_title: International Crisis Group
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanon caught in ongoing strikes and armed attacks

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
An Israeli drone strike hit a tent camp for displaced people at a United Nations school compound in Gaza City, killing at least six people and wounding at least 15 more. Women and children are believed to be among those killed or hurt. Many of the wounded are being treated in intensive care. Witnesses described a large explosion and scenes of panic, with people fleeing into the streets as civilian vehicles rushed to carry away the injured. A wedding was reportedly taking place nearby at the time of the strike.

A separate strike the same day in the Khan Younis area killed a man who had been scheduled to marry later that day. Israeli tanks and drones also opened fire near a children's hospital in Gaza City's Tuffah neighborhood. The Israeli military said it had targeted fighters in the area without providing details.

These attacks occurred against the backdrop of a stalled ceasefire. Palestinian representatives were meeting with mediators in Egypt the same weekend to discuss implementing existing ceasefire terms, including reopening border crossings and allowing humanitarian aid into Gaza.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Displaced families in Gaza City's tent camps caught in ongoing strikes, including women and children among the casualties

events.title: Michaelmas Island
events.body:
A man died Saturday after being bitten by a shark estimated at about 4.5 meters (nearly 15 feet) while spearfishing with family off Michaelmas Island, southeast of Perth. Paramedics met the boat at shore but were unable to save him. Police are preparing a report for the coroner.

This is the second fatal shark attack in Western Australia in less than a month. A father of two died in a separate incident at Horseshoe Reef, northwest of Rottnest Island, after being attacked by a shark of similar size.

Authorities are urging residents to report any shark sightings in the area. While shark attacks occur more frequently in Australian waters than in many other parts of the world, fatalities are less common. Popular beaches typically have protective measures in place.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: A family near Perth grieving the sudden loss of a loved one to a shark attack

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kfar Tebnit
events.body:
Three Lebanese soldiers were killed when an Israeli military strike hit their vehicle on a road near the village of Kfar Tebnit in southern Lebanon, close to the city of Nabatieh. The Israeli military said it launched an investigation after confirming the strike, stating the vehicle was moving suspiciously toward its forces in what it described as an active combat zone. The Lebanese Army called the strike deliberate and repeated its accusations of ongoing Israeli aggression against its forces.

The incident occurred amid a broader Israeli campaign against Hezbollah, which has been fighting Israel since March. Over the same weekend, Israeli forces struck around 150 Hezbollah sites across southern Lebanon. Ceasefire efforts remain stalled after Hezbollah's leadership rejected a US-backed agreement, and a previous ceasefire reached in April failed to stop the fighting.

At least 3,550 people have been killed in Lebanon since the war began, according to the country's health ministry. Lebanon's president has said the Lebanese people are exhausted by the conflict.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Lebanese soldiers and civilians in southern Lebanon amid ongoing Israeli military strikes

events.title: Armed Conflict in St Petersburg
events.body:
Ukrainian forces launched what Russian officials called an "unprecedented" drone attack on and around St Petersburg, coinciding with the final day of Russia's major annual economic forum. More than 140 drones were intercepted over the surrounding Leningrad region. For the first time since the war began over four years ago, the city's governor urged residents to stay indoors. A fire broke out at a military facility in the area, and some buildings sustained minor damage, with nearby residents evacuated.

Ukraine's president said the strikes targeted naval arsenals and a base at Kronstadt, home to the Russian Navy's Baltic Fleet. He said drones also hit an oil depot in the Krasnodar region, roughly 500 kilometers away. Meanwhile, in Russian-occupied Luhansk in eastern Ukraine, authorities suspended coach services on two major roads and banned commuter train services, citing ongoing Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian logistics routes.

Peace efforts remain stalled. Russia's leader repeated that negotiations would only occur on Russia's terms, while Ukraine's president called for a ceasefire and direct talks. Civilians in occupied eastern Ukraine now face disrupted transportation as the drone campaign continues to target supply lines.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of St Petersburg and surrounding Leningrad region sheltering during large-scale drone strikes

events.title: Displacement in Oleshky
events.body:
The southern Ukrainian city of Oleshky has been under Russian occupation since 2022 and is now almost completely cut off from the outside world. Up to 2,000 people remain there — mostly elderly residents, people with limited mobility, and nearly 50 children. Before the full-scale invasion, the city had 24,000 residents. Five of the district's 13 localities have been completely destroyed.

Conditions have become desperate. The bridge connecting Oleshky to Ukrainian-controlled Kherson was blown up in 2022, and all roads in and out are heavily mined. The local hospital runs on generators with almost no fuel, making complex procedures impossible. Food deliveries have been largely cut off since early this year, with residents describing near-famine conditions since March.

Ukraine is now pushing for evacuation, involving the Foreign Ministry, human rights officials, and international organizations. Humanitarian corridors are being discussed, but officials say any agreement would require international oversight from bodies such as the United Nations and the Red Cross, and that Russia has so far used the remaining civilians as leverage.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Nearly 2,000 trapped residents of Oleshky, including children and elderly, facing famine, landmines, and no medical care

events.title: Armed Conflict in eastern Congo
events.body:
Health workers in eastern Congo were forced to flee after being threatened when they arrived to help with a safe burial for a person who died from Ebola. Residents warned them that armed rebels would be called if they did not leave.

The incident reflects the danger facing those trying to contain the Ebola outbreak in the region, where armed groups are active. When safe burials cannot take place, the virus can spread more easily to family members and others who handle the deceased.

Communities living in areas where both armed conflict and disease outbreaks overlap face compounding dangers, with limited access to outside help.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Health workers threatened while responding to an Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo villages

events.title: Displacement in Jonglei State
events.body:
Months of fighting and insecurity in South Sudan's eastern Jonglei State have forced hundreds of thousands of people to flee their homes. The UN refugee agency described the situation as one of the most severe conflict-related displacement emergencies in recent years.

The scale of the crisis has drawn urgent international attention, with aid organizations working to respond to the needs of those uprooted by the violence. Displaced residents face enormous hardship as they leave behind homes, livelihoods, and communities.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Hundreds of thousands of people driven from their homes by fighting in Jonglei State

events.title: Civil Unrest in West Bank
events.body:
The European Union recently sanctioned four settler organizations and three individuals connected to the Israeli settler movement in the occupied West Bank, citing their roles in violence against Palestinians. Sanctioned groups include a settlement support cooperative and a land rights organization whose director was named. Several Western governments, including the United Kingdom and Canada, also sanctioned Israel's Finance Minister over his alleged role in enabling settler violence.

The targeted individuals and groups dismissed the sanctions publicly, with some calling them a "badge of honour." Analysts say the measures are unlikely to change behavior, as those sanctioned rarely travel to countries where the penalties would have practical effect.

Since October 2023, UN and human rights monitors have documented widespread settler attacks across the West Bank, including in areas around Nablus, Ramallah, the South Hebron Hills, and the Jordan Valley. An estimated 1,168 people have been killed and more than 12,600 injured in the West Bank during this period. Around 33,000 Palestinians have been displaced, and nearly 23,000 detained, many without charge.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Palestinian civilians in the West Bank facing ongoing settler raids, displacement, and violence

events.title: Armed Conflict in Hebron
events.body:
A seven-month-old Palestinian baby was shot and killed by an Israeli soldier while riding in a car with his family in the Tel Rumeida area of Hebron in the occupied West Bank. The family says they had stopped the vehicle and posed no threat when shots were fired. The father says the bullet passed through his arm before striking his son in the head; the baby's mother was also wounded and remains in hospital. The Israeli military says soldiers fired after perceiving the vehicle accelerating toward them.

The Israeli military has acknowledged the family were "uninvolved civilians" and expressed regret, though the father rejected that statement, saying no warning was given before shots were fired. The incident is under review.

Tel Rumeida is a long-standing flashpoint where Israeli settlers live among Palestinian residents under heavy military presence. Since the Hamas-led attacks of October 2023, more than 1,000 Palestinians have been killed across the occupied West Bank, according to Palestinian health ministry figures.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: A Palestinian family in Hebron grieving a seven-month-old baby killed during a military stop

events.title: Civil Unrest in New Delhi
events.body:
Thousands of members of an online satirical group called the Cockroach Janta Party took to the streets in New Delhi to protest recent exam paper leaks and marking errors in India's education system. Protesters are calling for the resignation of the country's education minister.

The demonstrations come after the founder of the group returned to India to take part in the first protest held in the capital. Organizers say the exam failures contributed to students taking their own lives.

The protests reflect growing frustration among students and families over the integrity of national examinations and the accountability of education officials.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Students and families in India grieving deaths linked to exam paper leaks and marking failures

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
At least six people were killed and 15 injured when projectiles struck near a wedding tent at a displacement camp in Gaza City. Shrapnel spread into a nearby school that was crowded with displaced families. Women and children are believed to be among the dead and wounded, and the casualty toll is expected to rise. Many of those injured are being treated in an intensive care unit.

The attack was one of several reported across Gaza on the same day. In the Khan Younis area of southern Gaza, a separate strike killed a man who was set to be married later that day. In the Tuffah neighbourhood of Gaza City, tanks and drones opened fire near a children's hospital.

The violence comes as a ceasefire agreement, which took effect in October, remains under strain. More than half of Gaza is under military control, and negotiations over the ceasefire's next phase — including humanitarian aid access and border crossings — were ongoing in Egypt at the time of the attacks.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families gathered for a wedding in Gaza City struck amid ongoing bombardment, women and children among the casualties

events.title: Civil Unrest in Jantar Mantar, New Delhi
events.body:
Hundreds of mostly young protesters gathered at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Saturday, many wearing cockroach masks and carrying worn exam books. The group, calling itself the Cockroach Janta Party, formed about three weeks ago as a satirical response after India's chief justice reportedly compared government critics and unemployed youth to "cockroaches" and "parasites." What started as a social media parody quickly became a channel for anger over exam failures, job shortages, and economic frustration.

The protesters demanded the resignation of the Education Minister following a series of exam paper leaks, technical failures, and cancelled tests. Many pointed to problems with the NEET medical entrance exam and reports of student suicides as signs that the system has lost credibility.

Police in riot gear and steel barricades were positioned at the scene. The group has built a large online following, and Saturday's rally was its first attempt to bring that energy into the streets.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Young Indians demanding accountability after repeated exam failures and reports of student suicides

events.title: Armed Conflict in Khardali-Nabatieh road
events.body:
Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon killed at least 10 people, including two army officers and a soldier traveling in a military vehicle on the Khardali-Nabatieh road. A brigadier general and a captain were among those killed — the highest-ranking Lebanese army officer to die since fighting resumed in early March. The Israeli military said the strike occurred in an "active combat zone" and that the incident is under investigation. Lebanese officials condemned the attack as a violation of sovereignty and international law.

A separate strike on the village of Saksakiyah in the Sidon district killed six people and wounded four others. Another person died in a drone strike targeting a car in the Deir al-Zahrani area. Israel also issued new forced displacement orders for five southern villages, directing residents to move north of the Zahrani River.

A ceasefire announced on April 17 has never been fully observed. A further conditional truce was announced this week following talks in Washington, but Hezbollah rejected it. Since fighting began on March 2, at least 3,558 people have been killed and more than 10,800 injured across Lebanon.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese army soldiers and civilians killed and displaced amid ongoing Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon

events.title: Displacement in Haiti
events.body:
Gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, according to new figures from the UN migration agency. Nearly 1.5 million people now have no stable place to live, deepening what aid workers describe as an already severe humanitarian crisis.

The UN is calling for long-term engagement to address the growing needs of displaced Haitians, signaling that short-term responses are not enough to meet the scale of what people are facing.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Nearly 1.5 million Haitians without stable shelter amid record displacement and gang violence

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
An Israeli airstrike hit a car in Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, while a journalist was on camera reporting from the area. The strike was captured on video, showing the moment the car was hit directly behind the reporter.

The attack came despite a recently announced ceasefire brokered by the United States. Israel has continued to carry out deadly strikes across southern Lebanon since that announcement, raising serious concerns for civilians living in the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Nabatieh and southern Lebanon amid continued airstrikes despite a ceasefire agreement

events.title: United States
events.body:
A federal judge struck down sweeping immigration restrictions the US administration had placed on citizens from 39 countries, mostly in Africa, the Middle East, and Asia. The restrictions, imposed in November 2025 following a shooting in Washington, DC, had frozen asylum cases, green card applications, work approvals, and citizenship processing for affected individuals.

The judge said the policy threw "countless immigrants" into legal limbo and found that claimed national security justifications masked anti-immigrant sentiment. Many of those affected had done nothing wrong — their cases were frozen solely because of the country where they were born.

The ruling comes as the administration has pursued broad limits on legal immigration, including a separate pause on visa processing from 75 countries and historically low refugee caps. Many immigrants affected by the November policy had gone more than six months without income, legal status, or any clear path forward. The court ruling now orders that processing resume.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Immigrants from 39 countries left without work, legal status, or ability to plan for their futures

events.title: Armed Conflict in Az-Zawayda
events.body:
Families in Az-Zawayda, in central Gaza, spent the morning clearing debris after an overnight strike destroyed multiple buildings in the area. Entire structures were reduced to rubble, leaving residents to dig through the wreckage of their homes.

The attack came during a period of intensifying military operations. Gaza recorded its highest death toll in six months around the time of the strike, according to reports.

Civilians in the area face the immediate challenge of finding shelter and recovering belongings from destroyed homes.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families in Az-Zawayda sifting through rubble as death tolls reach a six-month high

events.title: Armed Conflict in Khan Younis
events.body:
A 26-year-old man was killed in an air strike on his family home in Khan Younis hours before his wedding was set to take place. His family had been preparing to celebrate what should have been one of the most joyful days of his life.

His family says the day of celebration turned immediately into a day of mourning. The strike occurred as fighting continues in Gaza, even as a ceasefire has been reported to be in effect.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: A family in Khan Younis mourning a 26-year-old man killed hours before his wedding

events.title: Coup/Crisis in South Litani Sector
events.body:
On June 3, the United States, Lebanon, and Israel jointly announced an agreement to implement a ceasefire. The deal requires Hezbollah to stop firing and withdraw its fighters from the area south of the Litani River.

The following day, Hezbollah's top leader rejected the terms, saying the group would not halt operations while fighting continues and calling the conditions tantamount to surrender. Hezbollah insisted any ceasefire must cover all of Lebanon, not just the south.

Iran's Revolutionary Guard backed that position, saying peace in the region depends on a full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory. A senior Iranian commander specifically called for Israel to pull back to positions it held before what the source describes as a "40-day war." With key armed parties rejecting the announced terms, the path to a ceasefire remains deeply uncertain for civilians on both sides of the border.
events.source_title: International Crisis Group
events.prayer: Civilians in Lebanon and Israel amid stalled ceasefire negotiations and continued fighting

events.title: Armed Conflict in Sea of Azov
events.body:
Ukraine struck five cargo ships and tankers overnight in the ports of Mariupol and Berdyansk and surrounding coastal waters, saying the vessels were carrying military cargo and fuel and were involved in smuggling Ukrainian grain. The ships had their names painted over and radars switched off. Azerbaijan confirmed five of its citizens were killed in attacks on two of the vessels, identifying the ships as the Nastra and the Circon, and noted the ships were not Azerbaijani-owned.

On the same day, Ukraine confirmed one of its naval drones had exploded near an oil terminal in the Romanian Black Sea port of Constanta, saying Russian electronic jamming had knocked it off course. Romanian authorities said it caused significant damage to a ship and warehouses but no casualties. It was the second security incident in Romania that week.

Inside Ukraine, at least 13 people were killed and more than 70 injured in the past day, including four workers at a dairy factory near Kyiv and a woman killed at a petrol station in Kherson.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine killed in drone strikes, and communities in Romania affected by stray naval drones

events.title: Pyongyang
events.body:
North Korea's leader toured what South Korean officials believe is a new uranium enrichment plant, announcing plans to expand the country's nuclear arsenal "at an exponential rate." This came shortly after North Korea's foreign ministry declared that the country would never give up its nuclear weapons, responding to a joint statement from the United States, Japan, India, and Australia calling for denuclearization.

North Korea has steadily built up its nuclear program over recent years, enshrining its nuclear policy in its constitution in 2023 and, according to South Korean intelligence, codifying an "automatic nuclear launch" policy if its leadership is threatened. A US defense official told Congress in April 2026 that North Korea's nuclear forces are "increasingly capable of targeting the US" and present "a clear and present danger of nuclear attack."

Analysts estimate North Korea may have assembled around 50 nuclear warheads and is developing smaller tactical weapons as well as larger warheads. Independent oversight of the program is currently not possible.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Civilians in North Korea and neighboring countries living under growing nuclear threat

events.title: Armed Conflict in Lebanon
events.body:
Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has continued even after the United States announced a new ceasefire agreement. Israel kept up attacks on Lebanon following the announcement, and Hezbollah also kept fighting.

A key complication is that Hezbollah was not part of the talks that took place between Israeli and Lebanese officials in Washington, raising questions about whether any agreement reached there can hold on the ground.

The situation leaves ordinary people in Lebanon facing continued violence despite diplomatic efforts to stop it.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese people caught between ongoing Israeli attacks and Hezbollah fighting despite ceasefire announcement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Black Sea port
events.body:
A Ukrainian naval drone malfunctioned and exploded at a major Black Sea port in Romania, according to reporting from Al Jazeera. Romania is a NATO member bordering Ukraine, and its Black Sea ports handle significant cargo traffic.

The source does not provide details on casualties, property damage, or the port's current operational status. It is also unclear how the drone reached Romanian territory or what it was originally intended to target.

The incident raises concerns for civilians, port workers, and shipping communities in the area.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: People near a Romanian Black Sea port struck by an exploding naval drone

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
At least five people were killed in southern Lebanon on Friday as airstrikes and drone attacks continued despite a US-brokered ceasefire deal announced the day before. Strikes hit residential areas, roads, and buildings across several towns. Two people were killed in Habboush, including a doctor. A young man was killed and another wounded in Doueir. One person was killed in Qalawiya Tower, and a man sitting in a car was killed by a drone in Kfar Reman. Warplanes also struck close to a hospital in the area.

The Israeli military issued two rounds of displacement orders covering nine towns and villages. Israel's defense minister indicated the military campaign would continue, and the country's prime minister had not yet approved implementing the ceasefire.

Lebanese officials publicly urged Iran to stop treating their country as leverage in wider regional negotiations. Lebanese leaders also called on Hezbollah to pursue diplomacy rather than continued fighting. Israel now occupies roughly one-fifth of Lebanese territory, and more than 600 people were killed in the weeks since a previous ceasefire was announced in April.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in southern Lebanese towns killed and displaced as airstrikes continue despite a new ceasefire agreement

events.title: Armed Conflict in Naqoura and Nabatieh districts
events.body:
Despite active ceasefires in Gaza, Lebanon, and between Iran and the United States, fighting has continued across all three areas. In southern Lebanon, strikes on the Naqoura and Nabatieh districts killed at least one person. In Gaza, a bombing hit a residential building, killing nine people. Iran and the US have continued trading attacks of growing intensity, and Iran has also fired missiles and drones at Gulf nations including the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Bahrain.

Legal experts say ceasefires are agreements to pause fighting, not legally binding instruments with strong enforcement mechanisms. Unlike formal peace treaties, ceasefires rarely come with guarantors who can force compliance. In Gaza, a UN Security Council resolution calls for the ceasefire agreement to be honored in full, but experts note that the US veto effectively shields certain parties from any formal censure.

With no neutral authority empowered to declare violations and assign consequences, civilians in all three areas remain at risk even when agreements are formally in place.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Gaza, Lebanon, and across the Middle East caught in ongoing strikes despite active ceasefires

events.title: Syria
events.body:
Chemical weapons inspectors have found a significant cache of previously undeclared chemical weapons in Syria. The stockpile includes rockets of the same type used in the 2013 Ghouta attack, one of the most deadly chemical weapons incidents in recent history. The UN's top disarmament official described the find as a "momentous discovery" for international security.

The Ghouta attack, which took place near Damascus, killed hundreds of civilians and drew worldwide condemnation. The discovery of weapons matching that attack raises serious questions about what other materials may remain hidden and unaccounted for in the country.

Syria had previously agreed to surrender its chemical weapons stockpile following the 2013 attack. The finding suggests that declaration was incomplete. Inspectors have not publicly named a specific location where the cache was found.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Syrian civilians and the international community grappling with discovery of hidden chemical weapons stockpiles

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tripoli
events.body:
Hundreds of Libyans gathered outside the UN refugee agency's headquarters in Tripoli on Thursday to protest against undocumented migrants living in the country. Demonstrators chanted slogans calling for migrants to leave, demanded the closure of the UNHCR office, and blocked the main entrance with a truckload of sand. Signs carried by protesters rejected accusations of racism while demanding the removal of what they called "intruders."

Protesters accused the UN agency of working to permanently resettle migrants in Libya, a claim the UN firmly denied. The UN mission in Libya stated that no resettlement programs inside the country exist and that its work focuses on finding solutions outside Libya, including evacuation to third countries. The mission also warned against the spread of misleading information and condemned any threats or violence targeting UN staff and property.

Libya hosts an estimated 900,000 migrants in a country of about 7 million people. Many are Sudanese fleeing civil war. Thursday's protest was described as the largest in a series of recent anti-migrant demonstrations, as some Libyans increasingly blame migrants for economic and social pressures after 15 years of political division and conflict.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Migrants and refugees in Libya amid rising tensions and anti-migrant protests

events.title: Armed Conflict in Donetsk
events.body:
At least 12 people were killed and dozens injured in Russian missile, bomb, and drone strikes across multiple regions of Ukraine. The deadliest attacks hit the eastern Donetsk region, where five people were killed and eleven injured. Seven communities came under fire, with 42 civilian sites destroyed, including residential buildings, a medical facility, an evacuation vehicle, and an ambulance.

Three more people were killed and 21 injured in the northeastern Kharkiv region, while two were killed and four injured in the village of Yampil in the Sumy region. One person each was killed in the Dnipropetrovsk and Kherson regions, with fires breaking out at multiple sites.

The attacks fell on a day when Ukraine's president marked the International Day of Innocent Children Victims of Aggression, noting that at least 707 Ukrainian children have been killed since the war began in February 2022, with thousands more wounded, abducted, or still unaccounted for.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: At least 12 civilians killed and dozens wounded across Ukraine in ongoing Russian strikes, including 707 children lost since 2022

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bolivia
events.body:
Large protests have been rolling across Bolivia since May, with demonstrators blockading roads and clashing with police. Many protesters are teachers, miners, farmers, and union workers angered by the removal of fuel subsidies, a disputed land law, and years of economic trouble as the country's foreign currency reserves and natural gas exports have declined. Some have called for the president's resignation, though his administration has rejected that idea outright.

Bolivia's legislature approved military deployment in late May to clear blockades, but demonstrations have continued. The president reshuffled his cabinet and pledged a pay cut in response to the unrest.

The United States government, through its Defense Secretary, publicly described the protests as an attempted overthrow of Bolivia's elected government and linked demonstrators to drug traffickers — characterizations that protesters and their supporters dispute. Bolivia is the third-largest producer of coca, a crop tied to both the cocaine trade and traditional Andean culture, making anti-drug policy a long-running point of tension in the country's politics.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Teachers, miners, farmers, and union workers in Bolivia amid ongoing street protests and military deployments

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Lebanon
events.body:
A ceasefire framework brokered by the United States and accepted by both Israel and Lebanon has been rejected by Hezbollah. The group described the agreement as harmful to Lebanon's interests.

The proposed plan would create security zones near the Lebanon-Israel border, to be controlled by the Lebanese army. The arrangement would be conditional on Hezbollah pulling its fighters back from the area. Hezbollah's rejection leaves the framework's future uncertain.

With one of the key parties refusing the terms, it is unclear whether the agreement can move forward or whether border communities will see any reduction in conflict activity.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese civilians caught between a ceasefire deal rejected by Hezbollah and ongoing conflict pressures

events.title: Civil Unrest in Kenya
events.body:
Protests against a planned US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya turned deadly this week, leaving at least two people dead. The facility is being constructed on a US air force base and is intended for Americans who may be exposed to the Ebola virus.

Kenya's president defended the decision, saying it was the "right thing" to allow the United States to build the site. The deaths came amid public opposition to the project, though the source does not detail what specific concerns drove protesters into the streets.

The situation raises ongoing tension between government policy and public sentiment, with at least two families now grieving losses connected to the unrest.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families of the two people killed in protests over the Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kuwait International Airport
events.body:
Surveillance footage released by Kuwait's civil aviation authority shows the moment drones struck Kuwait International Airport, killing one person and injuring more than 60 others. The attack caused significant damage to a passenger terminal. Kuwait's defence ministry described the strike as "criminal Iranian aggression."

The foreign ministry also confirmed that diplomatic missions were damaged in the attack. No date beyond "Wednesday" is given in the source material.

The strike on a major civilian airport raises serious concerns for travellers and airport workers, and adds to regional tensions already affecting multiple countries across the Middle East.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: One person killed and dozens wounded in a drone strike on Kuwait's main airport

events.title: Armed Conflict in Dahieh, Beirut
events.body:
A ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon is under serious strain after Hezbollah rejected its terms. The group's leader called the negotiations "futile" and "humiliating," saying the deal was rejected by "broad segments of the Lebanese people." The agreement would create security zones inside Lebanon where Hezbollah operatives would be banned, and is conditional on a complete halt to attacks by the group.

Reporting from Dahieh, a Hezbollah stronghold in Beirut, a BBC correspondent described the ceasefire as deeply fragile. The deal's survival depends heavily on whether Hezbollah complies with conditions the group has publicly refused.

Separately, at least 10 people were killed and 27 injured in Lebanon, according to the country's state-run news agency. The ceasefire's future remains uncertain, and civilians in affected areas continue to face the risk of renewed violence if the agreement collapses.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Lebanese civilians in Beirut amid a fragile ceasefire and Hezbollah's rejection of its terms

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bondi Beach
events.body:
A man celebrated for disarming one of the alleged shooters during a deadly attack on a Jewish event at Bondi Beach last December has now been charged with domestic violence assault, as well as stalking and intimidation. The charges relate to an alleged incident involving his father in March. He has denied the allegations.

The man gained wide recognition after video showed him wrestling a firearm from one of the alleged attackers during a mass shooting at a Hanukkah gathering. He was shot several times in the process. The attack killed 15 people and injured dozens more. Police have classified it as a terrorist incident targeting the Jewish community — Australia's deadliest mass shooting since 1996.

The man is due to appear in a Sydney court on 29 June. The new charges are separate from the December attack and do not affect his account of events that night.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Victims and families of the Bondi Beach Hanukkah attack, and a community still processing loss

events.title: Armed Conflict in Simferopol
events.body:
Ukrainian strikes hit the occupied Crimean peninsula in back-to-back attacks, according to Russian-backed officials. Three people were killed and seven wounded in an attack on facilities in the regional capital, Simferopol — described as the first strike there to result in fatalities. Ukraine said it had hit a fuel depot. A separate attack struck a commuter train traveling toward the city of Kerch, killing one person and injuring three more.

The strikes are part of a broader Ukrainian drone campaign targeting supply routes in Russian-occupied territory. Fuel shortages have spread across Crimea, with authorities introducing rationing and temporarily suspending fuel sales to residents in Sevastopol. Ukrainian officials did not claim the attacks directly but stated that Ukraine targets only military infrastructure.

Separately, eight people were killed when a drone struck a passenger bus in the Russian-controlled Donetsk region. Meanwhile, Russian strikes on Ukrainian cities continue — at least 22 people were killed in combined missile and drone attacks across Ukraine on Monday night alone.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Crimea and Donetsk caught in escalating strikes on transport and fuel infrastructure

events.title: Armed Conflict in Gaza City
events.body:
At least 11 Palestinians were killed and dozens wounded when Israeli aircraft struck at least four residential buildings in Gaza City during the early morning hours. The strikes hit the Sheikh Radwan and Tel al-Hawa neighborhoods and Shati refugee camp, causing large explosions and sending residents fleeing in panic. Among those killed were women and children, including two children with disabilities.

Witnesses described three helicopters hovering over the coastline before launching simultaneous strikes. The Israeli military had no immediate comment.

The strikes come despite a US-brokered ceasefire that took effect last October. More than 940 people have been killed since that ceasefire began, according to Gaza's health ministry. Humanitarian conditions in the territory continue to worsen, and reports indicate Israel may seek to expand the area of land under its control to 70% of Gaza.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Women, children, and families in Gaza City killed and wounded in overnight airstrikes on residential buildings

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
A US-backed ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon has been rejected by Hezbollah, the Lebanese armed group that was not included in the talks. The deal, announced Wednesday after a fourth round of US-mediated talks in Washington, would require Hezbollah fighters to withdraw from the area between the Israeli border and the Litani River, roughly 30 kilometers north. It also calls for Lebanese army forces to take exclusive control of designated "pilot zones" in that area.

Despite the announcement, Israeli strikes continued in southern Lebanon on Thursday. Five people were killed in air strikes on the Bekaa Valley town of Sohmor, and one more person was killed when a motorcycle was targeted near the city of Tyre. A UN peacekeeper also died. The Israeli defense minister said military operations would continue "for the time being."

Residents in Beirut's southern suburbs expressed deep skepticism. Shopkeepers who have run businesses there for decades said they saw no hope in the agreement, with one calling it a surrender rather than a peace deal. Further talks are scheduled for June 22.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Lebanese civilians in southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs caught between ongoing strikes and a disputed ceasefire

events.title: Civil Unrest in Narta Lagoon
events.body:
On May 30, activists walked through pine forests and sand dunes near the Narta Lagoon on Albania's southern coast to reach a construction site connected to a large tourism development on the Zvernec Peninsula. Private security guards were filmed forcibly removing demonstrators from the area, sparking widespread outrage. In the days that followed, thousands gathered in Tirana under the slogan "Albania is not for sale," calling for the project to be halted.

The development is projected to cost over €4 billion and create more than 10,000 jobs. The site sits within a protected coastal landscape of wetlands, lagoons, pine forests, and sand dunes that supports hundreds of species, including pink flamingos migrating between Europe and Africa. Conservationists say more than 18,000 hectares of habitat could be affected.

Albania's prime minister defended the project as a strategic investment but condemned the security guards' behavior. He stated that no final environmental permit has been issued and that impact assessments are still ongoing, while the EU is also watching closely amid Albania's active accession talks with Brussels.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Protesters and residents in Albania's protected coastal wetlands facing displacement from major development pressure

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tiananmen Square
events.body:
On the anniversary of the June 4, 1989 crackdown in Beijing, a surviving student protest leader who has lived in exile for 37 years spoke publicly in Tokyo about the lasting impact of those events. Now living in Taipei, the man was among the students the Chinese government identified as protest ringleaders and has been told he will never receive amnesty or be allowed to return home.

He described how student demonstrations that spring had begun as a gathering to honor a reformist official who died in April 1989. The protests grew through April and May, with crowds filling Tiananmen Square and students sensing the government might meet their demands. Several protesters went on hunger strike. The government ultimately sent in troops to suppress the demonstrations.

The exiled dissident said ethnic minorities, including Tibetans and Uyghurs, have also faced ongoing repression inside China in the decades since. He remains unable to see his family in China, a separation now stretching nearly four decades.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Families separated by the 1989 Tiananmen crackdown and survivors still living in decades-long exile

events.title: Armed Conflict in Marjayoun
events.body:
A UN peacekeeper died early Thursday after mortar fire struck his position near Marjayoun in southeastern Lebanon, according to the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL).

The announcement described this as another attack, suggesting previous incidents have also targeted peacekeeping positions in the area. UNIFIL has been deployed in southern Lebanon as part of an international effort to monitor stability in the region.

No further details about the attacker or the circumstances of the strike were included in the announcement. The death adds to a pattern of danger facing international peacekeepers operating in the south of the country, where civilians and aid workers also remain at risk.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: UN peacekeeper killed by mortar fire in southeastern Lebanon, and colleagues still serving in the area

events.title: Civil Unrest in Mogadishu
events.body:
Heavy gunfire broke out in Mogadishu's Hawl Wadaag district when clashes erupted between government and opposition forces near the homes of former senior political leaders. Hundreds of families fled their neighbourhoods, and large parts of the city centre emptied by the following day. A main road was sealed off, and Bakara market — the city's largest commercial hub — shut down, with one estimate putting direct losses to businesses and services at roughly $3.8 million.

The violence stems from a dispute over whether the current president legally extended his term through constitutional amendments passed by parliament. Opposition figures reject the move and had been organising protests when the shooting began. Two of Somalia's most influential federal states also reject the amendments, and there is no constitutional court to settle the question.

The clashes ended a period of improving security in the capital. Somalia has not held a direct national election since the late 1960s, and similar standoffs over electoral timetables turned violent as recently as 2021. Ordinary residents and small business owners say they have no part in the political dispute but bear the heaviest costs when tensions spill into the streets.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Hundreds of families displaced by political gunfire in Mogadishu, with livelihoods and daily life disrupted

events.title: Civil Unrest in South Africa
events.body:
Anti-immigration violence has forced foreign migrants out of their homes across South Africa. Mozambique has confirmed that five of its citizens were killed, and hundreds of people are now crossing back over the border to escape the danger.

Displaced families are sheltering in community centres while Ghana and Nigeria are making plans to evacuate their citizens from the country. The violence has left many people without shelter, separated from their belongings, and uncertain about what comes next.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Foreign nationals in South Africa driven from their homes by anti-immigration violence, five confirmed dead

events.title: Civil Unrest in Nanyuki
events.body:
Hundreds of people took to the streets in Nanyuki, Kenya, on Monday and Tuesday to protest a planned US Ebola quarantine facility being built at a military air base in the town. At least two people were killed and one was injured when the demonstration turned violent on Monday. Protesters and health workers have raised concerns about the risk of bringing Ebola cases into a country that has never recorded the disease and has a fragile health system.

The facility is intended to house Americans who contract Ebola while overseas, rather than allowing them to return to the United States. A healthcare workers' union warned that Kenya should not be treated as a "containment colony." A court suspended the plans last Friday following legal challenges from civil society groups, but government officials have continued defending the project.

The protests come as an Ebola outbreak involving the rare Bundibugyo strain — for which no approved vaccine or treatment exists — has infected at least 321 people and killed 48 in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with cases also confirmed in Uganda.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Kenyan communities in Nanyuki facing deadly protests over an Ebola quarantine facility, and health workers across the region with no vaccines or protective gear

events.title: Civil Unrest in Tirana
events.body:
Nightly protests have been taking place in front of the prime minister's office in Tirana, Albania, over a proposed large-scale tourism development on the Adriatic coast. Demonstrators, who appear to be independent of mainstream political parties, are concerned that construction near Sazan island and a wetlands site at Zvernec, close to the city of Vlora, would damage habitat for flamingos and other protected species. One ecologist described the planned development as effectively "a new city with around 10,000 rooms" that would "completely destroy that wild region."

Protesters have also raised concerns about transparency in negotiations between the Albanian government and foreign investors, which reportedly began in 2024. The government argues the project involves privately acquired land and would bring roughly €4 billion in investment, along with jobs and infrastructure improvements. The prime minister has called the protesters "well-meaning but misinformed" and suggested outside regional interests are fueling the unrest. Communities near the protected coastal area remain caught between promised economic development and fears for the natural environment.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Young Albanian protesters and coastal communities facing disputed development of protected wetlands near Vlora

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
A new US-mediated ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon was announced by the Trump administration, but its prospects are already in serious doubt. Israel's defense minister has said military operations will continue regardless, and Hezbollah has rejected the deal, calling it a "surrender and defeat." Hezbollah was not part of the negotiations at all.

The agreement calls for a complete halt to Hezbollah fire, withdrawal of its fighters from south of the Litani River, and creation of zones under exclusive Lebanese army control. However, the text does not address an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, where Israeli forces now occupy roughly one-fifth of the country. A previous ceasefire reached in April failed to stop Israeli strikes, and more than 600 people have been killed in Lebanon since that agreement.

Talks are scheduled to resume the week of June 22, but whether that stage will be reached remains unclear. Civilians in Lebanon continue to face an active conflict with no reliable halt in sight.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Lebanese civilians amid ongoing strikes and an uncertain ceasefire in a country partly under foreign military occupation

events.title: Armed Conflict in southern Lebanon
events.body:
Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a new ceasefire framework, announced by the US State Department. The agreement depends on a complete halt to attacks from Hezbollah and the withdrawal of the group's fighters from southern Lebanon, between the Litani River and the Israeli border. The two sides are set to meet again on June 22 to work toward a fuller agreement.

The announcement came after a fragile partial ceasefire agreed on Monday was quickly tested. Israeli strikes killed at least nine people in southern Lebanon on Wednesday, including two paramedics whose ambulance was hit in the Chehour area. Hezbollah fired rockets and a drone into northern Israel the same day.

Hezbollah has not publicly responded to the new ceasefire framework. A senior member of the group said it does not recognize the Washington talks and will not be bound by their outcomes. At least 3,516 people have been killed in Lebanon since the conflict began.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Over 3,500 people killed in Lebanon, civilians caught between ongoing strikes and cross-border attacks

events.title: Armed Conflict in Chehour, Tyre, Khaldeh
events.body:
Israeli strikes hit locations across Lebanon on Wednesday, killing nine people and wounding others. Two medics died in the municipality of Chehour, six people were killed near the coastal city of Tyre, and a Lebanese soldier was killed while travelling on a road in the south. A strike in the Khaldeh area on the southern outskirts of Beirut injured two more people. Lebanese health officials said at least ten vehicles were struck, including an ambulance.

A nominal ceasefire agreed in mid-April has failed to hold, with both sides accusing each other of violations. Israel says it will continue operations as long as Hezbollah remains armed; Lebanon insists on a full ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched rockets into northern Israel on Wednesday, which Israeli forces said they intercepted.

Israeli and Lebanese negotiators continued direct talks in Washington, now in their fourth round. The US Secretary of State expressed hope for a joint statement and action plan, while mediation efforts were also underway in Qatar. Civilians across Lebanon remain exposed to strikes with no clear front line.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Nine people killed and others wounded across Lebanon as strikes reach Beirut outskirts amid stalled ceasefire talks

events.title: Civil Unrest in Howl Wadaag district, Mogadishu
events.body:
Heavy gunfire broke out in central Mogadishu after Somalia's former prime minister said government forces attacked his group ahead of planned protests. Witnesses heard RPG explosions and shooting lasting about 15 minutes in the Howl Wadaag district, near where the former official had relocated to join Thursday's demonstrations.

The violence is rooted in a political standoff over the presidency. Somalia's current president extended his term by one year after it expired on May 15, citing a newly passed constitution. Opposition figures and regional leaders reject the move as a power grab, and large protests were planned for Thursday. A former president also moved into central Mogadishu in solidarity with demonstrators.

Somalia remains deeply unstable, with al-Shabab controlling much of the country and ongoing tensions between rival clans. U.S. and U.K. efforts to broker talks between the government and opposition have not succeeded. Civilians in Mogadishu neighborhoods were caught in the crossfire as the political crisis turned violent.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Mogadishu caught between political gunfire and planned street protests

events.title: Armed Conflict in St Petersburg
events.body:
Ukraine launched a series of air strikes near St Petersburg late Tuesday night, hitting an oil facility and a naval air base in the area. The attacks took place hours before international guests were set to arrive in the city for a major economic forum.

No details were given in the source about casualties or the extent of damage to either site.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians near St Petersburg caught in overnight strikes on oil and military facilities

events.title: Armed Conflict in Donetsk
events.body:
A drone struck a passenger bus traveling through a Russian-occupied area of Ukraine's eastern Donetsk region, killing eight people and wounding ten others. Fifty-three passengers had been registered on the bus. Russia blamed a Ukrainian drone for the strike; Ukraine declined to comment on what it called statements from an aggressor state.

In a Ukrainian-held part of Donetsk, Russian shelling of the city of Kramatorsk killed three people and injured one more. Separately, an 86-year-old woman was killed in a drone attack on Ukraine's southern Kherson region overnight.

The strikes come a day after one of Russia's largest attacks of the war, in which more than 700 missiles and drones were launched across Ukraine, killing at least 22 people including women and children. Overnight, Ukraine also struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, temporarily restricting flights at the city's main airport during a major international economic forum.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Civilians in Ukraine and Russia caught in escalating drone and missile strikes

events.title: Malviya Nagar
events.body:
A fire tore through a multi-storey guesthouse in the Malviya Nagar area of south Delhi, killing at least 21 people and injuring dozens more. The building operated as a bed-and-breakfast for patients and relatives seeking care at a nearby private hospital. Many of those killed were foreign nationals who had traveled from South Asian, African, and Central Asian countries for medical treatment or to support family members receiving care.

More than 40 people were rescued and taken to hospital. Eyewitnesses described finding people unconscious beneath beds and in washrooms. Some jumped from upper floors to escape the flames, and a nearby shopkeeper spread quilts on the ground to cushion their fall.

The cause of the fire is not yet known. Authorities are investigating whether the guesthouse had proper permits to operate. Fires are frequent in Delhi, where building safety laws are often poorly enforced and electrical systems frequently found faulty.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Foreign nationals and South Asian travelers killed in a Delhi guesthouse fire, families awaiting news

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kuwait City
events.body:
Iranian drones struck Kuwait's international airport, killing one person and injuring more than 60 others. The person killed was identified as an Indian citizen, and several other Indian nationals were among the wounded. Kuwait's defense ministry called it "criminal Iranian aggression," and the foreign ministry said diplomatic missions had also been damaged. Kuwait ordered two Iranian diplomats to leave the country within 24 hours.

Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed the attack, saying it was retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran also said it targeted US military bases in the region. The US said it had acted in self-defense and that it intercepted Iranian missiles fired toward Kuwait and Bahrain.

The strikes come as ceasefire negotiations between the US and Iran have stalled. Diplomatic missions near the airport were reported damaged, raising concerns for international staff and civilians in the area.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: One person killed and dozens injured in drone strikes on Kuwait's international airport

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Tigray
events.body:
Ethiopia held a national election Monday, but the entire Tigray region was excluded from voting due to ongoing tensions between regional and federal authorities. Tigray has had no federal representation for six years, following a civil war with federal forces that lasted from 2020 to 2022. The election outcome is widely expected to favor the incumbent leader.

Across West Africa, military juntas have replaced elected governments in Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, and Guinea since 2020. In Guinea-Bissau, a coup took place in 2025. In April, Burkina Faso's junta leader publicly told citizens to "forget" democracy, calling it deadly — remarks that drew both criticism and some support among residents who feel security and economic needs outweigh electoral politics. Niger, Mali, and Burkina Faso formally left the regional ECOWAS bloc in January 2025 and formed their own alliance.

A UN report links coups to poverty, inequality, poor governance, and public disappointment with elected governments. Sudan remains in civil war between rival military factions, and instability continues across the Horn of Africa and Central Africa.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Civilians across the Sahel and Horn of Africa living under military rule, poverty, and political exclusion

events.title: Armed Conflict in eastern Congo
events.body:
Escalating violence in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo is making it harder to treat and contain Ebola, according to the UN's aid coordination office. Fighting in the region has disrupted humanitarian operations at a time when health workers need safe access to affected communities.

The combination of armed conflict and a disease outbreak puts civilians in a particularly difficult position — unable to reach medical care and cut off from aid workers trying to reach them. UN officials flagged the situation on a Tuesday briefing, though specific locations and casualty figures were not provided in the initial report.

Eastern Congo has faced years of instability driven by multiple armed groups. When violence flares, it delays vaccinations, contact tracing, and basic health services — all critical for stopping an Ebola outbreak before it spreads further.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Civilians in eastern Congo caught between ongoing violence and an active Ebola outbreak

events.title: Displacement in Lebanon
events.body:
The UN reproductive health agency (UNFPA) warned this week that women and girls in Lebanon are bearing a heavy toll from continued violence and displacement, even after a ceasefire was reached. The agency said the situation is putting lives at serious risk.

UNFPA's warning focused on the danger facing women and girls who cannot access the healthcare they need during pregnancy, childbirth, and other critical moments. Displacement has left many without clinics, medical staff, or basic supplies.

The agency called for urgent action, saying that delays in responding to these needs cost lives. Communities already struggling with the effects of conflict are now facing a growing health emergency that is falling hardest on women and girls.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Women and girls in Lebanon lacking access to reproductive healthcare amid ongoing displacement and violence

events.title: Armed Conflict in Nabatieh
events.body:
Israeli forces have captured Beaufort Castle, a 900-year-old Crusader fortress near the city of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon, as part of what officials describe as the deepest Israeli military advance into the country in 26 years. Troops have crossed north of the Litani River and continued pushing toward the Zahrani River, with heavy fighting reported across the south.

Lebanon's culture minister says Israeli bombardments have struck near the ancient city of Tyre and directly hit Beaufort Castle. Tyre, a UNESCO World Heritage Site containing major Phoenician and Roman ruins, has seen roughly 200,000 people flee the city and surrounding area. Across Lebanon, the broader conflict has displaced more than one million people.

UNESCO officials note that dozens of protected heritage sites in southern Lebanon carry the highest level of legal protection under international law. Several medieval castles and ancient sanctuaries in the region are now within active conflict zones, and further damage could put governments and individuals at risk of criminal responsibility under international conventions.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Roughly 200,000 people displaced from Tyre and surrounding areas amid ongoing bombardment and advancing forces

events.title: Armed Conflict in Qeshm Island
events.body:
Iran fired missiles and drones at Kuwait and Bahrain in the early hours of Wednesday, targeting areas where US military assets are based. Kuwait's international airport was struck, with flight suspensions, facility damage, and injuries reported. At least one Indian national was killed and others were wounded. US officials said some Iranian missiles fell short or broke apart before reaching their targets, and intercepted projectiles may have caused some of the airport damage. No US personnel or assets were reported harmed.

Shortly before the Iranian strikes, US forces hit a telecommunications tower on Iran's Qeshm Island and shot down Iranian drones targeting civilian ships in the Gulf. Iran said a US strike also damaged an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz, and its navy responded by targeting another vessel.

Iran accused Kuwait and Bahrain of allowing their territory to be used for US operations and warned it would respond to future attacks. The flare-up follows a temporary ceasefire that had been brokered by Pakistan in April, and raises fresh concerns for civilians and travelers across the region.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Civilians in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Iran caught in overnight missile strikes and a widening Gulf conflict

events.title: Civil Unrest in Borno State
events.body:
Teachers in north-eastern Nigeria took to the streets to protest the abduction of dozens of schoolchildren in Borno State last month. The demonstrators called on authorities to do more to protect schools and the children who attend them.

Borno State has long been affected by violence that has at times targeted schools and students. The latest abduction has prompted educators to speak out publicly, reflecting deep concern among communities about the safety of children trying to get an education.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Teachers and schoolchildren in Borno State after a mass abduction from a learning institution

events.title: Armed Conflict in Kuwait International Airport
events.body:
Kuwait's government has described a drone attack on the country's international airport as "heinous Iranian aggression." One person was killed and dozens were injured when drones struck a terminal, causing significant material damage.

Kuwait's defence ministry attributed the attack directly to Iran. The strike caused damage at the airport terminal, though the extent of disruption to flights and airport operations was not detailed in available reports.

The attack marks a serious escalation affecting civilian infrastructure. Travelers, airport staff, and families of those injured or killed are dealing with the immediate aftermath.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Dozens injured and one killed in drone strike on Kuwait's international airport

events.title: Sourton, Devon
events.body:
A military helicopter crashed in the early hours of Wednesday morning near the village of Sourton in Devon, killing three Royal Navy crew members. The crash happened around 4:00 a.m. during a training exercise. The Ministry of Defence announced the deaths, saying its thoughts were with the families and friends of those killed.

An investigation has been launched by the navy's leadership. Local police were at the scene, and officials described the incident as ongoing. Few details have been released, as the families of those who died asked for a period of grace before more information is made public.

Several naval bases are located near the crash site, including Western Europe's largest naval base. Senior government officials confirmed they were following the situation closely and said more information would be shared as soon as possible.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families of three naval crew members killed in a training helicopter crash in Devon

events.title: Civil Unrest in Bakersfield
events.body:
FBI agents fatally shot a man who had barricaded himself with several hostages inside a Bakersfield, California office building that houses a bank branch and school district office. The incident began after police received a bomb threat at the location and lasted roughly 12 hours before ending in a shooting.

During negotiations, two of the hostages were released. When the standoff ended, all remaining hostages were found unharmed and were evaluated by medical personnel at the scene. Authorities evacuated nearby City Hall and police headquarters and established a wide perimeter around the building during the standoff.

Police said the investigation is ongoing and that a significant law enforcement presence would remain in the area. No motive has been released, and the identity of the man who was killed had not been made public at the time of reporting.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Hostages and residents in Bakersfield shaken after a 12-hour standoff and shooting at a public building

events.title: Civil Unrest in Southampton
events.body:
Hundreds of protesters clashed with riot police in Southampton on Tuesday over the December murder of an 18-year-old finance student. Demonstrators threw stones, chairs, rocks, and flares at officers near the Portswood neighborhood where the student died. Police lines were forced to retreat as the crowd grew violent.

The unrest follows a court sentencing on Monday, in which the killer received a life sentence with a minimum of 21 years. A widely circulated police body-camera video shows officers initially disbelieving the stabbed student's repeated cries that he had been injured while he lay handcuffed on the ground. The victim's killer had accused him of racist abuse, a claim the sentencing judge dismissed entirely. National police leaders have since said they will review anti-racism guidance that some say shaped how officers responded.

The victim's own family has asked the public not to use his death to fuel division or hatred. An unconnected officer has received death threats after being wrongly identified in online posts.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Families and communities in Southampton divided by grief, anger, and street violence after a student's death

events.title: Armed Conflict in Calabria
events.body:
Four migrant farm workers were killed when two men blocked the doors of their minivan, poured a flammable liquid inside, and set it on fire at a petrol station near a village in Calabria, in southern Italy. Security camera footage captured the attack and led to the arrest of two Pakistani nationals. A fifth man, from Afghanistan, survived by breaking a window.

The survivor told Italian media the victims were three Afghans and a Pakistani, all working in the region's strawberry fields. He said the attack followed a dispute over a transportation fee the workers refused to pay, and that the workers had also not received wages for their labor, though they had been given food and shelter. Italian media reported 14 similar arson incidents involving vehicles carrying Pakistani workers in the same area in recent months, linked to tensions among migrants over access to farm work and housing.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Migrant farm workers in Calabria's strawberry fields, vulnerable to violence and exploitation

events.title: Coup/Crisis in Evin prison, Tehran
events.body:
A British couple arrested in January 2025 while passing through Iran on a round-the-world motorcycle trip has lost an appeal against their 10-year espionage sentence. The two deny the charges. Their family says they were not allowed to attend their own appeal hearing, and no reason was given for the rejection. The case has been passed to Iran's Supreme Court, though the family does not understand the process or timeline.

Both are currently on hunger strike at Tehran's Evin prison. The man has been refusing food for around 25 days and is reported to be visibly weakening. The woman is on day 16 of her hunger strike. The couple has been blocked from contacting their family in the UK for over a month, and their last consular visit was in December.

The UK Foreign Office called the appeal decision disappointing and their detention "unjustified and appalling." It currently warns British citizens that holding a British passport alone can be grounds for detention in Iran.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: British couple on hunger strike in Evin prison after losing appeal against espionage conviction

events.title: Armed Conflict in Vynohradar, Kyiv
events.body:
A large Russian missile and drone attack struck multiple Ukrainian cities overnight, killing at least six people in Kyiv and at least 16 in Dnipro, where two residential buildings were hit directly. More than 90 people were injured across Kyiv and Dnipro, and 10 more were hurt in Kharkiv, including a child. Energy facilities and civilian infrastructure were also hit in Kharkiv.

In Vynohradar, a quiet residential neighborhood of Kyiv, high-rise apartment blocks lost their windows, cars were reduced to burnt wreckage, and several residents were hospitalized with severe injuries. A record number of people — more than 41,000, including nearly 4,500 children — sheltered overnight in the Kyiv metro.

After the attack, rescuers checked on residents, government psychologists spoke with those in shock, and volunteers distributed food. Neighborhood residents and municipal workers worked side by side to clear rubble from a children's activity center damaged in the strike.
events.source_title: BBC News
events.prayer: Residents of Kyiv, Dnipro, and Kharkiv grieving losses and recovering from a devastating overnight missile and drone attack

events.title: Armed Conflict in Middle Belt
events.body:
New research from a German social science institute, drawing on over two decades of Nigerian data, finds that religious divisions — not climate change alone — are the key driver of violence between Muslim herders and mostly Christian farming communities. Drought and desertification across the Sahel have pushed Fulani pastoralists beyond their traditional grazing lands, but the study finds that violent clashes are most likely where these Muslim herders encounter majority-Christian farming settlements. Where both communities share the same faith, conflicts are less likely to turn deadly.

The research points to rising tensions since the late 1990s, linked to the introduction of Sharia law in parts of northern Nigeria, resistance in the Middle Belt, and the spread of Boko Haram — all of which have deepened mistrust. A US religious freedom body estimates that around 30,000 armed Fulani militants operate across Nigeria, and says violence linked to these groups has caused more deaths among religious communities in the past year than attacks by insurgent groups or criminal gangs.

Researchers are calling for better land and water management, early warning systems, and community-based mediation in religiously mixed areas to keep environmental pressures from escalating into bloodshed.
events.source_title: World | Deutsche Welle
events.prayer: Christian farming communities and Muslim herders in Nigeria's Middle Belt caught between drought pressures and deepening religious distrust

events.title: Armed Conflict in Tyre
events.body:
Israeli strikes continued across southern Lebanon on Tuesday, even as a partial ceasefire brokered by the United States appeared to hold for Beirut.
